sections.title: Payment Action Queue
sections.content:
Payment Action Queue

The accounting team's daily workspace. Surfaces rejected invoices, overdue payments, and ready-to-invoice work milestones in one prioritized view.

  • URL: /dashboards/payment-action-queue
  • Page-level role gate: ["ADMIN", "EXECUTIVE", "ACCOUNTING"] — non-matching roles are redirected to the home page

What's on the page

  • Rejected invoices — sent but bounced back; need re-issue or correction
  • Overdue payments — invoices past due date with aging
  • Ready-to-invoice milestones — work milestones hit without an invoice yet (pulls from /dashboards/ready-to-invoice source)

Workflow

  1. Start with rejected invoices — diagnose why each was rejected (wrong product line item, wrong customer, etc.) and re-issue
  2. Move to overdue — call/email customer, log contact in HubSpot, escalate via PE Action Queue if PE-tagged
  3. Finish with ready-to-invoice — generate invoices for milestones that have hit but haven't been billed

sections.title: Payment Tracking
sections.content:
Payment Tracking

Per-project payment status: not yet paid, partially paid, fully paid. Use this to answer "where are we with this project's payments?"

  • URL: /dashboards/payment-tracking
  • Page-level role gate: ["ADMIN", "EXECUTIVE", "ACCOUNTING"]

Status Buckets

StatusMeaning
Not yet paidNo payments received against any invoice for this project
Partially paidSome invoices paid, others outstanding
Fully paidAll invoiced amounts received
"Fully paid" is per invoiced amount. If a milestone hasn't been invoiced yet, the project can show "Fully paid" while still having work-to-bill outstanding. Cross-check with the Ready to Invoice view.

sections.title: Ready to Invoice
sections.content:
Ready to Invoice

Work milestones that have been hit but not yet invoiced. Grouped by milestone type so you can batch invoice generation.

  • URL: /dashboards/ready-to-invoice
  • Page-level role gate: ["ADMIN", "EXECUTIVE", "OWNER", "ACCOUNTING"]

Milestone Buckets

Standard PB invoice milestones (each bucket lists projects waiting on its specific invoice):

  • Design Approval (DA) — invoice issued at customer DA signature
  • Construction Complete (CC) — invoice issued at construction handoff
  • Permission to Operate (PTO) — final invoice at utility approval
  • PE Milestone 1 (M1) — Participate Energy first installment
  • PE Milestone 2 (M2) — PE second installment

Workflow

  1. Filter by milestone
  2. Verify each project actually hit the milestone in HubSpot (don't trust the queue blindly — work milestones can lag actual events)
  3. Generate invoices in Zoho Books for each verified row
  4. Once invoiced, the project drops out of this queue and moves to AR if unpaid, or Payment Tracking if paid
For the canonical trigger points of each milestone (when the invoice should fire), see the Reference tab's payment-milestone notes.

sections.title: Accounts Receivable
sections.content:
Accounts Receivable

Invoices sent but unpaid, grouped by aging bucket. Drives the collections workflow.

  • URL: /dashboards/accounts-receivable
  • Page-level role gate: ["ADMIN", "EXECUTIVE", "OWNER", "ACCOUNTING"]

Aging Buckets

BucketAction
0–30 daysWithin terms — monitor only
31–60 daysFirst reminder — friendly follow-up email
61–90 daysSecond reminder + phone call — log contact in HubSpot
90+ daysEscalation — collections review, possible mechanic's lien filing

Per-invoice columns

  • Invoice #, customer, project
  • Amount, days outstanding
  • Last contact date
  • Direct link to invoice in Zoho Books

sections.title: PE Deals & Payments
sections.content:
PE Deals & Payments

All Participate Energy-tagged deals with auto-calculated EPC, lease factor, and payment splits.

  • URL: /dashboards/pe-deals
  • Subtitle: shows expected PE receivable + customer + PE combined
  • Visible to: ADMIN, OWNER, EXECUTIVE, ACCOUNTING

Hero Stats

  • Of expected PE receivable (total)
  • Customer + PE combined value
  • Status counts: Paid, Partially Paid

EPC + Lease Factor

For each PE deal, the dashboard auto-calculates:

  • EPC (Engineering, Procurement, Construction) — what PB charges PE
  • Lease factor — PE's lease pricing input
  • Customer split — what the homeowner pays
  • PE split — what PE pays

Edge Cases

An ⚠️ icon means the EC (energy community) lookup failed for that deal. Check the deal address against the EC eligibility map and update the deal property manually.

sections.title: PE Dashboard
sections.content:
Participate Energy (PE Dashboard)

Project milestone tracker for Participate Energy deals. Different from the PE Deals dashboard (which is financial-only) — this one is operational, tracking the install / inspection / PTO progression for compliance.

  • URL: /dashboards/pe
  • Subtitle: "Project Milestone Tracker"
  • Accent: green

Hero stats

  • Forecasted Installation
  • Forecasted Inspection
  • Forecasted PTO

Workflow

This dashboard is read-only — it's a leadership view of where PE-tagged projects are in the pipeline. For active intervention on stuck PE deals, use the Payment Action Queue (PE M1/M2) and the regular Pipeline action queues.

Compliance note: PE deals have specific milestone deadlines tied to the PE contract. A slipping forecast can trigger contract penalties — pull this dashboard at least weekly during PE-heavy periods.

sections.title: Submitting a New Product
sections.content:
Submitting a New Product

How to add a new piece of equipment to the catalog so it's available across HubSpot, Zuper, and Zoho. The Submit Product wizard handles all three system pushes for you.

When to use this: the BOM tool flagged a missing item, you're processing a deal with new equipment, or a vendor sent something we haven't bought before.

Where to Start

Open Suites → Operations → Submit Product (or go to /dashboards/submit-product directly). The wizard has four steps:

StepWhat you do
1. StartPick how you want to populate the form
2. BasicsBrand, model, category, vendor, pricing
3. DetailsCategory-specific specs (wattage, capacity, etc.)
4. ReviewConfirm everything and submit

Step 1: Pick a Start Mode

Three ways to begin — pick whichever gets you closest to the finished product fastest.

Start from Scratch

Blank form. Fill in everything by hand. Use this when the product is unusual or you can't find a similar one to clone.

Clone Existing Product

Search the catalog for a similar product, copy its specs into a new entry, then change brand/model/specs as needed.

Best for: adding a new wattage variant of a module you already carry, or a new size of a battery you've used before. Most fields stay the same — just bump the wattage or capacity.

Import from Datasheet

Upload the manufacturer's datasheet PDF (or paste the spec text). AI reads it and pre-fills the form.

Best for: brand-new equipment with a clean PDF spec sheet. You'll still review every field before submitting — AI extraction is a starting point, not a final answer.

Step 2: Basics

The wizard checks for duplicates as you type. If a similar product already exists, it appears in the panel below the form — stop and check it before continuing. Submitting a duplicate creates cleanup work for ops and accounting.

Required fields

  • Category — module, inverter, battery, battery expansion, EV charger, racking, electrical BOS, monitoring
  • Brand — manufacturer (e.g., Enphase, Tesla, REC). Pick from the dropdown when possible so we stay consistent.
  • Model — exact model number from the datasheet
  • Description — short human-readable name, used in HubSpot line items and Zoho SOs

Recommended fields

  • Vendor — who we buy it from. Pick from the vendor list; if the vendor isn't there, add them via the vendor picker.
  • Vendor part number — what the vendor calls it (helps match POs to invoices)
  • Unit cost / sell price — required if this product will appear on a Sales Order
  • SKU — internal SKU; auto-generated if left blank
Duplicate panel: if the search finds an existing product that looks like a match, click into it instead of creating a new one. If the existing entry has wrong info, edit it rather than making a duplicate.

Step 3: Details

The fields shown depend on the category you picked. Pull these from the datasheet — don't guess.

CategoryKey spec fields
ModuleWattage (W), efficiency, cell type, Voc / Isc / Vmp / Imp, temperature coefficients
InverterAC output (kW), max DC input, phase, MPPT channels, type (string / micro / hybrid)
BatteryCapacity (kWh), usable capacity, continuous power, chemistry, round-trip efficiency
Battery ExpansionPass-through — link to its parent battery
EV ChargerPower (kW), connector type, amperage, voltage, level (1 / 2 / 3)
RackingMount type (roof / ground), material, tilt range, wind / snow ratings
Electrical BOSComponent type, gauge, voltage rating
MonitoringDevice type, connectivity (Wi-Fi / cellular / Ethernet), compatible inverters
Field tooltips: hover the ? icon next to any field for a definition and example value. If you're unsure, leave it blank and add a note — better to skip a field than guess wrong.

Step 4: Review & Submit

Final pass before the product hits the catalog.

  1. Scan all fields one more time — typos here propagate to HubSpot line items, Zuper jobs, and Zoho SOs
  2. Confirm the four system targets (HubSpot, Zuper, Zoho) are checked
  3. Hit Submit

What happens next

  • Auto-approved: if every required field validates and there's no duplicate concern, the product is live immediately. You'll see "Product Added to Catalog" with a green checkmark.
  • Pending review: if a field couldn't be validated or one of the system pushes failed, the product saves but goes into the admin review queue. An admin gets notified via email and works it from /dashboards/catalog/review.
Either way, the product is saved — you don't need to redo your work. Pending-review just means an admin needs to retry one of the external system pushes.

After Submission

The wizard pushes the product to four places automatically:

SystemWhat gets created
Internal catalog (Postgres)Source of truth — drives the BOM tool, BOM line items, the catalog page
HubSpot ProductsLine items on deals reference this — required for SO automation
Zuper Custom FieldsField service techs see the spec on the job
Zoho InventorySales Orders, Purchase Orders, stock counts

Once the product is created, the BOM tool and the deal-to-SO pipeline can use it on the next run — no extra step needed.

Common Pitfalls

Duplicates

The biggest source of cleanup work. Always pause when the duplicate panel shows a match — even if the brand or model is slightly different, it might be the same physical product with a typo. When in doubt, ping a TechOps admin before submitting.

If you discover a duplicate after the fact, use the Merge tool in the catalog dedup panel rather than deleting one — merging preserves the history and the link to existing line items.

Vendor not in the list

Don't type a free-text vendor name — the vendor picker syncs to Zoho and a free-text name won't match. Use the picker's "Add new vendor" option, which creates the vendor in Zoho first.

Missing pricing

If unit cost or sell price is blank, the product will save but Zoho SOs won't auto-populate the line. Always set pricing for anything that will be quoted or invoiced.

Wrong category

Category drives which spec fields appear and how the BOM tool counts the item (per-module vs per-system, etc.). Changing category later is a hassle — get it right on the first pass.

Datasheet extraction errors

AI extraction is convenient but not perfect. Always verify wattage, capacity, voltage, and current values against the actual PDF before submitting — those four numbers drive system sizing.

Editing an Existing Product

If a product already exists but has wrong specs:

  1. Go to Catalog and search for the product
  2. Open the detail page → Edit
  3. Change the fields → Save — the wizard re-pushes to HubSpot, Zuper, and Zoho automatically
Edits propagate to future line items and SOs, not historical ones. If a deal already has the wrong spec on its line items, you'll need to re-push the BOM for that deal.

Who to Ask

  • Duplicate / merge questions: TechOps
  • Pricing questions: Accounting
  • Vendor setup: Accounting (they own Zoho vendor records)
  • Category / spec questions: TechOps or Design Engineering

sections.title: Drafts — Read Me First
sections.content:
Drafts — Working Content for Review

This tab is admin/owner/executive only. It holds new SOP content that hasn't been promoted to the live guide yet — drop drafts here, compare them to the originals, then promote the ones we want.

What's in here right now

DraftReplaces / AddsStatus
Pipeline Overview (Plain English)NEW — adds a top-level walkthrough to the Project Pipeline tabFor review
PM Guide (Rewrite) — 7 sectionsReplaces the year-old PM Guide tabFor review

How to compare

  1. Open the live tab in one browser tab (e.g. /sop?tab=pm)
  2. Open the matching Drafts entry here in another (e.g. /sop?tab=drafts)
  3. Flip between them, leave suggestions/edits using the Edit button on either side

Promotion process

Once a draft is approved, the engineering side runs a one-line promotion:

  • PM Guide rewrite → drafts get copied into the existing /sop?tab=pm sections, replacing the year-old content. Old sections (e.g. "Schedule Site Survey", "Submit Permit") are deleted.
  • Pipeline Overview → draft is moved to the top of the Project Pipeline tab as a new section.
  • This Drafts tab stays around as a workspace — empty until the next batch of new content lands.
If a draft needs changes, click Edit in the top right of the section. The change saves directly to the draft (you're an admin) and doesn't affect the live SOPs at all.

sections.title: Project Pipeline Walkthrough
sections.content:
Project Pipeline — Plain English Walkthrough

How a deal moves through the HubSpot Project pipeline at PB. ~10 minute read.

Who this is for: Anyone at PB — sales, operations, design, permitting, accounting, leadership — who wants to understand how a project actually flows once the contract is signed. The structure mirrors the 8 deal stages in the HubSpot Project pipeline (Survey → Design → Permit/IC → RTB → Construction → Inspection → PTO → Complete). Each stage tells you what happens, who owns it, the sub-steps inside it, and what triggers the move to the next stage.

The 8 Project Pipeline Stages

#Deal StageOwnerWhat advances the deal out of this stage
1Site SurveySales schedules · Survey Tech performsSite Survey Complete checklist submitted in Zuper
2Design & EngineeringDesign Lead + Designer (Vishtik)Stamped planset uploaded → Design Status = Design Complete
3Permitting & InterconnectionPermit Lead + IC Lead (run in parallel)Both Permitting Status = Permit Issued AND Interconnection Status = Application Approved
4Ready to Build (RTB)System (automated gate)Permit + IC + DA invoice paid → automatically advances to Construction (or RTB-Blocked)
5ConstructionPM schedules · Install Crew executesConstruction Complete checklist submitted in Zuper
6InspectionInspections Tech + QC (Dan / Chad)Final inspection pass
7PTOPM confirms · Utility grantsUtility approves Permission to Operate
8CompletePMCloseout packet sent + HubSpot deal closed
Before the project pipeline: The Sales Rep closes the deal in the Sales pipeline (proposal signed → contract signed → Closed Won). When that flips, the deal moves into the Project pipeline at Stage 1 — Site Survey. The Deal Owner (Sales Rep) completes a presale checklist that mostly auto-fills from OpenSolar and a correctly-created HubSpot deal. The Sales Rep stays involved as the customer-facing contact through Design Approval and Construction.

1. Site Survey

The deal arrives in this stage automatically when it's Closed Won. The work splits into two activities:

1a. Survey Scheduling — Sales

Sales schedules the survey using /dashboards/site-survey-scheduler. Slots are per PB office, not per surveyor — the scheduler tool assigns whichever Survey Tech is available from that office's pool. Each office is capped at 3 surveys per day to keep utilization realistic.

PMs do not schedule surveys. If you're a PM and the survey isn't on the calendar, ping the Deal Owner — that's their lane.

1b. Survey Performed — Survey Tech

The assigned Tech goes on-site, captures system specs (roof, electrical, shade, etc.), and submits the Site Survey Complete checklist in Zuper. That checklist completion is what advances the deal — it automatically:

  • Syncs Site Survey Status to "Completed" in HubSpot
  • Calculates "Time from Sale to Survey Completion"
  • Advances the deal stage to Design & Engineering

If the surveyor flags a sales change (system size needs to drop, customer wants a different module, panel placement no longer fits), they tag "sales change needed" in Zuper. That kicks the work back to the Deal Owner before design can proceed.

2. Design & Engineering

The biggest stage by elapsed time. Three things happen inside it:

2a. Design Review meeting — Ops + Design

Before designers start drawing, Ops and Design meet to review the deal: what was sold, what the survey found, and what the planset should reflect. This is where most change orders surface — discrepancies between what Sales sold and what Survey found get caught here. Notes from this meeting feed PM downstream scheduling: difficulty rating, crew count, special equipment, etc.

2b. Planset drafting — Design Lead + Designer (Vishtik)

The drafting partner (Vishtik) generates the construction planset:

  • Vishtik tasks 1–2 retrieve project details and upload the initial design
  • The Design Lead reviews and either passes it through or kicks it back
  • When clean, Design Status flips to "Draft Complete — Waiting on Approvals"

2c. Design Approval (DA) — Design Lead → Sales Rep → Customer

The Design Lead sends the customer-facing DA via PandaDoc. The customer either signs (deal continues) or rejects (kicks back to Design with revisions). The Sales Rep is responsible for chasing the DA signature — they're the customer-facing person up through Construction. PMs and Designers don't touch the customer at this point.

DA revisions: if the customer rejects, the Designer makes the revision and the Sales Rep re-sends. Sales Reps get a HubSpot task whenever a DA needs their attention.

2d. Engineering stamps (if required)

If the design needs engineering stamps, Vishtik tasks 5–6 produce the stamped planset. If not, the deal proceeds straight to Permitting once the DA is approved.

Stage advances when: Task 6 (Upload Stamped Plans) completes → Design Status = Design Complete → deal moves to Permitting & Interconnection.

3. Permitting & Interconnection

Permitting and interconnection run in parallel — they're tracked as separate statuses on the deal but share a single pipeline stage. The deal can't leave this stage until both are clear.

3a. Permitting — Permit Lead

The Permit Lead submits the planset to the AHJ:

  • Colorado: Peter
  • California: Kristofer

The Permit Lead handles AHJ submission, RFIs, and follow-up. PMs and Sales Reps do not submit permits. When the permit is issued, the Permit Lead manually marks Permitting Status = Permit Issued in HubSpot, which flips:

  • Permit Issue Date is set
  • Time-to-Permit is calculated
  • Customer gets a Permit Status Update SMS + email automatically

3b. Interconnection — IC Lead

The IC Lead handles the utility interconnection application:

  • Colorado: Peter (same person as permits)
  • California: varies by utility

For Xcel Energy in Colorado, the IC application is submitted at the start of the project, so by the time we hit this stage it's usually already approved. For other utilities, the IC application is submitted now.

When the utility approves, the IC Lead manually marks Interconnection Status = Application Approved.

Stage advances when: both Permitting Status = Permit Issued AND Interconnection Status = Application Approved → deal moves to Ready to Build (subject to the RTB gate below).

4. Ready to Build (RTB)

RTB is an automated gate, not a stage anyone has to "do." When all three of these are true, the deal advances:

  1. Permitting Status = Permit Issued
  2. Interconnection Status = Application Approved
  3. DA Invoice = Paid

If any one is missing, the deal sits at RTB — Blocked and shows up on action queues. Whoever owns the missing piece is responsible for unblocking:

  • Permit blocker → Permit Lead
  • IC blocker → IC Lead
  • Unpaid DA invoice → Accounting (surface at standup)

Stage advances when: all three conditions are true → deal automatically moves to Construction.

5. Construction

Two activities happen here:

5a. Schedule the install — PM

The PM schedules construction in /dashboards/scheduler (or the construction-specific view at /dashboards/construction-scheduler). Once scheduled:

  • The Zuper job is created automatically with the right crew assignment
  • A Google Calendar event is created on the install calendar for that office
  • The crew lead and Operations Manager get email notifications

5b. Install + handle plan revisions — Crew + Ops Director

The crew installs the system. If the field finds something the design didn't anticipate (different roof condition, panel that won't fit, electrical issue), the Ops Director (Drew, Joe, or Ro depending on the install) marks the change in the Zuper construction-complete checklist. That triggers a revision workflow: Design updates the planset, Permit Lead files an as-built revision with the AHJ if needed, and the deal continues to inspection once everything's reconciled.

Stage advances when: the crew lead submits the Construction Complete checklist in Zuper → deal moves to Inspection.

6. Inspection

The Inspections Tech runs the final inspection. Dan or Chad creates the QC tasks ahead of the inspection date.

  • If it passes: deal automatically advances to PTO
  • If it fails: the Inspections Lead marks the failure reason in the Zuper inspection-fail checklist, which triggers the corrective workflow (rework task, planset revision, or AHJ correspondence depending on the cause)
Failed inspections are a project-aging risk — they often involve a rework site visit, a re-inspection fee, and a customer who's already waited weeks. Watch the inspection action queues for stalls.

Stage advances when: the final inspection passes → deal moves to PTO.

7. PTO

The utility grants Permission to Operate. PTO arrival varies by utility — Xcel typically 1–3 business days, CSU 2–5, smaller co-ops can be 5–10. The PM:

  1. Verifies the PTO email/letter/portal notice and saves it to the deal's Drive folder
  2. Updates HubSpot: PTO Status = Granted, sets the PTO Date
  3. Verifies the PTO invoice was triggered (Accounting picks it up automatically)
  4. Confirms the customer PTO email + SMS sent

If PTO is > 5 business days past inspection pass with no utility response, the PM escalates to the IC Lead — they have the utility relationships and the application records.

Stage advances when: PM marks PTO Status = Granted → deal moves to Complete.

8. Complete (Closeout)

Final stage. The PM owns three things:

  1. Close the deal in HubSpot — verify all date fields populated, all milestones invoiced, system generation start date set
  2. Send the customer closeout packet — system manuals (module / inverter / battery / EV charger), warranty info (equipment + workmanship), monitoring login, what-to-do-if-it-stops-generating, and tax/incentive paperwork (final ITC docs, state/utility rebate paperwork)
  3. Hand to Service — if the system has any active service plan or known follow-up items, create a Service ticket; otherwise the deal is fully done

Once closeout is sent, the deal stops appearing on PM action queues. Future customer issues become Service pipeline tickets, not reopens of this deal.

Cross-Pipeline Notes

The Sales Rep's involvement after Closed Won

The Sales Rep (Deal Owner) stays involved as the customer-facing contact from sale through Construction. They:

  • Handle DA signature chasing
  • Handle change-order conversations with the customer
  • Get tasks whenever a DA revision needs customer attention

The PM only takes over customer comms at Construction scheduling and PTO/Closeout.

D&R (Detach & Reset) and Roofing

Projects that need a roof replacement before solar install run through the D&R pipeline in parallel. Standalone roofing-only jobs run through the Roofing pipeline. Both are out of scope for this overview — see the dedicated D&R + Roofing suite for those flows.

Service tickets

After Complete, any future customer issue (warranty claim, monitoring problem, equipment failure) becomes a service ticket in the Service pipeline. That's a different lifecycle entirely.

sections.title: What PMs Actually Do
sections.content:
PM Guide — What PMs Actually Do

Rewritten 2026-04-27. The previous PM Guide was a year old and described work PMs no longer own.

The PM job in one sentence: intake the deal cleanly from Sales, schedule construction, monitor everything in between, and close the project out with the customer when PTO lands.

What PMs Do

#ActivityWhere
1Deal Intake & Review — verify the handoff from Sales is clean before the project starts movingHubSpot deal record
2Schedule Construction — the heart of the role. Pick the date, crew, and equipment./dashboards/scheduler · /dashboards/construction-scheduler
3PTO Confirmation — work with the utility-side info, mark PTO in HubSpot when grantedHubSpot
4Closeout — close the deal, send the customer their closeout packetHubSpot · email
5Monitor everything else — watch the deal as it moves through Survey → Design → DA → Permitting → IC → Construction → Inspection. Escalate stalls to whoever owns that stage.Action queues, HubSpot, this guide

What PMs Don't Do (Anymore)

The previous version of this guide had PMs scheduling surveys, submitting permits, and submitting interconnection applications. None of that is the PM's job today. Each piece is owned by someone else now:
Used to be PMNow owned by
Schedule the site surveySales (Deal Owner / Sales coordinator) using /dashboards/site-survey-scheduler
Submit the building permit to the AHJPermit Lead — Peter (CO) / Kristofer (CA)
Submit the utility interconnection applicationIC Lead — Peter (CO) / varies (CA)
Send the Design Approval PandaDoc to the customerDesign Lead → Sales Rep
Handle install plan revisions during constructionOps Director — Drew, Joe, or Ro
Run final inspections / create QC tasksInspections Tech + QC (Dan or Chad)

How to use this guide

Use the sidebar to jump to the stage that matters. Each section says: what to verify, what to watch, and who to escalate to. Keep this open in a tab while you're working through the day's action queue.

For the full project lifecycle (including the work other teams do), see the Pipeline Overview.

sections.title: 1. Deal Intake & Review
sections.content:
1. Deal Intake & Review

Verify the handoff from Sales is clean before the project starts moving.

Goal: by the end of intake, you should be able to answer "does this deal have everything Operations needs to execute?" If yes, let it flow. If no, kick it back to the Deal Owner with specifics.

Trigger

Deal hits Closed Won and flips into the Project pipeline at the Site Survey stage. Sales has scheduled (or is scheduling) the survey. You'll see the new project in the standard PM intake queue.

Intake Checklist

Most of these auto-populate from OpenSolar and a correctly-created HubSpot deal — your job is to verify, not data-enter.

Deal record basics

  • Deal name follows the standard format: PROJ-XXXX | Last, First | Address
  • Deal Owner = the actual selling rep
  • Pipeline = Project; current stage = Site Survey
  • Install Location set (one of: Westy, DTC/Centennial, COSP, Camarillo/SLO)
  • System specs populated: kW DC, module model + count, inverter model, battery model (if applicable)

Customer / contacts

  • Homeowner's contact record exists and is associated to the deal
  • Person responsible for payment is labeled Payor
  • If the system is loan-financed: the person on the loan is labeled Payor + Loan Applicant; the loan provider is associated as a contact and labeled Payor

Documents in Google Drive

  • Signed proposal and signed contract are in the deal's Sales folder (auto-uploads from PandaDoc — verify they're actually there)
  • Any addenda or change-order documents are in the same folder

Payment type

  • Open the proposal "Quotation" page (typically page 11). Confirm cash vs loan.
  • The Accounting section in HubSpot matches that payment type
  • If loan: financing approval is uploaded to Drive

If something is missing

Don't fix Sales's work for them. Tag the Deal Owner in HubSpot with a note explaining what's missing and what needs to happen. Track stuck intake in the standard PM action queue.

Common kickbacks:

  • Missing Payor designation → Deal Owner needs to update HubSpot contact roles
  • System specs blank or "TBD" → Sales needs to finalize before survey
  • Loan provider not linked → Sales needs to associate the contact

Once intake is clean

Step back. The next thing you'll need to act on is scheduling construction, which won't happen for weeks. In the meantime, you're in monitoring mode.

sections.title: 2. Monitoring Sales & Design
sections.content:
2. Monitoring Sales & Design

Survey, Design Meeting, Design & Engineering, Design Approval — you're not driving these, but you are watching.

Your role here: nothing day-to-day except making sure things are progressing. If a deal sits in any of these stages too long, escalate to whoever owns it.

Stages you're monitoring

StageWho's drivingWatch for
Site SurveySurvey TechSurvey scheduled? Survey performed on the booked date? Sales-change-needed flags from the surveyor
Design Review meetingOps + DesignDid the meeting happen? Were change orders flagged?
Design & EngineeringDesign Lead + Designer (Vishtik)Planset drafted within 3–5 business days? Stuck on engineering stamps?
Design Approval (DA)Design Lead → Sales Rep → CustomerDA sent to customer? Customer signed? Revisions in flight?

What "stalled" looks like

Use the action queues to surface stalls — don't try to track this by clicking through individual deals. Reasonable thresholds:

  • Site Survey — booked but not performed within 2 days of the slot date
  • Design — > 5 business days in "Retrieve Project Details" without movement
  • DA — sent > 5 business days without customer action

Who to escalate to

If stalled at…Escalate to
Survey not scheduledDeal Owner (Sales)
Survey scheduled but not performedOperations Manager for that office (techs are assigned out of a pool, not by name — go to the manager who runs the pool)
Design Meeting didn't happenDesign Lead + Ops Director
Designer stuckDesign Lead
DA not signed by customerSales Rep / Deal Owner — they own the customer relationship
DA revision stalledDesigner — they make the revision; Sales Rep re-sends

What you should NOT do

You don't schedule the survey. You don't write the design. You don't email the customer about the DA. The previous PM Guide had PMs in all of these — they're not your job anymore. If someone asks why you "aren't fixing it," explain who owns it and help connect them.

Tools

  • /dashboards/de-overview — Design & Engineering pipeline state
  • /dashboards/design-revisions — DA revision queue
  • /dashboards/at-risk — at-risk projects across all stages

sections.title: 3. Monitoring Permitting & IC
sections.content:
3. Monitoring Permitting & Interconnection

Permit Lead and IC Lead drive these. You watch dates and escalate stalls.

Your role here: same as Design — make sure things are progressing. The Permit Lead and IC Lead handle submissions, AHJ correspondence, and utility follow-up. You're a backstop.

Who owns what

StageOwner — COOwner — CA
Permit submission to AHJPeterKristofer
AHJ correspondence (RFIs, revisions)PeterKristofer
Marking Permitting Status = Permit IssuedPeterKristofer
Interconnection submissionPeterVaries by utility
Marking Interconnection Status = Application ApprovedPeterIC owner per utility

What to watch

  • Time in Permitting — if the AHJ is slow, that's the AHJ; if we haven't submitted yet, that's a real stall
  • RFIs from the AHJ — Permit Lead handles, but you should know the deal is paused until resolved
  • Interconnection — Xcel deals — IC apps for Xcel are submitted at the start of the project, so by this stage they should already be approved. If not, escalate to Peter.
  • Interconnection — non-Xcel — varies wildly by utility. Some utilities take 30+ days. The IC action queue surfaces aging.

The RTB Gate

Once permits and IC both clear, the system automatically advances the deal to Ready to Build — but only if all three of these are true:

  1. Permitting Status = Permit Issued
  2. Interconnection Status = Application Approved
  3. DA Invoice = Paid

If any one is missing, the deal sits in RTB — Blocked. Whoever owns the missing piece needs to clear it. If the DA invoice is the blocker, that's an Accounting follow-up — surface it in the next standup.

Tools

  • /dashboards/permitting — permitting pipeline state
  • /dashboards/interconnection — IC pipeline state
  • /dashboards/pi-action-queue — combined permitting + IC action queue
  • /dashboards/at-risk — anything aging across the pipeline

sections.title: 4. Schedule Construction
sections.content:
4. Schedule Construction

The heart of the PM job. Pick the date, the crew, and confirm the equipment is in place.

Trigger: deal advances to Ready to Build (RTB). RTB-Blocked deals shouldn't be scheduled — get them unblocked first.

Tool

Use /dashboards/scheduler for the unified scheduling view, or /dashboards/construction-scheduler for the construction-only view with crew availability and capacity heatmaps.

Pre-schedule checks

  • Permit issued (you're past RTB if you got here, but double-check)
  • IC application approved (same)
  • DA invoice paid (same)
  • Materials in inventory or PO confirmed for the install date — check the BOM line items in HubSpot
  • Crew availability for the install location and install size
  • Customer is reachable and has confirmed the date

Scheduling

  1. Open the scheduler. Filter by install location.
  2. Pick a date with crew availability. The capacity heatmap shows green / yellow / red for each office.
  3. Assign the crew based on the install size and any special equipment needs (battery installs, EV chargers, complex roofs).
  4. Save the schedule. The system auto-creates:
    • A Zuper job with the correct crew and Job Category
    • A Google Calendar event on the install calendar for that office
    • Email notifications to the crew lead and the Operations Manager
  5. Send the customer a scheduling confirmation (email template handled by the system; verify it sent).
The Zuper API only allows setting the assigned crew at job creation time. If you need to change the crew after the job is created, you have to delete and recreate the Zuper job. Avoid by getting the crew assignment right the first time.

Office-specific notes

OfficeCalendar bucketNotes
WestminsterWesty install calendar—
DTC / CentennialDTC install calendar—
Colorado SpringsCOSP install calendar—
Camarillo / SLOCalifornia install calendar (shared)SLO and Camarillo intentionally share one calendar — do not split

If you need to reschedule

Customer reschedules happen. The scheduler supports rescheduling; the Zuper job and calendar event move with it. If the new date pushes the crew assignment past their availability, swap the crew before saving.

If a reschedule pushes the install > 30 days from the original date, the deal may need to re-verify pre-schedule conditions (e.g. permit hasn't expired, DA invoice still applicable). Check before saving.

sections.title: 5. Monitor Construction → Inspections
sections.content:
5. Monitoring Construction → Inspections

The crew installs. Ops handles plan revisions. Inspections Tech handles the final inspection. You watch.

Your role here: after you've scheduled construction, the build itself isn't your job. You watch progress and escalate if something stalls.

During construction

The crew lead runs the install. When the install is complete, they submit the Construction Complete checklist in Zuper. That automatically advances the deal to Inspection.

If something changes during install

The Ops Director (Drew, Joe, or Ro depending on the install) handles plan revisions during construction. If the field finds something the planset didn't anticipate, the Ops Director marks it via the Zuper construction-complete checklist, which triggers a revision workflow.

You don't need to do anything — the workflow handles it. Just be aware: a deal that goes into a plan revision will pause at Construction until Design + Permitting (and sometimes the AHJ) reconcile the change.

Inspection

The Inspections Tech runs the final inspection. Ahead of the inspection, Dan or Chad creates the QC tasks. You don't need to involve yourself unless something goes wrong.

If inspection passes

Deal automatically advances to PTO. See PTO →

If inspection fails

The Inspections Lead marks the failure reason in the Zuper inspection-fail checklist. That triggers the corrective workflow:

  • If it's a workmanship issue → corrective install task assigned
  • If it's a planset issue → kicks back to Design / Permitting
  • If it's an AHJ-specific quirk → Permit Lead handles AHJ correspondence
Failed inspections are a project-aging risk. They typically involve a rework site visit, a re-inspection fee, and a customer who's been waiting weeks. Watch /dashboards/inspections for stalled-on-failure projects and surface them at standup.

Tools

  • /dashboards/construction — construction in flight
  • /dashboards/inspections — inspection state across all deals
  • /dashboards/at-risk — at-risk projects regardless of stage

sections.title: 6. PTO
sections.content:
6. PTO (Permission to Operate)

Utility grants the system permission to actually generate. PM confirms and updates HubSpot.

Trigger: inspection passed, system installed, paperwork submitted to the utility for PTO. The utility comes back with a PTO approval (sometimes by email, sometimes via portal, sometimes by mail — varies by utility).

What you do

  1. Verify PTO — confirm the utility approval is real (PTO email, portal screenshot, or letter). Save the document to the deal's Drive folder.
  2. Update HubSpot:
    • Set PTO Status = Granted
    • Set the PTO Date
    • Verify the PTO invoice has been triggered (Accounting picks this up automatically when PTO Status flips)
  3. Notify the customer — the system sends a templated PTO email + SMS automatically when the status flips. Verify it sent.

By utility

UtilityHow PTO arrivesTypical lag from inspection pass
Xcel Energy (CO)Email from the dedicated Xcel solar inbox1–3 business days
CSU (Colorado Springs Utilities)Portal status update + email2–5 business days
Mountain View / Estes Park / etc.Varies — usually email or letter5–10 business days
SCE / PG&E (CA)Portal — Permission to Operate noticeHighly variable; check daily

If PTO is taking too long

If you're > 5 business days past inspection pass and there's no PTO yet, escalate to the IC Lead — they have the utility relationships and the application records.

Once PTO is granted

Move directly to closeout. Don't let a PTO'd project sit — the customer is waiting on their packet, the closeout invoice triggers from this stage, and Service can't take ownership until the deal is closed.

sections.title: 7. Closeout
sections.content:
7. Closeout

Close the deal in HubSpot. Send the customer their closeout packet. Hand to Service.

Trigger: PTO has been granted and the customer's system is generating. Closeout is the final PM responsibility on the project.

1. Close the deal in HubSpot

  • Stage = Complete in the Project pipeline
  • All HubSpot date fields populated (Survey, Design Complete, Permit Issue, IC Approve, Construction Complete, Inspection Pass, PTO)
  • All deal milestones have an associated invoice and payment status
  • System generation start date set (= PTO Date in most cases)

2. Send the customer closeout packet

The closeout packet is a templated customer email (or shared Drive link) containing:

  • System manuals — module, inverter, battery (if applicable), EV charger (if applicable)
  • Warranty information — equipment warranties (manufacturer) + workmanship warranty (PB)
  • Monitoring login — credentials for the inverter app, instructions to download
  • "What to do if your system stops generating" — service contact info, what counts as a real problem vs. a normal weather-driven dip
  • Tax / incentive paperwork — final ITC documentation, any state/utility rebate paperwork the customer needs for filing

Use the standard closeout email template. Verify the customer received it by checking the HubSpot email log.

3. Hand to Service

If the system has any active service plan or known follow-up items (a touch-up needed, a customer-reported concern from inspection, a pending utility process), create the Service ticket and assign to the Service team. Otherwise the deal is fully done.

4. Final HubSpot updates

  • Closeout email logged in deal timeline
  • Closeout packet attached to deal record
  • Any active service items linked to a Service ticket
  • Internal notes capturing anything notable for future reference (difficult AHJ, picky customer, equipment quirk, etc.)

That's the project

After closeout, the deal stops appearing on PM action queues. If a customer comes back with a service issue 6 months later, that's the Service team's lane via a new Service pipeline ticket — not a reopening of this deal.

sections.title: Revenue
sections.content:
Revenue

Revenue dashboard — total deal value across all in-flight projects with stage and location breakdowns. Use this for board reporting, monthly P&L, and topline pacing.

  • URL: /dashboards/revenue
  • Visible to: ADMIN, OWNER, EXECUTIVE

What's on the page

  • Hero stats (StatCards) — total active project value, RTB value, PE-tagged value, backlog value
  • Backlog by stage — counts and revenue per pipeline stage
  • By location — revenue distribution across the five PB offices (excludes "Unknown")
  • Forecasted installations — project-by-project list with forecast install dates and overdue flags

How to read it

The numbers are active project value, not booked revenue. A project counts toward the total from the moment a deal is signed until construction is complete. To see realized revenue (invoiced), use the Accounting tab's payment tracking views.

sections.title: Command Center
sections.content:

Command Center has been split. The page at /dashboards/command-center now redirects directly to the Executive Suite hub at /suites/executive.

Where the original Command Center content lives now

The original "Command Center" was a single dashboard combining many leadership views. Each section now has its own dashboard:

Original sectionWhere it lives now

sections.title: Capacity Planning
sections.content:
Capacity Planning

Crew capacity vs. forecasted installs by location and month. Use this to identify overload risk, justify hiring, and balance crew assignments across offices.

  • URL: /dashboards/capacity
  • Visible to: ADMIN, OWNER, EXECUTIVE

What's on the page

  • Per-location capacity heatmap — month columns, location rows, color intensity by load
  • Forecasted install count vs. crew-day capacity, per month per location
  • Overload flags when forecast exceeds available capacity

Crew capacity inputs

Capacity is computed from CrewMember active rosters, CrewAvailability default schedules, and AvailabilityOverride rows for PTO / sick / blocked days. New hires and terminations need CrewMember updates to reflect in this view.

sections.title: Territory Map
sections.content:

What's on the page

  • Deal markers colored by office

Use cases

  • New office siting — visualize underserved areas
  • Boundary disputes — see actual deal distribution vs. assumed coverage
  • Sales territory planning — set rep coverage based on map view

sections.title: Location Comparison

Location Comparison

Side-by-side comparison of all five Photon Brothers locations: performance, capacity, and pipeline breakdown. Use this for executive review of which offices are over- and under-performing.

sections.title: Preconstruction Metrics
sections.content:
Preconstruction Metrics

Site survey, design approval, permitting, and interconnection KPIs with 12-month trend lines. Use this to track operational health over time and spot deteriorating turnarounds before they become emergencies.

  • URL: /dashboards/preconstruction-metrics
  • Visible to: ADMIN, OWNER, EXECUTIVE

Hero stats (12-month windows)

  • Surveys (12 months)
  • Design Approvals (12 months)
  • Permits (12 months)
  • Interconnection (12 months)

Filters

Multi-select filters with state persisted via the usePreconstMetricsFilters Zustand store. Locations, stage, and project owner can all be narrowed.

Trend Lines

Each KPI has a 12-month trend showing whether the metric is improving or eroding. Look for sustained drops or sudden cliffs as triggers for ops investigation.

Active deals, funnel visualization, and proposal tracking — leadership view.

  • URL: /dashboards/sales

For the deeper sales-rep workflow on this page, see the Sales & Marketing tab.

sections.title: Forecast Accuracy
sections.content:
Forecast Accuracy (Executive Reference)

How well the forecasting model predicts reality across milestones and segments. The same data also lives in the Forecast tab for ops use; this section captures the executive lens: trust signals on the underlying pipeline projections.

  • URL: /dashboards/forecast-accuracy
  • Subtitle: "How well the forecasting model predicts reality"

Hero stats (with subtitles)

  • Median Error (Install milestone — original forecast)
  • Mean Absolute Error (Average deviation from actual)
  • Within 1 Week (Forecasts within 7 days)
  • Within 2 Weeks (Forecasts within 14 days)

Reading it as an executive

If the median error trend line is widening or the "within 14 days" % is dropping below 60%, the model is degrading and forecasts shouldn't be trusted for board commitments. Push ops to update underlying milestone targets and re-run.

sections.title: Customer Estimator (Preview)
sections.content:
Customer Estimator (Preview)

Public-facing instant solar estimator. The Executive Suite surfaces the customer-facing tool so leadership can preview what prospects see before quoting.

  • Marketing site: /estimator with tiles for new install, EV Charger, System Expansion, Battery, Detach & Reset, Out of Area
  • Direct entry: /estimator/new-install?step=address — drops into the address-first flow

Estimator Flows

FlowPath
New Install (solar)/estimator/new-install
Battery/estimator/battery
EV Charger/estimator/ev-charger
System Expansion/estimator/system-expansion
Detach & Reset/estimator/detach-reset
Out of Area (declined)/estimator/out-of-area

What customers see

Address-first flow: enter an address, the estimator returns an instant estimate using EagleView measurements, OpenSolar pricing, and our adder rules. Results page collects contact info and creates a HubSpot lead.

For Sales-side use of the estimator (creating quotes mid-call), see the Sales & Marketing tab.

sections.title: Timeline
sections.content:
Forecast Timeline

Project-by-project view of forecasted PTO dates and milestone variance. Use this to spot projects at risk of slipping their PTO commitment.

  • URL: /dashboards/forecast-timeline
  • Visible to: all roles

What's on the Page

Hero stats (4 cards):

  • Active Projects (total)
  • On Track (variance ≤ 7 days)
  • At Risk (8–14 days)
  • Behind (> 14 days)

Main table — six sortable columns:

  1. Project name
  2. Location
  3. Stage
  4. Next Milestone
  5. Forecast PTO date
  6. Variance (days)

Variance color-codes: green (on track), amber (at risk), red (behind), gray (no forecast). Default sort: variance descending — biggest slips at the top.

Expand a Row

Click any row to reveal the milestone forecast detail:

  • Milestone name
  • Basis — segment / location / global / actual (the data the forecast was built on)
  • Original Forecast vs Live Forecast
  • Actual date (when reached)
  • Calculated variance
A segment basis means the forecast uses historical data from the same location + AHJ + utility combo. Global means the system fell back to portfolio-wide averages because there wasn't enough segment-specific history.

Filters

Multi-select dropdowns: Location, Stage, PTO Month, Variance status. The hero stats recalculate live as you filter.

Export

CSV export includes Project, Customer, Location, Stage, Next Milestone, and all date fields.

Cache

  • Stale time: 5 minutes
  • Cache time: 15 minutes

sections.title: Schedule
sections.content:
Forecast Schedule

Calendar grid showing pre-construction projects (survey, RTB, blocked, design, permitting) plotted against their forecasted install dates.

  • URL: /dashboards/forecast-schedule
  • Visible to: all roles

What's on the Page

Calendar grid: one month at a time. Navigation buttons for prev/next month and a "Today" jump button.

Filter buttons (multi-select):

  • Stage: survey, RTB, blocked, design, permitting
  • Location: any combination of the five locations

Event pills on each day:

  • Customer name
  • Dashed amber border = forecast date is in the past (overdue)
  • Solid blue = future date

Sidebar (visible on wide screens)

  • Pipeline Breakdown: stage distribution counts and revenue
  • Location Distribution: sorted by revenue
  • Overdue Forecasts callout

Revenue is shown in compact notation (e.g., 1.2M, 250K).

Forecast Ghosts

Pre-construction projects without a real scheduled date appear as "ghost" events on their forecast date. Once a real installation date is set, the ghost disappears.

Cache

  • Stale time: 5 minutes
  • Cache time: 15 minutes

sections.title: Accuracy
sections.content:
Forecast Accuracy

Backward-looking review of how good our forecasts have been. Useful for spotting milestones where the model is consistently off so we can recalibrate.

  • URL: /dashboards/forecast-accuracy
  • Visible to: all roles

Hero Stats (2×2 grid)

  • Median Error (days)
  • Mean Absolute Error (days)
  • Within 1 Week (%)
  • Within 2 Weeks (%)

Per-Milestone Accuracy

Sortable table showing every milestone with:

  • Median error bar (color-coded by magnitude)
  • ±7 day accuracy %
  • ±14 day accuracy % with letter grade A–F
  • Sample count

Grade Scale

GradeWithin 14 days
A≥ 80%
B≥ 60%
C≥ 40%
D≥ 20%
F< 20%

Basis Distribution

Stacked horizontal bars showing what percentage of forecasts used each basis: actual, segment, location, global, insufficient.

A high "global" or "insufficient" share means we don't have enough history for that combo of location/AHJ/utility — forecasts in that bucket will be less reliable.

Monthly Trend

Bar chart of mean absolute error for the last 12 months, color-coded by accuracy threshold. Useful for seeing whether forecasts are getting better or worse over time.

Cache

  • Stale time: 10 minutes
  • Cache time: 30 minutes

Data scope: completed projects with actual dates in the last 12 months.

Activities Tab (Default View)

This is the most-used tab. It shows everything that's happened on the deal in chronological order.

See Activities & Workflows for the detailed breakdown.

Contracts, Billing & Savings Tab

Finance Card

Deal Overview & Project Details Tab

Auto-generated AI deal summary with numbered source citations. Updated periodically. Has thumbs up/down feedback and an "Ask a question" AI prompt for querying deal details.

sections.title: Activities & Workflows
sections.content:
Activities Tab — Workflows & Automation

The Activities tab is the most information-dense part of a deal record. Understanding what's automated vs manual helps you know what to act on and what to ignore.

Activity Sub-Tabs

Sub-TabShowsWhen to Use
All activitiesEverything in chronological order (default)Full timeline view
NotesManual + automated notes onlyFinding specific project notes
EmailsTracked/logged email threadsReviewing customer communication
CallsCall recordings and logsListening to customer calls
More → TasksOpen/overdue/completed tasksChecking what's due or overdue
Activity filter: The default view shows ~18 of ~32 activity types. The filter is set to "Activity" which excludes raw property change logs. If you need to see every property edit, change the Activity filter dropdown.

Activity Types You'll See

1. Pinned Notes

Automated notes pinned to the top of the activity feed. The pinned note changes as the deal moves through stages — it could be sales notes, a design thread, permitting & interconnection thread, ready-to-build notes, inspection notes, PTO notes, or project turnaround notes. Created by the "Automated Project Thread" workflow.

Use the pinned thread for ongoing discussion at each stage. Add comments to the pinned note rather than creating separate notes — this keeps everything in one thread.

2. Automated Tasks

Tasks created by numbered workflow sequences and assigned to specific team members. These follow the project lifecycle:

01a → 03 → 05e → 06 → 07 → 07a → 09
Permit Utility Review Transition PTO PTO photos Insp→PTO
Workflow #StageExample Task
01aPermittingSubmit Permit To AHJ – ZRS
03Utility/InterconnectionSubmit Interconnection Application To The Utility – ZRS
05eSupplemental ReviewFollow Up On Supplemental Review – ZRS
07aPTO FlowXcel PTO Photos Ready to Submit – ZRS
09Inspection → PTOVerify & Upload Inspection Results – ZRS
"ZRS" suffix: All automated tasks end with "– ZRS" — this is a queue/routing identifier. It helps distinguish automated tasks from manually created ones.

3. Workflow Activities (Enrolled/Completed)

These make up 60-70% of the activity feed. Each workflow generates TWO entries: "enrolled in" and "completed". They're mostly noise for day-to-day use but valuable for auditing automation.

Workflow categories you'll see:

CategoryExamplesWhat They Do
Stage Transitions06. Transition | RTB - Blocked to Ready To Build
09. Transition | Inspections to PTO		Move the deal between stages
Date StampsDate Stamp | Ready To Build Date
Date Stamp | Final Inspection Pass Date		Auto-populate date fields on transitions
Zuper SyncDeal to Zuper Job and Project
Zuper Job Scheduled
Zuper Job Passed		HubSpot ↔ Zuper bidirectional sync
Bot HooksBot Hook | Inspection
Bot Hook | PTO		Trigger external automations (PB Ops Suite)
MetricsInspection Pass (365 Days)
CC Last 365 Days		Track rolling metrics for reporting
FinancialLine Items Updated Notification
Q1 Tesla Cash Rebate		Invoice triggers, rebate tracking

4. Automated Notes

Notes created by workflows with calculated data:

  • Construction Complete note — Includes turnaround time calculation: "Construction Turnaround Time was 24 hours"
  • Accepted Proposal note — Created by Tray.io when customer accepts PandaDoc proposal
  • Permitting Thread — Running pinned note (see above)

5. Emails (Tracked & Logged)

Two types of email entries:

TypeShowsTracking
Tracked outboundEmails sent from HubSpot or logged via extensionOpens count + Clicks count (e.g., "Opens: 16, Clicks: 0")
Logged inboundCustomer replies captured by HubSpotNo tracking data (just content)

6. Deal Activity (Stage Changes)

Logged automatically when the deal moves between pipeline stages. Shows the exact transition: "moved from Ready To Build to Construction". Click "View details" for the full property change log.

7. Zuper Activities

Field service status changes synced from Zuper. Format: "[Person] updated the status of the associated field visit job [#] to [Status]". Confirms that field work is progressing.

8. Manual Notes

Notes entered by team members. Common examples: Install Reports from crew leads, project update notes from PMs, customer interaction summaries.

Reading the Activity Feed Effectively

What to Focus On

If You Need To...Look For...Filter To...
Check what tasks are dueTask entries with "Upcoming" or "Overdue" statusMore → Tasks sub-tab
See customer communicationEmail threads and call logsEmails or Calls sub-tab
Understand project timelineDeal Activity (stage changes) + Date Stamp workflowsAll activities, scan for "moved from"
Find a specific noteManual and automated notesNotes sub-tab
Check field work statusZuper activity entriesAll activities, look for Zuper entries
Pro tip: Workflow enrolled/completed pairs are noise for daily work. Focus on task assignments, stage changes, notes, and emails. If you want to audit automation behavior, filter to "All activities" and trace the numbered workflow sequences (01a → 03 → 05e → 06 → 07 → 07a → 09).

sections.title: Automation Legend
sections.content:
Automation Legend & System Map

Every action in this SOP is tagged to show what's automated, what's manual, and what triggers downstream automations.

How to read this guide: Throughout the SOP, you'll see colored tags on each step. These tell you whether you need to do something, or whether the system handles it automatically.
  • Purple AUTO = the system does this for you — no action needed
  • Orange MANUAL = you need to take this action yourself
  • Green ZUPER SYNC = data flows automatically from Zuper field software to HubSpot
  • Pink CHECKLIST = submit a checklist in Zuper to trigger the next step

Tag Reference

AUTO

Happens automatically via HubSpot workflow. No human action needed.

MANUAL

Requires a team member to take action — update a field, make a call, upload a doc.

TRIGGER

Your manual action kicks off downstream automations. You act, then the system reacts.

ZUPER SYNC

Data synced from Zuper Pro field software. Update in Zuper → auto-syncs to HubSpot.

CHECKLIST

Completed by submitting a Zuper checklist (survey complete, construction complete, inspection failure). Submission syncs to HubSpot and triggers automations.

CALCULATED

Auto-calculated timing metric. Computes when date fields are populated.

NOTIFICATION

Auto-sent customer SMS + Email notification triggered by a status change.

Pipeline Overview

PipelinePurposeStages
SalesLead qualification → contract signingQualified → Proposal → Accepted → Finalizing → Closed
ProjectPost-sale execution → completionSurvey → Design → Permit/Interconnection → RTB → Construction → Inspection → PTO → Complete
D&RDetach & Reset for roof workKickoff → Survey → Design → Permit → Detach → Reset → Inspection → Complete
ServicePost-install service & warrantyPrep → Site Visit → WIP → Inspection → Invoicing → Complete
RoofingStandalone roofing projectsAppointment → Qualified → Presentation → Contract → Closed

Key Automation Chains

These are the most important automation sequences. Understanding these tells you what happens after you take an action.

Site Survey Completion

ZUPER Field crew completes survey in Zuper → Site Survey Status syncs to "Completed"
AUTO Is Site Survey Completed? → Yes
CALC Time from Sale to Survey Completion computed
AUTO Deal stage → Design & Engineering

Design Task Workflow → DA → Engineering → Design Complete

AUTO Vishtik tasks 1–2: Retrieve details → Upload design → Design Status advances through "In Progress" → "Ready For Review"
AUTO Design Lead task 3: Initial review complete → Design Status → "Draft Complete - Waiting on Approvals"
AUTO PandaDoc sent → Design Approval Status → "Sent For Approval"
NOTIF Design Approval Update SMS + Email sent to customer
AUTO PandaDoc approved/rejected → Design Approval Status → "Design Approved" or "Design Rejected"
AUTO DA approved → Final Design Review task created → Design Lead task 4
AUTO Task 4 complete → routes to engineering stamps (Vishtik tasks 5–6) OR straight to permitting
AUTO Task 6 (Upload Stamped Plans) complete → Design Status → "Design Complete"
AUTO Is Design Completed? → Yes, Design Completion Date set
CALC Design Turnaround Time
AUTO Deal stage → Permitting & Interconnection

Permit + Interconnection → RTB Gate

AUTO Permitting task workflow advances statuses: "Submitted To Customer" → "Customer Signature Acquired" → "Submitted to AHJ" (all task-driven)
AUTO Interconnection task workflow advances statuses: "Submitted To Customer" → "Ready To Submit" → "Submitted To Utility" (all task-driven)
MANUAL Permitting Status → "Permit Issued" (set manually when permit received)
MANUAL Interconnection Status → "Application Approved" (set manually when utility approves)
AUTO Is Permit Issued? → Yes, Permit Issue Date set
CALC Time to Permit
NOTIF Permit Status Update SMS + Email
RTB Gate: AUTO When permit = Issued AND interconnection = Approved AND DA invoice = Paid → deal moves to Ready To Build. If any condition is incomplete → RTB - Blocked.

Construction Complete

CHECKLIST Construction Complete Checklist submitted
AUTO Construction Status → Construction Complete
AUTO Construction Complete Date set
CALC Construction Turnaround Time, Time from RTB to CC, Time to CC
AUTO Deal stage → Inspection
AUTO M2 (CC) invoice workflow triggered
NOTIF Construction Update SMS + Email

Inspection Pass → PTO → Close Out

ZUPER Inspection passes in field → Final Inspection Status syncs to "Passed"
AUTO Is Inspection Passed? → Yes
AUTO PTO Status → "Inspection Passed - Ready for PTO Submission"
AUTO Deal stage → Permission To Operate
TRIGGER PTO Status → "PTO Granted"
AUTO Deal stage → Close Out
CALC PTO Turnaround Time, Time To PTO, Time Between CC & PTO
AUTO PTO invoice workflow triggered
NOTIF PTO Update + Closeout Update SMS + Email

Zuper Sync

ZUPER Field crew updates job status in Zuper Pro
AUTO Site Survey Status, Construction Status, Detach Status, Reset Status, Final Inspection Status all sync from Zuper
Known Race Condition — Zuper Inspection Sync: When an inspection fails in Zuper, the system auto-creates a new job, overwriting the "Failed" status before HubSpot captures it. Always use the Inspection Failed Checklist in Zuper as the reliable method for failure tracking.

Project Lead Assignment

All project leads are automatically assigned at the beginning of the project when the deal enters the Project Pipeline. This includes Design Lead, Permit Lead, Interconnections Lead, Incentive Lead, and more. These assignments happen upfront via automation — not at each individual stage.

sections.title: Site Survey
sections.content:
Site Survey

Scheduling, conducting, and documenting site surveys with full automation mapping.

At a glance: The site survey is the first field visit after a deal is sold. Here's what happens:
  • Survey gets scheduled (via Zuper or the scheduler) — customer gets a text and email
  • Crew goes to the site, takes photos and measurements, uploads everything
  • Crew marks the job complete in Zuper — HubSpot automatically advances the deal to Design
  • If there are issues (bad roof, HOA, etc.), flag them before marking complete

Status Flow Site Survey Status

Ready to Schedule→ Awaiting Reply→ Scheduled→ On Our Way→ Started→ In Progress→ Completed
Other: Needs Revisit Scheduling On-Hold No Site Survey Needed Pending Loan Approval Waiting on Change Order

Procedure

1. Schedule Survey ZUPER SYNC

When the site survey is scheduled in the scheduler or directly in Zuper, Site Survey Status auto-updates to "Scheduled".

Site Survey Scheduler

ZUPER Survey scheduled in Zuper/scheduler → Site Survey Status → "Scheduled"
AUTO Is Site Survey Scheduled? → Yes
CALC Time Taken to Schedule Site Survey (time from deal entry to schedule)
NOTIF Customer notification sent: Site Survey Update - SMS + Site Survey Update - Email

2. Conduct Survey ZUPER SYNC

Surveyor updates job status in Zuper Pro. Zuper auto-syncs to HubSpot: On Our Way → Started → In Progress.

ZUPER Site Survey Status updated from Zuper
ZUPER Site Surveyor - Zuper assigned from Zuper

3. Upload Survey Data MANUAL

Upload photos, measurements, and notes to the Site Survey Documents folder. If an MPU (meter panel upgrade) is needed, note it in the MPU Scheduled For field.

4. Survey Completed ZUPER SYNC

Mark the survey job as complete in Zuper.
HubSpot picks up the completion, marks the survey done, calculates timing metrics, and advances the deal to Design & Engineering.
ZUPER Survey completed in Zuper → Site Survey Status → "Completed"
AUTO Is Site Survey Completed? → Yes
AUTO Site Survey Checklist Completed By records who
CALC Time from Sale to Survey Completion (time from sale to survey done)
CALC Time from Sale to Survey Completion
AUTO Deal stage advances to Design & Engineering
If survey reveals issues (bad roof, inadequate electrical, HOA restrictions), set status to "Needs Revisit" or "Scheduling On-Hold" in Zuper before marking complete — the Zuper completion sync triggers the stage advance.

Site Survey Complete Checklist

After the field survey, the surveyor or Site Survey Lead fills out the Site Survey Complete Checklist in Zuper (synced to HubSpot's Center Panel → Zuper & Operations tab). This checklist captures:

Checklist ItemWhy It Matters
Sales Change Order Needed?If the survey reveals conditions that require a contract change (system size, equipment, price), flag here. Stops the design process until resolved.
Specifics Needed for Design?Capture anything the design team needs: roof pitch measurements, shading notes, attic access, conduit run requirements, etc.
Notes for Design TeamFree-text field for surveyor observations. This is the primary handoff mechanism from field to design.
MPU Required?If the meter panel / main panel upgrade is needed, note here. MPU work must be scheduled separately and may delay interconnection.

Customer Self-Service Portal

For site surveys, customers can self-schedule through a booking portal:

StepWhat Happens
1. Invite SentSite Survey Lead sends booking link via POST /api/portal/survey/invite — customer receives email with scheduling link
2. Customer BooksCustomer selects from available time slots (filtered by crew availability, travel time, and existing bookings)
3. ConfirmationCustomer receives confirmation email. Survey job auto-created in Zuper with status "Scheduled"
4. RescheduleCustomer can reschedule via the portal link. Original slot freed, new slot booked.

Handoff to Design

When the survey is marked complete, the deal automatically advances to Design & Engineering. The design team picks up from:

  • Survey photos and measurements in Site Survey Documents
  • Checklist notes in the Zuper & Operations tab
  • MPU requirements flagged during the survey
  • Any sales change order notes that need resolution first

Key Fields

FieldSet ByPurpose
Site Survey StatusZUPER / AUTOCurrent survey status
Is Site Survey Scheduled?AUTOFlips when Zuper/scheduler sets status to Scheduled
Is Site Survey Completed?AUTOFlips when Zuper syncs status to Completed
Site Survey Booked DateAUTOAuto-set the day the survey is scheduled
Site Survey Schedule DateMANUALActual date of the site survey
Site Survey DocumentsMANUALLink to survey documents
Time Taken to Schedule Site SurveyCALCDays from deal entry to scheduling
Time from Sale to Survey CompletionCALCTotal time from sale to survey done

sections.title: Design Approval (DA)
sections.content:
Design Approval (DA)

The customer-facing design approval process that runs within the Design & Engineering stage. This is a critical sub-workflow with its own status tracking.

Status Flow Design Approval Status

Review In Progress→ Draft Complete→ Sent For Approval→ Design Approved
Rejection path: Design Rejected→ In Revision→ DA Revision Ready To Send→ Resent For Approval
Blocked: Needs Clarification Pending Sales Changes Pending Ops Changes Pending Design Changes Pending Resurvey Pending Review

Procedure

1. Design Ready for Review AUTO

Design Approval Ready Date auto-recorded when design enters review status.

2. Sent to Customer AUTO (PandaDoc)

When the design approval PandaDoc is sent to the customer, Design Approval Status auto-updates to "Sent For Approval".

AUTO PandaDoc sent → Design Approval Status → "Sent For Approval"
AUTO Is DA sent? → Yes
AUTO Design Approval Sent Date recorded
CALC Time between DA Ready and DA Sent
CALC Time Between Survey Schedule Date & DA Ready Date
CALC Time Between Survey Schedule Date & DA Sent Date
NOTIF Design Approval Update - Email + Design Approval Update - SMS sent to customer

3. Customer Responds AUTO (PandaDoc)

If Approved

Customer signs/approves the PandaDoc → Design Approval Status auto-updates to "Design Approved".

AUTO PandaDoc approved → Design Approval Status → "Design Approved"
AUTO Is Design Approved? → Yes
AUTO Design Approval Date recorded
CALC DA Turnaround Time
CALC Time to DA (total time from sale to DA)
AUTO DA Last 365 Days flag set
When DA is approved and Design Status is "Draft Complete - Waiting on Approvals", the Final Design Review task is triggered for the Design Lead.

If Rejected

Customer rejects the PandaDoc → Design Approval Status auto-updates to "Design Rejected".

AUTO PandaDoc rejected → Design Approval Status → "Design Rejected"
AUTO DA Revision Counter incremented
AUTO First DA Rejection Reason / DA Rejection Reason captured from Design Approval Notes from Customer

Revision cycle: In Revision → DA Revision Ready To Send → Resent For Approval (re-sent via PandaDoc).

If re-sent: AUTO DA Revision Complete Date recorded.

Key Fields

Design Approval StatusAUTOFully driven by PandaDoc — auto-set when sent, approved, or rejected
Design Approval Ready DateAUTOWhen design entered review
Is DA sent?AUTOFlips when sent for approval
Design Approval Sent DateAUTODate DA was sent to customer
Is Design Approved?AUTOYes when customer approves
