sections.title: Design & Engineering
sections.content:
Design & Engineering

Full engineering design process from survey handoff through design completion and stage advance to Permitting.

Design Dashboard

At a glance: After the site survey, the design team creates the solar system plan. Here's the flow:
  • Vishtik (design vendor) creates the initial design and uploads it
  • Design Lead reviews → customer approves the design via PandaDoc (the "DA")
  • Once approved, engineering stamps the plans
  • When stamped plans are uploaded, the deal automatically advances to Permitting
  • If the customer rejects the design, it goes through a revision cycle

Status Flow Design Status

Ready for Design→ In Progress→ Ready For Review→ Final Review/Stamping→ Draft Complete - Waiting on Approvals→ DA Approved→ Submitted To Engineering→ Design Complete
DA revision path: Revision Needed - DA Rejected→ DA Revision In Progress→ DA Revision Completed
Permit revision path: Revision Needed - Rejected by AHJ→ Permit Revision In Progress→ Permit Revision Completed
Utility revision path: Revision Needed - Rejected by Utility→ Utility Revision In Progress→ Utility Revision Completed
As-Built revision path: Revision Needed - As-Built→ As-Built Revision In Progress→ As-Built Revision Completed
Blocked / Clarification: Needs Clarification Needs Clarification from Customer Needs Clarification from Sales Needs Clarification from Operations Pending Resurvey On Hold No Design Needed
New Construction path: New Construction - Design Needed→ New Construction - In Progress→ New Construction - Ready for Review→ New Construction - Design Completed
Xcel path: Xcel - Design Needed→ Xcel - In Progress→ Xcel - Site Plan & SLD Completed

Workflow-Driven Task Flow (7-Step)

Design statuses are driven by 7 HubSpot workflows in the Design Flow / Normal Process folder, shared between Vishtik (external design company) and the Design Lead. Each task completion triggers a workflow that auto-advances the Design Status.

#WorkflowTask / TriggerAssigned ToStatus After
01Design In ProgressRetrieve Project Details completedVishtikAUTO → "In Progress"
02Design Uploaded / Ready for ReviewUpload Completed Design completedVishtikAUTO → "Ready For Review"
03Initial Review CompleteComplete Initial Design Review completedDesign LeadAUTO → "Draft Complete - Waiting on Approvals"
04DA Approved / Final Design ReviewDA approved by customerDesign LeadAUTO Creates "Final Design Review" task
05Final Review CompleteComplete Final Design Review completedDesign LeadAUTO Solar → "Submitted To Engineering" + stamps task; Non-solar → "Design Complete"
06Design Stamps in ProgressRetrieve Plans for Stamping completedVishtikAUTO → stamps in progress
07Stamped Plans Uploaded / Design CompleteUpload Stamped Plans completedVishtikAUTO → "Design Complete"
Workflow 05 branches on Project Type: Solar and Solar Add-On projects route to engineering stamps (Vishtik tasks 06–07). Non-solar projects skip stamps and go directly to "Design Complete." This is why some projects have 5 steps and others have 7.
Additional Design Flow folders: Beyond these 7 core workflows, the Design Flow folder contains 12 date stamp workflows, 12+ revision workflows (DA, Permitting, Interconnection, As-Built), 2 new construction workflows, 3 Xcel-specific workflows, and 7 D&R/Service workflows.

Procedure

1. Design Queue Entry AUTO

After site survey completes (Zuper sync), deal enters Design & Engineering stage. Design Lead is already assigned at project start.

AUTO Design Status defaults to "Ready for Design"

Vishtik receives first task automatically.

2. Vishtik Retrieves & Designs AUTO (Tasks 1–2)

Vishtik retrieves project details (Task 1), then uploads completed design (Task 2). Each task completion auto-advances the status.

AUTO Task 1 complete → Design Status → "In Progress"
AUTO Task 2 complete → Design Status → "Ready For Review"

3. Design Lead Initial Review AUTO (Task 3)

Design Lead completes initial design review.

AUTO Task 3 complete → Design Status → "Draft Complete - Waiting on Approvals"

At this point, the DA PandaDoc is sent to the customer (see Design Approval).

4. DA Approved → Final Design Review AUTO (Workflow 04)

Triggered when DA is approved. Creates "Final Design Review" task for the Design Lead.

AUTO DA approved → Final Design Review task created for Design Lead

5. Final Review Complete AUTO (Workflow 05)

Design Lead completes the final design review. This workflow branches based on Project Type:

AUTO Solar / Solar Add-On: Design Status → "Submitted To Engineering" + Engineering Submission Date recorded + "Retrieve Plans for Stamping" task created for Vishtik
AUTO Non-solar: Design Status → "Design Complete" (skips engineering stamps)

6. Engineering Stamps AUTO (Workflows 06–07, solar only)

Vishtik retrieves plans for stamping (Workflow 06), then uploads stamped plans (Workflow 07).

AUTO Workflow 06 → stamps in progress
AUTO Workflow 07 → Design Status → "Design Complete"

7. Design Complete AUTO

Whether via Workflow 05 (non-solar) or Workflow 07 (solar), reaching "Design Complete" triggers stage advance.

AUTO Is Design Completed? → Yes
AUTO Design Completion Date recorded
CALC Design Turnaround Time (time from survey to design complete)
CALC DA To Permit (time from DA approved to permit issued)
AUTO Deal stage advances to Permitting & Interconnection

7. Design Revisions

If revisions are needed (DA rejection, AHJ rejection, utility rejection, or as-built), the appropriate revision path is triggered.

AUTO Revision Counter incremented
AUTO Revision tasks assigned to Vishtik / Design Lead as needed

Key Fields

FieldSet ByPurpose
Design StatusAUTODriven by 7-step workflow (Vishtik + Design Lead)
Design LeadAUTOAssigned at project start — reviews and approves designs
Is Design Completed?AUTOFlips when design_status = Design Complete
Design Completion DateAUTODate design was completed
Engineering Submission DateAUTODate engineering was submitted
Engineering Stamped DateAUTODate stamped plans received
Design Turnaround TimeCALCSurvey to design complete duration
Revision CounterAUTONumber of design revisions

sections.title: Permitting
sections.content:
Permitting

Permit application, tracking, and approval process. One half of the RTB dual-gate.

Permitting Dashboard

At a glance: Permitting is one of two gates before construction can start (the other is Interconnection).
  • Normal path: permit goes straight from "Ready for Permitting" → "Submitted to AHJ" (the "Submitted to Customer" and "Customer Signature" steps only trigger when the AHJ requires it — based on a property on the AHJ record associated to the deal)
  • When the permit is issued, someone manually sets the status to "Permit Issued"
  • This is a manual step — the AHJ doesn't update HubSpot directly
  • Once both permit AND interconnection are approved (and DA invoice paid), the deal moves to Ready To Build

Status Flow Permitting Status

Ready For Permitting→ Submitted To Customer→ Customer Signature Acquired→ Submitted to AHJ→ Permit Issued
Rejection path: Permit Rejected - Needs Revision→ Design Revision In Progress→ Revision Ready To Resubmit→ Resubmitted to AHJ
Non-design rejection: Non-Design Related Rejection
SolarApp path: Ready to Submit for SolarApp→ Submit SolarApp to AHJ→ Permit Issued
As-Built path: As-Built Revision Needed→ As-Built Revision In Progress→ As-Built Ready To Resubmit→ As-Built Revision Resubmitted
Other: Awaiting Utility Approval Waiting On Information Not Needed

Procedure

1. Queue Entry AUTO

When deal enters Permitting & Interconnection stage, permit work begins. Permit Lead is already assigned at project start.

2. Submit to Customer AUTO (task)

Permit application package is prepared and submitted to customer for signature via task workflow.

AUTO Task completion → Permitting Status → "Submitted To Customer"

3. Customer Signature & AHJ Submission AUTO (task)

Once customer signature is acquired, the permit application is submitted to the AHJ via task workflow.

AUTO Task completion → Permitting Status → "Customer Signature Acquired"
AUTO Task completion → Permitting Status → "Submitted to AHJ"
AUTO Permit Submit Date recorded

Check for SolarApp eligibility — if eligible, follow the SolarApp fast-track path ("Ready to Submit for SolarApp" → "Submit SolarApp to AHJ").

4. Track Review

Monitor AHJ response. If rejected, the revision path is followed: "Permit Rejected - Needs Revision" → "Design Revision In Progress" → "Revision Ready To Resubmit" → "Resubmitted to AHJ".

5. Permit Issued MANUAL

When you receive the permit from the AHJ, set Permitting Status to "Permit Issued" in HubSpot. This is a manual step — the AHJ doesn't update HubSpot directly.
HubSpot records the permit date, calculates turnaround time, notifies the customer, and checks the RTB gate.
TRIGGER Permitting Status changes to "Permit Issued"
AUTO Is Permit Issued? → Yes
AUTO Permit Issue Date recorded
CALC Time to Permit
CALC Permit Turnaround Time (submission to issued)
NOTIF Permit Status Update - SMS + Permit Status Update - Email
AUTO RTB Gate check triggered (see RTB Gate section)
As-Built permits: Some jurisdictions require as-built permits after construction. Track separately using the As-Built path statuses. This does NOT affect the RTB gate — only the initial permit matters for stage advance.

Automated Follow-Up Cadence

HubSpot workflows automatically send follow-up reminders to the Permit Lead when a permit remains outstanding:

IntervalAction
14 days after submissionAUTO First follow-up reminder
30 days after submissionAUTO Second follow-up reminder
45 days after submissionAUTO Third follow-up reminder
60 days after submissionAUTO Escalation follow-up

Key Fields

FieldSet ByPurpose
Permitting StatusAUTO (task) / MANUALTask-driven through normal flow; "Permit Issued" is set manually
Permit LeadAUTOAssigned at project start
Is Permit Submitted?AUTOFlips when permit application submitted to AHJ
Is Permit Issued?AUTOFlips when permit issued
Permit Submit DateAUTODate application submitted
Permit Issue DateAUTODate permit was issued
Permit Turnaround TimeCALCSubmission to issued duration
Time to PermitCALCSale to permit issued duration
PV Permit #MANUALPV (solar) permit number from AHJ
Electrical Permit #MANUALElectrical permit number (if separate)
ESS Permit #MANUALEnergy storage system permit number (if separate)
Zoning Permit #MANUALZoning permit number (if required)
Fire Protection Permit #MANUALFire protection permit number (if required)

sections.title: Interconnection
sections.content:
Interconnection

Utility interconnection application and approval. The second half of the RTB dual-gate.

Interconnection Dashboard

Status Flow Interconnection Status

Ready for Interconnection→ Submitted To Customer→ Ready To Submit→ Submitted To Utility→ In Review→ Application Approved
Pre-design: Ready To Submit - Pending Design
Rejection path: Rejected→ Rejected - Revisions Needed→ Design Revision In Progress→ Revision Ready To Resubmit→ Resubmitted To Utility
Non-design rejection: Non-Design Related Rejection
Pending signatures: Application Approved - Pending Signatures Conditional Application Approval
Blocked / Waiting: Waiting On Information Waiting on Utility Bill Waiting on New Construction Transformer Upgrade Supplemental Review RBC On Hold Pending Rebate Approval
Xcel path: Xcel Site Plan & SLD Needed
Other: Not Needed

Procedure

1. Queue Entry AUTO

Runs in parallel with permitting. Interconnections Lead is already assigned at project start.

2. Submit to Customer AUTO (task)

Interconnection application is prepared and submitted to customer for signature via task workflow.

AUTO Task completion → Interconnection Status → "Submitted To Customer"

Note: If design is not yet complete, status may be set to "Ready To Submit - Pending Design". MPU requirements identified during survey may also block interconnection submission.

3. Customer Signature & Utility Submission AUTO (task)

Once customer signature is acquired, the application is submitted to the utility via task workflow.

AUTO Task completion → Interconnection Status → "Ready To Submit"
AUTO Task completion → Interconnection Status → "Submitted To Utility"
AUTO Interconnection Submit Date recorded

4. Track Review

Monitor utility response. Handle corrections or rejections as needed.

Track blockers in Interconnection Blocker field if delays occur (Transformer Upgrade, Supplemental Review, RBC On Hold, etc.).

5. Interconnection Approved MANUAL

Manually set Interconnection Status → "Application Approved" once the utility approves the application.

TRIGGER Interconnection Status changes to "Application Approved"
AUTO Is Interconnection Approved? → Yes
AUTO Interconnection Approval Date recorded
CALC Interconnection Turnaround Time
CALC Time to Interconnection
NOTIF Interconnection Status Update - SMS + Interconnection Status Update - Email
AUTO RTB Gate check triggered (see RTB Gate section)

Key Fields

FieldSet ByPurpose
Interconnection StatusAUTO (task) / MANUALTask-driven through normal flow; "Application Approved" is set manually
Interconnections LeadAUTOAssigned at project start
Is Interconnection Submitted?AUTOFlips when interconnection application submitted to utility
Is Interconnection Approved?AUTOFlips when interconnection approved
Interconnection Submit DateAUTODate interconnection application submitted
Interconnection Approval DateAUTODate interconnection was approved
Interconnection Turnaround TimeCALCSubmission to approval duration
Interconnection BlockerMANUALBlocker reason if delayed

sections.title: RTB Gate
sections.content:
RTB Gate (Ready To Build)

The multi-condition gate that controls when a project can move to construction. Permit issued, interconnection approved, AND DA invoice paid must all be satisfied.

BOM Tool

At a glance: A project can't start construction until three things are true:
  • ✅ Permit issued — the local building authority approved the plans
  • ✅ Interconnection approved — the utility approved the grid connection
  • ✅ DA invoice paid — the customer paid the design approval milestone
When all three are met, the deal automatically moves to Ready To Build. If any are missing, it goes to "RTB - Blocked" with the reason tracked.

Gate Logic

ConditionFieldRequired Value
PermitIs Permit Issued?Yes
InterconnectionIs Interconnection Approved?Yes
DA InvoiceDA Invoice StatusPaid
RTB Gate Logic: AUTO When a permit is issued, the system checks the other conditions. If ALL three are met (permit issued + interconnection approved + DA invoice paid) → deal auto-advances to Ready To Build. If any condition is incomplete → deal moves to RTB - Blocked with the blocking reason tracked. Once the remaining conditions clear, the deal auto-advances from RTB - Blocked to Ready To Build.

RTB - Blocked

When permit is issued but other conditions are not yet met, the deal enters RTB - Blocked.

AUTO RTB Blocked Reason set based on which condition is missing:
ScenarioBlocked Reason
Permit issued, interconnection pending"Waiting on Interconnection Approval"
Permit issued, DA invoice unpaid"Waiting on DA Invoice Payment"
Permit issued, interconnection pending + DA invoice unpaidMultiple blockers tracked
Interconnection approved, permit pendingDeal stays in Permitting & Interconnection stage

When all remaining conditions clear, the deal auto-advances from RTB - Blocked to Ready To Build and becomes eligible for Construction scheduling.

Ready To Build Stage

AUTO Deal stage → Ready To Build
CALC Time to RTB (sale to RTB)
CALC Time Between Permit & RTB
NOTIF RTB Update - SMS + RTB Update - Email sent to customer
⚠ Needs Review: Preconstruction review checklist items are inferred from typical RTB-to-construction handoff — confirm against PB's actual preconstruction process.

Preconstruction Review

Once a deal reaches Ready To Build, the Preconstruction Lead reviews the project before it enters the construction scheduler:

CheckWhat to VerifyWhere
Equipment orderedAll BOM items ordered or in stockZoho Sales Order status
Permit on fileApproved permit document attached to dealDeal attachments / permit portal
IC approval letterUtility interconnection approval receivedInterconnection Status field
Design currentNo pending revisions — permitted plans match final designDesign Status field
Customer contactCustomer reachable and aware of upcoming installDeal notes / last communication
RTB → Construction handoff: Once the preconstruction review passes, the project is eligible for scheduling on the construction scheduler. The Project Manager schedules the install date; the Operations Lead assigns crew members.

Key Fields

FieldSet ByPurpose
Is Permit Issued?AUTOGate condition 1
Is Interconnection Approved?AUTOGate condition 2
DA Invoice StatusAUTOGate condition 3 — DA invoice must be paid
RTB Blocked ReasonAUTOWhy project is blocked from RTB
Time to RTBCALCTotal time from sale to RTB

sections.title: Construction
sections.content:
Construction

Physical installation of solar system. Status driven by Zuper Pro field sync with Zuper checklist for completion.

Construction Scheduler Construction Dashboard BOM Tool

At a glance: This is the actual installation. Here's the flow:
  • Project Manager schedules the install; Operations Lead assigns the crew
  • Crew updates their status in Zuper (On Our Way → Started → In Progress) — it auto-syncs to HubSpot
  • When done, submit the Construction Complete Checklist in Zuper
  • That checklist triggers everything: stage advance to Inspection, M2 invoice, customer notification
  • Don't submit the checklist early — it's irreversible and fires invoicing

Status Flow Construction Status

Ready to Build→ Scheduled→ On Our Way→ Started→ In Progress→ Construction Complete
Revision path: Revisions Needed→ In Design For Revisions→ Revisions Complete
Other: Blocked Rejected Loose Ends Remaining Pending New Construction Design Review

Procedure

1. Schedule Installation ZUPER SYNC

When construction is scheduled in the scheduler or directly in Zuper, Construction Status auto-updates to "Scheduled".

ZUPER Construction scheduled in Zuper/scheduler → Construction Status → "Scheduled"
AUTO Deal stage → Construction (if not already there)
AUTO Is Construction Scheduled? → Yes
NOTIF Construction Update - SMS + Construction Update - Email

2. Field Installation ZUPER SYNC

Field crew updates job status in Zuper Pro. Auto-syncs to HubSpot:

ZUPER Construction Status: On Our Way → Started → In Progress
ZUPER Project Manager - Zuper assigned

3. Complete Construction CHECKLIST

Submit the Construction Complete Checklist in Zuper when the install is fully done.
HubSpot records the completion date, advances the deal to Inspection, fires the M2 (CC) invoice, and notifies the customer.
CHECKLIST Construction Complete Checklist submitted
AUTO Construction Status → Construction Complete
AUTO Construction Complete Date recorded
AUTO Is Construction Complete? → Yes
CALC Construction Turnaround Time
CALC Time from RTB to CC
CALC Time to CC (total sale to construction complete)
AUTO Deal stage → Inspection
AUTO M2 (CC) invoice workflow triggered
NOTIF Construction Update - SMS + Construction Update - Email
Do NOT submit the Construction Complete Checklist if installation is only partial. Use "Partial Install" status and document what remains. Submitting the checklist triggers the stage advance and M2 (CC) invoicing.

Pre-Install Preparation

Before the install date, operations should verify:

CheckWhere to Verify
Permit issued?Left Sidebar → Permitting → Is Permit Issued? = Yes
Interconnection approved?Left Sidebar → Interconnection → Interconnection Status = "Application Approved"
Equipment ordered / in stock?Left Sidebar → Equipment/System group — verify BOM matches what's available
Crew assigned?Construction Scheduler — verify crew and date are confirmed (not just tentative)
Customer notified?Auto-sent when status changes to "Scheduled" — check Activities tab for delivery confirmation

Construction Complete Checklist

The CC checklist is filled out in Zuper at construction completion (synced to HubSpot's Center Panel → Zuper & Operations tab). It captures:

Checklist ItemPurpose
Panels Installed CountConfirm actual panel count matches the plan. Discrepancies may need a change order.
Loose Ends Remaining?If Yes, document what remains. Does NOT block stage advance but flags follow-up needed.
Permitted Plans Accurate?If No, as-built plans may be needed. Flag issues for the permitting team.
What Changed from Plans?Free-text documenting any field changes from the permitted design.
Ready for Inspection?If Yes, the deal advances and inspection scheduling can begin.
Inspection Checklist ItemsPre-flight items the field crew verifies before calling for inspection (labels, grounding, etc.).
After submission: The checklist triggers stage advance to Inspection, records the Construction Complete Date, fires the M2 (CC) invoice workflow, and sends customer notifications. This is irreversible — don't submit until the install is truly complete.

Construction Forecasting

Planning fields in the Center Panel → Zuper & Operations tab help operations estimate crew and time requirements:

FieldHow It's Used
Expected Install DaysEstimated days for the full install. Multi-day installs need consecutive scheduling.
Installer CountHow many installers needed. Larger systems may require 3-4 crew members.
Electrician CountDedicated electricians needed (separate from general installers).
Difficulty Rating1-5 scale. Higher difficulty may need senior crews or extra time.
Disco/Reco Needed?If disconnect/reconnect work is needed (affects utility coordination).
Interior Access Required?Customer must be home if interior access is needed for battery install, sub-panel, etc.

Revision Path

If field conditions require design changes during construction:

MANUAL Set Construction Status → "Revisions Needed"
AUTO Design team creates revision → Construction Status → "In Design For Revisions"
AUTO Revisions complete → Construction Status → "Revisions Complete" — reschedule install
Revisions do NOT reset the stage. The deal stays in Construction stage throughout the revision process. Only the Construction Status cycles through the revision sub-statuses.

Key Fields

FieldSet ByPurpose
Construction StatusZUPER / AUTOCurrent installation status
Construction Schedule DateZUPERScheduled installation date (set from Zuper/scheduler)
Is Construction Scheduled?AUTOFlips when Zuper/scheduler sets status to Scheduled
Is Construction Complete?AUTOFlips when checklist submitted
Construction Complete DateAUTODate construction completed
Construction Turnaround TimeCALCSchedule to completion duration
Time from RTB to CCCALCRTB to construction complete
Time to CCCALCTotal sale to construction complete
Project Manager - ZuperZUPERPM assigned from Zuper
Kickoff Completion DateAUTODate the PM reviewed and kicked off the project
Construction Completed ByZUPERField user who completed construction (from Zuper checklist)

sections.title: Inspection
sections.content:
Inspection

Final building inspection by the AHJ. Pass or fail with distinct automation paths for each outcome.

Inspection Scheduler Inspections Dashboard

At a glance: After construction, the local building authority (AHJ) inspects the installation.
  • Inspections Lead schedules the inspection via Zuper or the scheduler
  • If it passes: the deal automatically advances to PTO and the customer gets notified
  • If it fails: use the Inspection Failed Checklist in Zuper (not the job status — there's a known sync issue)
  • After corrections, re-schedule the inspection. The fail count tracks how many attempts.

Status Flow Final Inspection Status

Ready For Inspection→ Scheduled→ On Our Way→ Started→ In Progress→ Passed
Fail path: Failed Rejected→ Waiting on Permit Revisions→ Revisions Complete
Other: Partial Pass Not Needed Pending New Construction Sign Off Pending Fire Inspection Pending BUS Install Pending New Construction

Assignment

Inspection coordination is handled by the Inspections Lead and Operations Lead assigned on the deal. Tasks are automatically routed to the assigned Inspections Lead.

Procedure

1. Schedule Inspection ZUPER SYNC

When the inspection is scheduled in the scheduler or directly in Zuper, Final Inspection Status auto-updates to "Scheduled".

ZUPER Inspection scheduled in Zuper/scheduler → Final Inspection Status → "Scheduled"

2. Day of Inspection ZUPER SYNC

Zuper auto-syncs field status: On Our Way → Started → In Progress.

ZUPER Final Inspection Status synced from field

3a. Inspection Passed ZUPER SYNC

When the inspector passes, mark the inspection as passed in Zuper.
HubSpot records the pass date, advances the deal to PTO, and notifies the customer.
ZUPER Inspection passes in field → Final Inspection Status syncs to "Passed"
AUTO Is Inspection Passed? → Yes
AUTO Inspections Pass Date recorded
AUTO PTO Status → "Inspection Passed - Ready for PTO Submission"
AUTO Deal stage → Permission To Operate (PTO)
NOTIF Inspection Update - SMS + Inspection Update - Email

3b. Inspection Failed CHECKLIST

Known Race Condition — Zuper Inspection Sync: When an inspection fails in Zuper, the system auto-creates a new job, overwriting the "Failed" status before HubSpot captures it. Always use the Inspection Failed Checklist in Zuper as the reliable method for failure tracking.
Submit the Inspection Failed Checklist in Zuper (not the job status — use the dedicated checklist).
HubSpot records the failure reason and increments the fail count.
CHECKLIST Inspection Failed Checklist submitted
AUTO Final Inspection Status → Failed
AUTO Inspection Failure Reason captured
AUTO Inspection Fail Count incremented

MANUAL Address corrections. Set Final Inspection Status → "Corrections In Progress" → "Re-Inspection Scheduled".

Pre-Inspection Checklist

Before scheduling an inspection, the Inspections Lead should verify:

CheckWhere to Verify
Construction Complete Checklist submitted?Center Panel → Zuper & Operations tab — checklist should show as submitted
"Ready for Inspection" = Yes?CC checklist response — if No, follow up with field crew on outstanding items
All labels and grounding complete?CC checklist inspection items — verify field crew confirmed pre-flight items
Permit on file?Left Sidebar → Permitting → Is Permit Issued? = Yes. AHJ may require permit on-site.
Customer aware?Some AHJs require customer presence. Check AHJ requirements via AHJ & Utility tab.

Inspection Failure Workflow

When an inspection fails, there's a specific recovery procedure:

StepActionWho
1. Submit Failure ChecklistFill out the Inspection Failed Checklist in Zuper with failure reason and corrections neededInspections Lead
2. Route CorrectionsIf electrical: assign to electrician crew. If structural: assign to install crew. If permit issue: route to permitting.Operations Lead
3. Complete CorrectionsField crew addresses all failure items. Update status to "Corrections In Progress".Field Crew
4. Re-Schedule InspectionSchedule new inspection in Inspection Scheduler. Status → "Re-Inspection Scheduled".Inspections Lead
5. Re-InspectionSame flow as initial inspection. Inspection Fail Count tracks attempts.AHJ Inspector
Tracking: Inspection Fail Count increments with each failure. First Time Inspection Pass? is set to No after any failure. These metrics feed into the compliance dashboard for crew quality tracking.

Special Inspection Types

StatusWhen Used
Partial PassInspector approves most work but flags minor items. Corrections needed but a full re-inspection may not be required — check with AHJ.
Pending Fire InspectionSome jurisdictions require a separate fire department inspection for battery installations.
Pending New Construction Sign OffNew construction projects may need builder sign-off before AHJ final inspection.
Pending BUS InstallBackup switch installation pending — required before certain inspections can proceed.

Key Fields

FieldSet ByPurpose
Final Inspection StatusZUPER / AUTOCurrent inspection status
Is Inspection Passed?AUTOFlips when passed

sections.title: PTO
sections.content:
Permission To Operate (PTO)

Utility permission to energize the solar system. Includes PTO application, monitoring setup, and Xcel-specific photo submission path.

At a glance: PTO is the utility's permission to turn on the solar system. It's the last step before Close Out.
  • After inspection passes, someone submits the PTO application to the utility
  • When granted, manually set the status to "PTO Granted"
  • That triggers: deal advances to Close Out, final invoice fires, customer gets notified
  • Xcel projects have an extra step — photo submission is required before PTO

Status Flow PTO Status

Inspection Passed - Ready for PTO Submission→ Inspection Submitted to Utility→ PTO Granted
Rejection path: PTO Submission Rejected Inspection Rejected By Utility Ops Related PTO Rejection→ PTO Revision Resubmitted
Xcel Photos path: Xcel Photos Ready to Submit→ Xcel Photos Submitted→ Xcel Photos Approved
Xcel Photos rejection: XCEL Photos Rejected→ Xcel Photos Ready to Resubmit→ Xcel Photos Resubmitted
Blocked / Waiting: PTO Waiting on Interconnection Approval Waiting On Information Waiting on New Construction Conditional PTO - Pending Transformer Upgrade Pending Truck Roll
Other: Not Needed

Procedure

1. PTO Entry AUTO

When inspection passes, deal auto-advances to PTO stage with status "Inspection Passed - Ready for PTO Submission".

2. Submit PTO Application MANUAL

Submit inspection docs to utility. Set PTO Status → "Inspection Submitted to Utility".

AUTO PTO Submit Date recorded

3. Xcel Photo Path (if applicable) MANUAL

For Xcel Energy projects, photo documentation is required. Set PTO Status → "Xcel Photos Ready to Submit" → "Xcel Photos Submitted" → "Xcel Photos Approved". If rejected: "XCEL Photos Rejected" → "Xcel Photos Ready to Resubmit" → "Xcel Photos Resubmitted".

4. PTO Granted TRIGGER

Set PTO Status → "PTO Granted"

TRIGGER PTO Status changes to "PTO Granted"

After PTO, set up system monitoring. Track via Monitoring Setup Status.

sections.title: Close Out
sections.content:
Close Out

Final project wrap-up after PTO. Verify invoicing, incentives, documentation, and customer satisfaction before marking project complete.

At a glance: Close Out is the final cleanup after PTO is granted. Make sure everything is buttoned up:
  • Verify all invoices are sent and paid (DA, CC, PTO milestones)
  • Confirm incentive paperwork is submitted (SGIP, 3CE, rebates)
  • Set up monitoring and verify the system is producing
  • Mark the deal as "Project Complete" when everything checks out

Procedure

1. Close Out Entry AUTO

Deal auto-advances to Close Out when PTO is granted.

NOTIF Closeout Update - SMS + Closeout Update - Email

2. Invoice Verification MANUAL

Verify all milestone invoices are issued and paid:

InvoiceTriggerFields
M1 — Design Approval (DA)Design Approval approvedM1 Invoice Status
M2 — Construction Complete (CC)Construction Complete Checklist submittedM2 Invoice Status
PTO InvoicePTO GrantedPTO Invoice Status

3. Incentive Verification MANUAL

Confirm all applicable incentive/rebate applications are submitted or approved. See Incentives & Rebates.

⚠ Needs Review: Close-out checklist items, system activation verification steps, and monitoring platform details are inferred — confirm against PB's actual close-out procedure.

4. As-Built Documentation MANUAL

If the AHJ requires as-built documentation (or if changes were made during construction), the permitting status flow has a dedicated as-built revision path:

As-Built Revision Needed→ As-Built Revision In Progress→ As-Built Ready To Resubmit→ As-Built Revision Resubmitted

The Permit Lead handles as-built revisions. Check the Permitting Status field — if it's in an as-built status, the project cannot close until the revision is accepted.

5. System Activation MANUAL

Verify the solar system is turned on and producing. Confirm monitoring is set up and connected:

EquipmentMonitoring PlatformVerification
Tesla Inverter (residential)Tesla AppConfirm gateway is online and reporting production
Enphase MicroinvertersEnphase EnlightenConfirm all microinverters reporting; no offline units
Tesla PowerwallTesla AppConfirm battery is charging/discharging and gateway connected

6. Project Complete MANUAL

When all items verified, move deal stage to Project Complete.

AUTO Project Completion Date recorded
CALC Total Project Duration (sale to project complete)

Close Out Checklist

Before marking a project complete, verify all items:

ItemWhere to CheckStatus
PTO grantedPTO Status = PTO GrantedRequired
M1 (DA) invoice paidM1 Invoice Status = PaidRequired
M2 (CC) invoice paidM2 Invoice Status = PaidRequired
PTO invoice paidPTO Invoice Status = PaidRequired
Incentive applications submittedCPA Status, RRF Status, Incentive StatusRequired if applicable
As-built submitted (if required)Permitting Status not in as-built revisionRequired if AHJ requires
System monitoring connectedTesla App / Enphase EnlightenRequired
Customer aware of system operationDeal notes / closeout communication sentRecommended
Don't close prematurely. A project moved to Project Complete is considered fully done. If invoices are still outstanding or incentive applications haven't been submitted, keep the deal in Close Out until resolved.

sections.title: New Construction
sections.content:
New Construction

Special handling for new construction projects. These follow the same project pipeline stages but have additional workflows and modified timelines.

Identification

New construction projects are identified by the New Construction tag on the deal. This tag triggers specialized workflows throughout the pipeline.

TRIGGER Deal has New Construction tag

Key Differences by Stage

StageNew Construction DifferenceImpact
Site SurveySurvey may happen before framing is complete — need structural plans from builderDesign may start from builder blueprints rather than on-site measurements
DesignDesign depends on builder timelines and framing completion; revision paths differLonger design cycles — expect multiple revision rounds as builder plans change
PermittingSolar permit coordinated with builder's overall permit processMay be bundled with building permit or submitted as an addendum
ConstructionTwo-phase: pre-wire before drywall, full construction when house is readyRequires two separate crew dispatches; gap between phases depends on builder timeline
InspectionMultiple inspections coordinated with builder's inspection schedulePre-wire inspection + final inspection may be separate events
PTOPTO delayed until certificate of occupancy (CO) is issuedSystem cannot be energized until CO — may be weeks/months after solar install

Two-Phase Construction

New construction solar installations happen in two phases:

PhaseTimingWork Performed
Pre-WireAfter framing, before drywallConduit runs, junction boxes, wire pulls, inverter mounting location prep
ConstructionWhen house is readyPanel mounting, inverter install, battery install, final wiring, labeling
Scheduling complexity: Both phases must be coordinated with the builder's schedule. The gap between pre-wire and construction can be weeks or months. Track the builder's projected timeline in deal notes.

Builder Coordination

Coordination PointWhoWhen
Structural plans for designBuilder → PB DesignBefore design starts
Pre-wire scheduleBuilder → PB OpsWhen framing is complete
Drywall hold (if conduit needs verification)PB → BuilderAfter pre-wire, before drywall
Drone site surveyPB OpsOnce install can be scheduled — capture aerial imagery of the site
Construction scheduleBuilder → PB OpsWhen house is ready
Certificate of OccupancyBuilder → PB PTOBefore PTO application

Automated Workflows

7 HubSpot workflows are triggered by the New Construction tag, handling stage-specific automation adjustments within the same project pipeline. These run in parallel with standard workflows but modify timing, notifications, and task assignments for new construction projects.

TRIGGER All 7 workflows key off the New Construction tag — removing the tag will stop the new construction workflow overrides

sections.content:
On-Hold & Cancellation

Procedures for pausing or cancelling projects at any stage.

On-Hold

Any deal can be placed on hold at any stage. The On-Hold stage is tracked separately from the main pipeline flow.

Place on Hold MANUAL

MANUAL Set On-Hold Reason (dropdown) — choose from:

Waiting on Internal Processes

Resume from Hold MANUAL

Move deal back to the appropriate active stage. The system will track total time spent on hold.

StepAction
1. Verify hold reason resolvedCheck deal notes and On-Hold Notes — ensure the blocking issue is actually cleared
2. Determine correct return stageMove deal to the stage it should be in NOW (not necessarily where it was before the hold)
3. Update notesDocument when and why the hold was lifted in deal notes
4. Re-engage customerIf hold was extended (2+ weeks), contact the customer to confirm they're still proceeding
⚠ Needs Review: Hold management best practices, resume procedure, and pre-cancellation checklist are inferred — confirm against PB's actual hold/cancel procedures.

Hold Management Best Practices

PracticeWhy
Review on-hold deals weeklyPrevents deals from being forgotten — check the On-Hold pipeline view regularly
Set a follow-up date in notes"Check back on [date]" helps track when to revisit the hold
Don't use On-Hold as a parking lotIf a project truly won't proceed, cancel it — On-Hold is for temporary pauses
Document every hold reason changeIf the reason for the hold changes, update On-Hold Reason and add a note

Cancellation

Cancel Project MANUAL

Move deal stage to Cancelled.

MANUAL Set Cancellation Reason
MANUAL Set Cancellation Notes with details
AUTO Cancellation Date recorded

Pre-Cancellation Checklist

CheckAction if Yes
Equipment already ordered?Cancel or return the order — check Zoho SO status
Permit already submitted?Withdraw the permit application if possible
Interconnection application submitted?Notify utility of cancellation
Incentive applications submitted?Cancel incentive applications (CPA, RRF, etc.)
Customer paid any invoices?Process refund per company policy
Zuper jobs scheduled?Cancel any scheduled Zuper jobs
Cancellations are tracked but not easily reversed. Ensure the cancellation is confirmed before moving the deal. If a cancelled project restarts, document the restart reason in notes and move the deal back to the appropriate active stage.

sections.title: Service Pipeline
sections.content:
Service Pipeline

Post-installation service and warranty work. Handles troubleshooting, repairs, and system maintenance.

Service Dashboard

Pipeline Stages

Project Preparation→ Site Visit Scheduling→ Work In Progress→ Inspection→ Invoicing→ Completed
Other:Cancelled
⚠ Needs Review: Service triage categories (warranty/workmanship/billable), pre-dispatch checklist, WIP scenarios, and invoicing rules are inferred from standard solar service operations — confirm they match PB's process.

Procedure

1. Project Preparation MANUAL

Receive service request and create a deal in the Service Pipeline. Triage the issue:

DeterminationActionInvoice?
WarrantyFile warranty claim with manufacturer (Tesla, Enphase, etc.)No — covered by manufacturer warranty
WorkmanshipPB crew fixes at no chargeNo — PB responsibility
Billable ServiceQuote customer, get approval before dispatchingYes — invoice after completion
Troubleshooting OnlyRemote diagnosis first; site visit if neededDepends on diagnosis outcome

Document the issue in deal notes. Pull the original project deal (if available) for system specs and install history. Assign the appropriate service tech.

2. Site Visit Scheduling MANUAL / ZUPER SYNC

Schedule the service visit through Zuper. The Service Visit category in Zuper maps to this pipeline stage.

Before DispatchingVerify
Original install detailsPull equipment list, inverter type, panel count from original project deal
Parts availabilityIf replacement parts are needed, confirm they're in stock or ordered
Customer accessConfirm customer will be home or provide access instructions
Safety considerationsNote any roof access issues, dogs, locked gates, etc. from original survey

3. Work In Progress ZUPER SYNC

Service work performed on site. Zuper status syncs back to HubSpot. Tech documents findings and work performed in Zuper job notes.

ScenarioNext Step
Issue resolved on first visitMove to Inspection (if permit-required work) or Invoicing
Parts needed — not in stockOrder parts, keep in Work In Progress, schedule return visit
Escalation neededDocument in notes, assign to senior tech or manufacturer support
Different issue found on siteDocument new findings, update deal scope, notify customer if cost changes

4. Inspection (if needed) MANUAL

Required if the service work involved permitted modifications (panel replacement, re-wiring, structural changes). Schedule inspection with AHJ using the inspection scheduler.

5. Invoicing MANUAL

Issue invoice for billable service work. For warranty work, document the manufacturer claim details for tracking.

Service TypeInvoicing Action
Warranty claimNo invoice to customer — file claim with manufacturer, track in deal notes
Workmanship fixNo invoice to customer — internal cost tracked
Billable serviceIssue invoice per quoted amount — track in Service Invoice Status

6. Completed MANUAL

Close out service deal. Document the resolution summary in deal notes for future reference. If this was a recurring issue, flag in notes for the operations team.

sections.title: Your Deal Record
sections.content:
Sales — Your Deal Record

What you need to fill out and why it matters

Why Deal Data Quality Matters

Every field you fill in on the deal record flows downstream. Incomplete data causes delays:

  • Missing address → design team can't pull permits
  • Missing utility info → interconnection can't be submitted
  • Missing roof type → wrong racking ordered
  • Missing contact info → customer doesn't get notified about install dates

Critical Fields at Close

When you close a deal and it moves from the Sales Pipeline to the Project Pipeline, make sure these fields are complete:

Pre-Sale Checklist (Deal Overview tab)

FieldWhy It Matters
AmountRevenue tracking, invoice milestones
Close DateTimeline calculations for all downstream stages
Project Number (PROJ-XXXX)Universal identifier across all systems
PB LocationRoutes to correct ops team and warehouse
Project TypeDetermines equipment and design approach
Customer SegmentResidential vs. commercial handling
Utility CompanyDrives interconnection and design requirements
Roof TypeDetermines racking and mounting hardware
Payment MethodAffects invoicing schedule
Tags / Incentives SelectionPE enrollment, rebates, special handling

Address & Contact

FieldWhy It Matters
Full Address (street, city, state, zip)Permit applications, crew dispatch, AHJ lookup
Latitude / LongitudeSolar production modeling, route planning
Associated ContactCustomer notifications, DA delivery, scheduling calls

Deal Tags

Tags flag special handling requirements. Common tags that affect downstream work:

  • Participate Energy tags — triggers PE milestone tracking with financial deadlines
  • Non-PB Install — different crew handling
  • Battery Rebate tags — affects incentive tracking
  • Roof Type tags — determines racking in the BOM

sections.title: Scheduling Surveys
sections.content:
Sales — Scheduling Surveys

How to book site surveys for your customers

Site Survey Scheduler

The Site Survey Scheduler is your primary scheduling tool. It allows you to book a site survey for your customer and assign a surveyor.

Open the Site Survey Scheduler from your dashboard.
Search for your deal by PROJ number or customer name.
Select an available date and surveyor. The scheduler shows surveyor availability and existing bookings.
Confirm the booking. This sets the Site Survey Booked Date and Site Surveyor fields on the deal record in HubSpot.
The survey syncs to Zuper — creating a Site Survey job for the assigned surveyor.

What Happens After the Survey

After the site survey is completed:

  1. The surveyor uploads photos and measurements to the Site Survey Documents folder
  2. Site Survey Status is updated to "Completed"
  3. The Site Survey Turnaround Time is calculated automatically
  4. The deal moves to Design & Engineering stage
  5. HubSpot workflows trigger the design process

Site Survey LeadAuto-assigned or manager
Site Survey Turnaround TimeCalculated automatically

sections.title: Sales → Ops Handoff
sections.content:
Sales — Sales → Ops Handoff

Clean handoffs mean faster installations

The Handoff Checklist

When your deal closes and moves from the Sales Pipeline to the Project Pipeline, the operations team takes over. A clean handoff means:

All Pre-Sale Checklist fields are complete. Missing fields = delays. Operations will send the deal back if critical info is missing.
Contact is associated. The customer's email and phone must be on the deal record — this is how they get notifications about survey, DA, and install scheduling.
Documents are uploaded. Contract, proposal, and any change orders should be in the Sales Documents folder on Google Drive (linked from the deal record).
Tags are set correctly. PE enrollment, rebates, special handling — set these at close so nothing is missed downstream.
Site Survey is scheduled (or ready to schedule). If you can schedule the survey before handoff, do it — it shaves days off the timeline.

What Ops Needs From You

CategoryRequired Fields
CustomerName, email, phone, associated contact on deal
SiteFull address, lat/lng, roof type, utility company
ProjectAmount, close date, PROJ number, PB location, project type
FinancialPayment method, incentives/tags, customer segment
DocumentsSigned contract in Google Drive

HubSpot Workflows After Handoff

Once the deal enters the Project Pipeline, HubSpot workflows automatically:

  • Assign a Project Manager based on location
  • Create initial tasks (Site Survey, Design, etc.)
  • Start tracking pipeline stage durations
  • Begin PE milestone timers (if PE tags are set)

sections.title: Tracking Your Projects
sections.content:
Sales — Tracking Your Projects

Stay informed about your deals after handoff

Sales Dashboard

The Sales Dashboard shows your pipeline from the sales perspective — deal values, stages, and status. Use it to:

  • See where your closed deals are in the project pipeline
  • Track which deals are approaching install (for customer communication)
  • Monitor your location's overall pipeline health

Looking Up a Deal

Use Deal Search to find any deal by PROJ number or customer name. You can see the current pipeline stage, key dates, and who is assigned to each role on the project team.

Staying in the Loop

You don't need to check dashboards daily — HubSpot sends notifications at key milestones. But if a customer asks "where is my project?", here's how to find out:

Project StageWhat's HappeningTypical Timeline
Site SurveySurveyor is visiting or has visited the site1–2 weeks after close
Design & EngineeringPlans being created, customer approval pending2–4 weeks
Permitting & InterconnectionPermits submitted, waiting on AHJ/utility2–8 weeks (varies by AHJ)
Ready To BuildPermits issued, waiting to be scheduled1–2 weeks
ConstructionInstall scheduled or in progress1–3 days on site
InspectionWaiting for or completed inspection1–4 weeks
PTOPermission to operate submitted to utility2–8 weeks
Close OutFinal paperwork, customer closeout packet1–2 weeks

Customer Communication Tips

  • Check the deal's Activity tab before calling a customer — see what emails and calls have already happened
  • The Agent Conversations card shows AI-analyzed sentiment on recent interactions — useful for gauging customer mood
  • Your salesperson name appears on the PM Guide's closeout email template (CC'd) — you'll be looped in at project completion

sections.title: Deal Intake & Review
sections.content:
Deal Intake & Review

First steps after a new deal is assigned to you as Project Manager.

Colorado — Westminster & Centennial • Colorado Springs
At a glance: When a deal is assigned, the PM reviews the contract, proposal, utility bill, and deal page for accuracy before any field work begins.
  • Deal is auto-created when the customer signs in OpenSolar
  • PM is auto-assigned based on project type (New Construction, Commercial, Residential)
  • System generates a PROJ number and Google Drive folder structure
  • PM gets HubSpot + email notifications of assignment

1. Deal Page & Notes Review

Open the deal page in HubSpot. Click the "Note" tab to review any notes left by Sales about the project.
Open Google Drive folder. Located in the "About this Deal" section on the left side. Navigate to the "Sales" folder.
Verify Sales documents are present:
  • Proposal uploaded
  • Signed agreement uploaded
  • Customer utility bill uploaded
If the utility bill is missing, the project can still proceed. Reach out to the sales rep to inquire about the status of the document.

2. Utility Bill Review

Verify address match. The service address on the utility bill must match the address listed in the deal.

3. Contract Review

  • Customer signature is present
  • Agreement price matches the "Amount" section on the deal page
  • Equipment listed in the contract matches the "Line Items" section on the right side of HubSpot
  • Address on contract matches the utility bill and deal service address
If "Line Items" is blank, click "+Add" to update. If equipment is not listed in the system, submit an IT ticket to request adding it.
PM to make any necessary changes/updates to ensure alignment across all documents.

4. Proposal Review

Set project type. In "About this Deal", click all that apply for "Project Type": Solar, Battery, Electrical Upgrades, EV Charger, and/or Solar Add-on.
Confirm payment type. Check the "Quotation" page of the proposal (typically page 11). Determine if cash or loan. Ensure the "Accounting" section in HubSpot matches.

5. Customer Contact Setup

  • Homeowner's contact page listed as an associated contact
  • Contact responsible for payment listed as "Payor"
  • If loan: person on the loan listed as "Payor" & "Loan Applicant"
  • If loan: loan provider added as associated contact and labeled "Payor"

sections.title: Schedule Site Survey
sections.content:
Schedule Site Survey

Goal: have the site survey scheduled within 2 days of the deal becoming sold.

Colorado — Westminster & Centennial • Colorado Springs

Survey Technicians by Region

RegionTechnician
Westminster & CentennialSam Miller
Colorado SpringsKenny O'Brian

Arrivy Scheduling Procedure

Open Arrivy.com in a new tab. Hover over "Tasks" in the banner, select "Calendar" from the dropdown.
Filter by technician. In the Calendar view, add a filter and search by the Site Survey technician in the "Team" dropdown.
Open Arrivy task from HubSpot. Locate the "Arrivy" section on the deal page (right side). Open the SITE SURVEY task in a new window.
Update the task name to include the project type and deal info:
ESS-SITE SURVEY - PROJ-7270 | Olson, Taylor | 13068 Westlake Place, Broomfield, CO 80020
Configure the task:
  • Verify "Arrivy" template has "Site Survey" selected
  • Set group type to match install location (Westy, Centennial/DTC, or COSP)
  • Assign appropriate Site Survey technician based on region
Do not select "General Group" — that option should not be utilized.

Customer Scheduling Call

Check technician availability. If existing appointments are present but slots are available, map out the distance between the existing appointment and the project you are trying to schedule.
Call the customer to schedule for the next available date based on schedule availability or whatever works best for the homeowner. Some homeowners may request the survey take place 1-2 weeks out.
Update the Arrivy task once date and time are confirmed:
  • Un-click the "Unscheduled Task" checkbox
  • Set Start & End date to the survey day
  • Set Start time to the technician's arrival time
  • Set duration to 1 hour (default is 4 hours — update this)
  • Click "Save" in the upper right

Follow-Up

The project will remain in the "Site Survey" status until the survey is complete. Follow up with the Site Survey technician within 2 days of the scheduled date if the Arrivy task is not marked complete.
When the survey is marked complete, HubSpot automation moves the project from "Site Survey" to "Design & Engineering" stage. The PM receives a notification, and a status email is sent to the homeowner automatically.

sections.title: Design & Engineering
sections.content:
Design & Engineering

This step typically takes about 2 weeks to complete.

Colorado — Westminster & Centennial • Colorado Springs
At a glance: The drafting partner creates the construction planset. Design & Operations review it together. PM monitors progress and escalates delays.
  • System generates a "Retrieve Project Details" task for the drafter
  • Construction planset should be drafted within 3-5 business days
  • Design/Ops meetings happen on Tuesdays and Thursdays

Drafter Workflow (Automated Tasks)

Drafter begins work. They complete the "Retrieve Project Details and Site Survey Results - WMS" task when the design is in progress. The system then generates an "Upload Completed Design - WMS" task.
Monitor timeline. The planset should be drafted within 3-5 business days of the survey being completed.
If not available within 5 business days, leave a comment in the "Design and Revision Communication Thread" tagging the design lead to inquire about the status.
Drafter uploads planset. Vishtick (drafting partner) completes the "Upload Completed Design - WMS" task once the planset is ready.

Design/Ops Review

System generates review task. "Complete Initial Design Review and Trigger DA Document - WMS" is assigned to the head design lead (Zach Rosen).
Design/Ops meeting. Design & Operations meet on Tuesdays and Thursdays to review the draft construction planset. Zach will note in the "Design and Revision Communication Thread" which date the project will be reviewed.
Review outcome. After the meeting, Zach posts notes on how the review went and if any revisions are needed. Once ready for customer delivery, the review task is completed — triggering "Send DA to Customer - WMS" for the PM.

sections.title: Send Design Approval
sections.content:
Send Design Approval (DA)

Prepare and send the DA document to the homeowner for review and signature.

Colorado — Westminster & Centennial • Colorado Springs
At a glance: PM reviews the planset vs proposal, creates the DA in PandaDoc, emails it to the customer, and calls to notify them.
  • PM receives HubSpot + email notification that the project is ready for DA
  • ESS-only projects can skip the comparison steps and send immediately
  • PB Advantage projects have a 5% production tolerance threshold

1. Review Design/Ops Meeting Notes

Check meeting notes to determine what has changed from proposal to current planset.
ESS-only shortcut: If the project is ESS (battery) only, the DA form can be created and sent right away — skip the comparison steps below and jump to PandaDoc creation.
Note customer-facing items. If items like installing drywall or clearing personal belongings are needed, these must be communicated to the homeowner.

2. Compare Proposal vs Construction Planset

Open the proposal from the "Sales" folder in Google Drive. If solar is being installed, scroll to the proposed roof layout.
Open the construction planset from the "Design" folder in a second tab.
Compare panel count and layout. Ensure the number of panels matches what was sold unless otherwise stated in the Design/Ops meeting notes.
PB Advantage rule: If the project was sold with Photon Brothers Advantage, production tolerance is up to 5% loss. If production loss exceeds 5%, up to 4 solar panels can be added at no additional cost to achieve the contracted production number.

3. Create DA in PandaDoc

Print the construction planset and save it as a PDF file.
Navigate to PandaDoc on the deal page (right side) and click "Create Document".
Select the correct template:
  • "PB Design Approval with PV" — for solar projects
  • "PB Design Approval with ESS Only" — for battery-only projects
Name the document descriptively:
PROJ-7211 | Torres, Mike | Design Approval
Attach the planset. Scroll to the bottom of the form, click the greyed-out "+ Add Content", select "Upload file" to attach the planset PDF.
Trim the document. Delete all pages from the Single Line Diagram onward to shorten the signature request.

4. Send to Customer

Download the DA form from PandaDoc.
Email the homeowner via Gmail. Attach both the DA form and the full construction planset.
Send signature request in PandaDoc by hitting "Send".
Call the homeowner to advise that the DA has been sent via email.
Mark "Send DA to Customer - WMS" task as complete. HubSpot will create "Receive DA Approval - WMS" task for PM.

sections.title: Receive DA Approval
sections.content:
Receive DA Approval

Track the customer's DA response and handle approvals or rejections.

Colorado — Westminster & Centennial • Colorado Springs

Follow-Up Cadence

Set the task due date to within 2 business days. Follow up with the homeowner every 2 business days for approval. Rule of thumb: use two contact methods (e.g., call + voicemail, then follow-up text).

If DA is Approved

Download signed DA. Retrieve from the PandaDoc completed email notification or from PandaDoc directly.
Upload to Google Drive. Place the signed DA in the "Design" folder within the project's Google Drive.
Mark task complete. Complete the "Retrieve DA Approval - WFM" task.
Completing this task triggers the next engineering steps: Complete Final Design Review For Stamping - WMS, Retrieve Plans & Create Letter - WMS, Upload Plans & Create Letter - WMS.

If DA is Rejected

Update DA status. Manually update "Design Approval Status" in the "Design Approval" section on the right side of HubSpot.
Log revision requests. Place notes of requested system changes in the "Design and Revision Communication Thread".
The system creates a "Send Plans for Revisions - WMS" task for engineering. Once revisions are complete, PM receives a "Resubmit Design Revision for DA - WMS" task to send a revised DA.

sections.title: Permitting & Interconnection
sections.content:
Permitting & Interconnection

Submit building permits and utility interconnection applications.

Colorado — Westminster & Centennial • Colorado Springs
At a glance: Once design completes plan stamping, the PM receives two tasks: permit submission and interconnection application.

Submit Permit to AHJ

Receive "Submit Permit to AHJ - WMS" task.
Review the CO Login Sheet to determine the AHJ's preferred method of submission, then proceed with submission.

Submit Interconnection Application

Receive "Submit Interconnection Application For Customer Signature - WMS" task.
Determine utility type:
Xcel Energy: If the utility is marked as "Xcel", the utility application should have already been submitted at the start of the project. The interconnection tasks can be marked as complete right away.
All other utilities: The interconnection application will need to be submitted at this stage. Refer to the Colorado Interconnections FAQ for utility-specific procedures.

sections.title: Ready to Build
sections.content:
Ready to Build

Coordinate with Operations and the customer to confirm installation dates.

Colorado — Westminster & Centennial • Colorado Springs
At a glance: Once the building permit and utility approval are both received, the project automatically advances to RTB. PM creates an RTB note for Operations, then confirms install dates with the customer.

Stage Transition Rules

When both building permit and utility approval are received, the project automatically moves from "Permitting & Interconnections" to "Ready to Build".
Xcel exception: If the utility is marked as "Xcel", the project will move to RTB once the building permit is issued — even if utility approval is still underway.
Evaluate the Xcel exception. If the project moved to RTB but utility approval is still pending, determine if installing ahead of the utility's approval would benefit the customer. If not, manually move the project back from "Ready to Build" to "Permitting & Interconnection" until utility approval is received.

RTB Note & Scheduling

Create an RTB note for Operations advising the project is ready for installation scheduling. Use the HubSpot template available via "Insert Snippet" in the Notes window.
Wait for Ops response. Operations will review the project and leave a comment on the RTB note tagging the PM with a proposed install date.
Confirm with customer. Call the customer to confirm the proposed dates, then respond to Operations with confirmation.

sections.title: Close Out
sections.content:
Close Out

Final steps to complete a project and deliver the closing packet to the customer.

Colorado — Westminster & Centennial • Colorado Springs
At a glance: Verify everything is finalized (invoices, tasks, system health), create the closeout packet in PandaDoc, and send it to the customer.

1. Pre-Close Verification

  • All invoices are paid
  • No pending loose ends or tasks remain (check communication log)
  • System is present in Powerhub and healthy
If an invoice is not paid, add a note to the task about the open invoice and reschedule the task as needed.
If there are loose ends, create a task to address them before proceeding.

2. Powerhub Health Check

  • Check proposal for estimated daily average production — verify it matches Powerhub
  • No errors or alerts in Powerhub
  • Export is on
  • Backup reserve is set to ~20%
If the system is not in remote access mode and you need to fix an issue, reach out to the customer directly. Create a follow-up task as needed.

3. Create Closeout Packet

Create PandaDoc from HubSpot Deal — this allows for prefill options.
Remove unnecessary forms:
  • If not a battery project — remove CO storage tax form
  • If not an inverter project — remove the inverter section
Add project documents. Download the contract, design plans, and any change orders from Google Drive. Upload them to the PandaDoc form.
Download the full closeout packet as a PDF.
Upload to Google Drive — place in the "PTO & Closeout" folder.

4. Send to Customer

Use email templates from the Deal page. Select the "Closing Packet with Review" template.
  • Choose the correct location so the customer gets the appropriate links
  • Attach the closeout document
  • CC the salesperson
Mark the task as complete.
Assign "Itemized Receipt" task to Jolien (optional).
Move the project from "Closeout" to "Complete" stage.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Action Queues Overview

Action queues are role-specific dashboards that show what needs attention right now. Each queue filters the project pipeline to items where someone needs to take an action, sorted by urgency.

The Queues

QueueOwnerURL
Plan Review QueueDesign / TechOps/dashboards/plan-review
Design Approval QueueDesign / TechOps/dashboards/pending-approval
Design RevisionsDesign / TechOps/dashboards/design-revisions
Permit Action QueuePermit team/dashboards/pi-permit-action-queue
IC & PTO Action QueueIC team/dashboards/pi-ic-action-queue
Permit RevisionsPermit team/dashboards/pi-permit-revisions
IC RevisionsIC team/dashboards/pi-ic-revisions

Common Patterns

Every action queue shares the same conventions:

  • Sortable columns — click a header to sort. Default sort is days-in-stage / days-in-status descending
  • Search — case-insensitive substring match across project name, customer, location, lead, and stage
  • Multi-select filters — Location, Lead, Stage. Filter state persists in a Zustand store across sessions
  • CSV export — every queue exposes Export via the DashboardShell header
  • Days-in-status color coding — yellow at 14d, red at 21d (where applicable)

Workflow Tips

  1. Filter by your location and your name (Lead column)
  2. Sort by days-in-status descending — oldest first
  3. Work the top of the list, marking action taken in HubSpot directly
  4. The queue refreshes via SSE within seconds of a HubSpot update

sections.title: Plan Review
sections.content:
Plan Review Queue

Projects that are in initial design review or final design review with a designer or engineer. Use this to prioritize whose plans you tackle next.

  • URL: /dashboards/plan-review
  • Visible from: D&E Suite

Statuses Included

Three HubSpot status values qualify a project for this queue (status names are HubSpot internal names, not display labels):

  • Initial Review — display label "Ready For Review" — initial design review
  • DA Approved — final design review
  • Submitted To Engineering — currently with engineering

Columns

ColumnNotes
Project nameLinked to HubSpot
OwnerSortable
Review TypeSortable; "Initial" / "Final" / "Engineering"
Days WaitingSortable; default sort descending
DC/AC RatioSortable
AHJSortable
LocationSortable
StageSortable
Design Draft DateSortable
DA DateSortable
AmountSortable

System Performance Review Toggle

Every row has a toggle for "system performance review" — flipping it sends a PATCH to /api/projects/[id] updating the system_performance_review property in HubSpot. Useful when a designer flags a project for additional perf review during the plan check.

Filters

  • Search bar (project name, customer, location, AHJ, owner)
  • Review Type multi-select
  • Filter state persisted via the usePlanReviewFilters Zustand store

sections.title: Design Approval
sections.content:
Design Approval Queue

Three-section action queue for the Design Approval workflow: surveys done that need design started, designs ready that need to go to the customer, and DAs sent that are awaiting customer signature.

  • URL: /dashboards/pending-approval
  • Visible from: D&E Suite
  • CSV export filename: design-approval-queue.csv

Three Sections (top to bottom)

  1. Survey Done — Needs Design — site survey complete, designer needs to start the design
  2. Design Ready — Send to Customer — design finished, ready to package and send the DA
  3. DA Sent — Awaiting Customer — DA delivered, waiting for customer signature

Each section is independently filterable and sortable. Stages excluded from the queue (e.g., "Closed Lost", "On Hold") are removed up-front via the EXCLUDED_STAGES filter.

Filters

  • Search across project name, customer, location, owner
  • Multi-select: Locations, Stages
  • Persisted via usePendingApprovalFilters

Workflow

  1. Start with section 1 (Survey Done — Needs Design) — these are the oldest items in the funnel
  2. Pull the project, do the design, advance to section 2
  3. Section 2 — package the DA, send to customer
  4. Section 3 — call/email customers who haven't signed (sort by days-in-stage descending)

sections.title: Design Revisions
sections.content:
Design Revisions

Projects currently in a revision cycle — rejected by AHJ, customer, or QC and back with a designer for fixes.

  • URL: /dashboards/design-revisions
  • Visible from: D&E Suite
  • CSV export filename: design-revisions.csv

Inclusion Rule

The queue includes any project where designStatus matches a known revision status (handled by isDesignRevisionStatus()). Projects with no revision status set are excluded.

Columns & Sort

  • Default sort field: daysInRevision, descending — longest stuck at the top
  • All standard project columns sortable
  • Rejection reason and revision count surfaced when present

Filters

  • Search across project name, owner, location, stage
  • Multi-select: Locations, Stages
  • Persisted via useDesignRevisionsFilters

Workflow

  1. Sort by daysInRevision desc — focus on the longest-stuck
  2. Open the project, read the rejection reason
  3. Make the revision in OpenSolar / planset
  4. Re-submit and update the design status in HubSpot — the project drops out of this queue

sections.title: Permit Action Queue
sections.content:
Permit Action Queue

Permit-only items needing action: ready-to-submit, ready-to-resubmit, and stale (no movement in 14+ days).

  • URL: /dashboards/pi-permit-action-queue
  • Visible from: P&I Suite
  • CSV export filename: pi-permit-action-queue.csv

Default Sort

Sort field: daysInStatus descending — projects sitting longest in their current permit status appear first.

Filters

  • Search bar — project name, address, AHJ, lead
  • Multi-select: Locations, Permit Lead, Stage
  • Persisted via usePIPermitActionQueueFilters

Action

For deeper per-project work, click into the project from this queue and take action via the Permit Hub — the queue is the "what to work on" view; the Permit Hub is the "do the work" view.

sections.title: IC & PTO Action Queue
sections.content:
IC & PTO Action Queue

Interconnection and PTO action items in one place. Sort by status urgency, filter by action type.

  • URL: /dashboards/pi-ic-action-queue
  • Visible from: P&I Suite
  • CSV export filename: pi-ic-action-queue.csv

Action Type Filter

This queue has an extra single-select action type filter beyond the standard multi-selects:

  • all — show everything
  • Specific action types (e.g., submit, resubmit, follow up) — narrow the view

Default Sort

Sort field: daysInStatus descending.

Filters

  • Search across project, customer, utility, lead
  • Multi-select: Locations, IC Lead, Stage
  • Persisted via usePIICActionQueueFilters

Action

Click into a project to take action via the IC Hub.

sections.title: Permit Revisions
sections.content:
Permit Revisions

Permit revision queue — projects that have been rejected by an AHJ and are either ready to resubmit or already resubmitted and awaiting decision.

  • URL: /dashboards/pi-permit-revisions
  • Visible from: P&I Suite
  • CSV export filename: pi-permit-revisions.csv

Sort & Filters

  • Default sort: days descending (days since rejection / days since resubmission)
  • Search across project name, customer, AHJ, lead
  • Multi-select: Locations, Permit Lead, Stage
  • Persisted via usePIPermitRevisionsFilters

Workflow

  1. Sort by days desc — longest-rejected at the top
  2. Open the project; read the AHJ rejection reasoning in the Permit Hub's Correspondence tab
  3. Make the planset revision and re-submit via the Permit Hub's "Resubmit to AHJ" form

sections.title: IC Revisions
sections.content:
IC Revisions

Interconnection revision queue — projects rejected by a utility and either ready to resubmit or already resubmitted.

  • URL: /dashboards/pi-ic-revisions
  • Visible from: P&I Suite
  • CSV export filename: pi-ic-revisions.csv

Sort & Filters

  • Default sort: days descending
  • Search across project name, customer, utility, lead
  • Multi-select: Locations, IC Lead, Stage
  • Persisted via usePIICRevisionsFilters

Workflow

Same pattern as Permit Revisions, but for utilities. Use the IC Hub's "Resubmit to Utility" or "Provide Information" forms to take action.

sections.title: How to Use the SOP Guide
sections.content:
How to Use the SOP Guide

The SOP Guide (this page) is the canonical knowledge base for the PB Tech Ops Suite. It's organized by tab, sub-organized by sidebar group, and each section is a focused page with steps, tables, and callouts.

Finding What You Need

  • Search bar at the top (or Ctrl+K) — searches across every section you have access to
  • Tab bar — top navigation, one tab per topic area
  • Sidebar — within a tab, lists every section grouped by category
  • Direct URL — every tab is reachable at /sop?tab=<tabId> and every section at /sop?tab=<tabId>#<sectionId>

Why You Can't See Some Tabs

Tabs and sections are gated by your role. The lock icon next to a tab means it's gated. You'll only see tabs your role grants access to.

Visibility tierWho can see it
PublicAll authenticated users
Tab-gatedAnyone with one or more matching roles (multi-role users get the union)
Section-gatedWithin a visible tab, specific sections may still be hidden
Admin / Owner / ExecutiveBypass all gates — see everything
Multi-role users get the union of every role's permissions. So a user with SALES + SERVICE sees both teams' tabs.

Public Tabs (everyone)

  • HubSpot Guide — basic CRM workflows
  • Project Pipeline — pipeline-stage overviews
  • Reference — general reference (you're here)
  • Zoho Inventory — inventory workflow
  • Catalog — submitting new products
  • Suites — directory of the 10 departmental suites

Role-Gated Tabs

You'll see these only if your role qualifies:

  • Service — Service triage workflows
  • Scheduling — Site Survey / Construction / Inspection / Service schedulers
  • Forecast — Timeline / Schedule / Accuracy
  • AHJ & Utility — Per-AHJ and per-utility analytics
  • Tools — BOM Pipeline, Hubs, Surveyor, Schedules, Maps, Workflows
  • Action Queues — D&E and P&I daily action queues
  • Executive — Leadership dashboards (admin / owner / executive only)
  • Accounting — Payment workflows (accounting + admin)
  • Sales & Marketing — Sales pipeline and quoting tools

Editing Sections

Three permission tiers control what you can do with a section:

Your roleWhat you can do
ADMIN / OWNER / EXECUTIVEFull edit — write directly with the in-app HTML editor (CodeMirror). Edits write through immediately and create a SopRevision row in the audit log.
Any other authenticated role (not VIEWER)Submit a suggestion — same editor, but saves as a SopSuggestion in PENDING state. An admin reviews and approves or rejects.
VIEWERRead-only.

The Edit / Suggest Flow

  1. Open a section you want to edit or suggest a change to
  2. Click the Edit button in the top-right (or Suggest if you don't have edit rights)
  3. The editor opens with the current HTML content + a live preview pane
  4. Make your changes — write semantic HTML (<h2>, <table>, <ul>, etc.)
  5. Add a brief edit summary describing what changed and why
  6. Click Save (admins) or Submit suggestion (non-admins)

HTML Conventions Used Here

The SOP renderer styles a small set of semantic classes consistently. Use these in your edits:

PatternRenders as
<div class="info">...</div>Blue informational callout
<div class="warn">...</div>Amber warning callout
<div class="tip">...</div>Green tip callout
<div class="sys">...</div>System-action callout
<code>something</code>Inline code formatting
<table>...</table>Striped data table

Optimistic Locking

Every section has a version number. When you save, your edit must match the current version — if someone else saved while you were editing, you'll get a "version conflict" error and need to merge their changes into yours before retrying.

Revision History

Every save creates a SopRevision row capturing the previous content, who edited it, when, and the edit summary. Admins can browse the history of any section.

Suggestion Review (Admins)

If non-admins submit suggestions, a badge appears next to the Edit button showing the pending count. Admins click through to review, approve, or reject each suggestion with optional review notes. Approved suggestions become live edits with attribution to the original suggester.

Maintaining the Guide

The SOP guide stays useful only if it stays current. A few habits:

  • When you change a tool, update its SOP section in the same PR. Don't ship workflow changes without updating the docs.
  • When you onboard someone, point them at the public tabs first, then their team-gated tabs. Watch them use it. Anything confusing? Edit the SOP.
  • When you fix a recurring confusion in chat or a meeting, ask yourself if the fix belongs in an SOP section. Add a "Common Pitfalls" entry if so.
  • When a feature is deprecated, mark its section with a <div class="warn"> deprecation notice rather than deleting — the institutional knowledge of why something was removed is valuable.

Adding a New Tab or Section

Adding new content via seed scripts (rather than the in-app editor) is the right move when:

  • You're adding many sections at once
  • You want the content version-controlled in git
  • You're building a new tab from scratch

The pattern lives in scripts/seed-sop-*.ts. Each script:

  1. Imports PrismaClient + PrismaNeon
  2. Defines a tab ID, label, sortOrder
  3. Lists sections with HTML content as TS template strings
  4. Idempotent main() — skips existing rows; --force overwrites with version increment

If the tab needs to be gated, also update src/lib/sop-access.ts:

  • Add to PUBLIC_TABS if everyone should see it
  • Add to TAB_ROLE_GATES with the allowed roles if team-specific
  • Add to SECTION_ROLE_GATES for sensitive sections within otherwise-visible tabs (empty array = admin-only)

After running the seed, the new content is live immediately — Vercel rebuilds aren't required since the SOP system is database-driven.
