sections.title: DA Revision
sections.content:
DA Revision Workflow

Design Approval rejection → revision → re-send cycle. Triggered when a customer rejects the DA document.

Trigger

TRIGGER Design Approval Status → "Revision Needed - DA Rejected"

Revision Flow

Revision Needed - DA Rejected→ DA Revision In Progress→ DA Revision Completed→ Corrected DA Re-Sent to Customer

Step-by-Step

AUTO Customer rejects DA → Design Approval Status → "Revision Needed - DA Rejected"
AUTO Design team picks up → Design Approval Status → "DA Revision In Progress"
AUTO Revisions complete → Design Approval Status → "DA Revision Completed"
AUTO Corrected DA re-sent via PandaDoc
AUTO DA Revision Counter incremented
CALC DA Revision Complete Date recorded
After re-send: The normal DA approval flow resumes — PandaDoc tracks the new document and triggers approval/rejection events. If rejected again, the cycle repeats with the counter incrementing.

sections.title: Permitting Revision
sections.content:
Permitting Revision Workflow

AHJ permit rejection → design revision → resubmission cycle. Supports up to 2 rejection rounds.

Trigger

TRIGGER Permitting Status → "Rejected - Needs Revision"

1st Rejection Cycle

Rejected - Needs Revision→ Revision Needed - Rejected by AHJ→ Permit Revision In Progress→ Permit Revision Completed→ Revision Ready To Resubmit→ Resubmitted to AHJ
AUTO AHJ rejects → Permitting Status → "Revision Needed - Rejected by AHJ"
AUTO Design picks up → "Permit Revision In Progress"
AUTO Design done → "Permit Revision Completed" → "Revision Ready To Resubmit"
AUTO Creates task for permitting coordinator to resubmit
MANUAL Permitting coordinator completes task → "Resubmitted to AHJ"

2nd Rejection Cycle

Rejected 2nd Time - Needs Revisions→ 2nd Revision Needed - Rejected by AHJ→ 2nd Permit Revision In Progress→ 2nd Permit Revision Complete→ 2nd Revision Ready To Submit→ Resubmitted to AHJ
Same pattern as the 1st cycle — a task is created for the permitting coordinator at the "2nd Revision Ready To Submit" step. If rejected a 3rd time, manual escalation is required.

sections.title: Interconnection Revision
sections.content:
Interconnection Revision Workflow

Utility rejection → design revision → resubmission cycle. Supports up to 2 rejection rounds.

Trigger

TRIGGER Interconnection Status → "Rejected - Revisions Needed"

1st Rejection Cycle

Rejected - Revisions Needed→ Revision Needed - Rejected by Utility→ Utility Revision In Progress→ Utility Revision Completed→ Revision Ready To Resubmit→ Resubmitted to Utility
AUTO Utility rejects → Interconnection Status → "Revision Needed - Rejected by Utility"
AUTO Design picks up → "Utility Revision In Progress"
AUTO Design done → "Utility Revision Completed" → "Revision Ready To Resubmit"
AUTO Creates task for interconnection coordinator to resubmit
MANUAL IC coordinator completes task → "Resubmitted to Utility"

2nd Rejection Cycle

Rejected 2nd Time - Revisions Needed→ 2nd Revision Needed - Rejected by Utility→ 2nd Utility Revision In Progress→ 2nd Utility Revision Complete→ 2nd Revision Ready To Submit→ Resubmitted to Utility
Same pattern as the 1st cycle — a task is created for the IC coordinator at the "2nd Revision Ready To Submit" step. If rejected a 3rd time, manual escalation is required.

sections.title: As-Built Revision
sections.content:
As-Built Revision Workflow

Post-construction as-built revision cycle. Triggered by inspection failure or as-built requirement.

Triggers (either one)

TRIGGER Permitting Status → "As-Built Revision In Progress"
TRIGGER Final Inspection Status → "Rejected - As-Built Revision Needed"

Revision Flow

As-Built Revision Needed→ As-Built Revision In Progress→ As-Built Revision Completed→ As-Built Ready To Resubmit→ As-Built Revision Resubmitted
AUTO Inspection fails with as-built issue → triggers as-built revision
AUTO Design team revises as-built plans → "As-Built Revision In Progress"
AUTO Revision done → "As-Built Revision Completed" → "As-Built Ready To Resubmit"
AUTO Creates task for permitting to resubmit as-built
MANUAL Permitting completes task → "As-Built Revision Resubmitted"
As-built revisions do NOT block PTO. The initial inspection pass is what advances the deal to PTO. As-built revisions run in parallel and are tracked separately via the permitting status sub-flow.

sections.title: Job Categories & Statuses
sections.content:
Zuper Job Categories

Every Zuper job belongs to one of these categories, which determines its status workflow.

  • Site Survey — Initial property assessment — roof measurements, electrical panel, shading analysis.
  • Construction — Solar panel installation (the main install day).
  • Inspection — Municipal building inspection after installation.
  • Service Visit — Warranty or maintenance call on an existing system.
  • Service Revisit — Follow-up visit for a previous service call.
  • Additional Visit — Extra visit beyond the standard scope.
  • Detach — Removing panels from roof (D&R projects).
  • Reset — Reinstalling panels after roof work (D&R projects).
  • D&R Inspection — Inspection specific to detach & reset work.
  • Walk Roof — Roofing-specific roof walkthrough assessment.
  • Mid Roof Install — Roofing installation in progress (mid-project check).
  • Roof Final — Final roofing inspection / completion.

Common Zuper Job Statuses

Not Started

StatusDescription
NewJob just created, not yet assigned or scheduled.
UnassignedJob exists but no crew assigned.
Ready to ScheduleJob is ready but no date set.
ScheduledDate set, crew assigned, waiting for job day.
Ready to BuildAll prerequisites met, waiting for install day.
Ready for InspectionInstall done, inspection not yet scheduled.

In Progress

StatusDescription
On Our WayCrew dispatched, heading to site.
StartedWork has begun on site.
In ProgressActive work underway.

Completed

StatusDescription
CompletedJob finished successfully.
Construction CompleteInstall finished (construction jobs).
PassedInspection passed.
Partial PassInspection partially passed — does NOT proceed to PTO until fully passed.
FailedInspection failed — corrections needed.

Compliance note: Jobs in "On Our Way", "Started", or "In Progress" for more than 24 hours are flagged as stuck — usually means the crew forgot to update the status.

sections.title: User Roles
sections.content:
User Roles & Permissions

Each user has one role that determines which dashboards and features they can access.

RoleDescription
ADMINFull access to everything, including user management and admin tools.
OWNERSame as Admin but cannot manage users (for Matt & David).
PROJECT_MANAGERProject tracking, scheduling, reporting across all job types.
OPERATIONSConstruction flow management — can schedule installs & inspections.
OPERATIONS_MANAGERCrew oversight, scheduling, availability management.
TECH_OPSField technicians — view schedules, manage own availability.

PERMITTINGPermitting dashboard access (normalized to TECH_OPS).
SALESSales team — only survey scheduler access.
VIEWERRead-only access to all dashboards.

Permission Flags

Individual users can have boolean overrides that grant additional abilities on top of their role defaults. These only add access — they never remove abilities the role already grants.

  • canScheduleSurveys — Can create and manage site survey appointments.
  • canScheduleInstalls — Can schedule installation jobs.
  • canSyncToZuper — Can trigger Zuper data syncs.
  • canManageUsers — Can create/edit/delete user accounts.
  • canManageAvailability — Can edit crew availability calendars.

allowedLocations — Array of office locations the user can access. Empty means all locations (default).

Key Thresholds

ThresholdValueUsed For
Stale P&I threshold14 daysPermits/IC items with no status change are flagged.

Milestone Forecasting

Forecast dates (install, inspection, PTO, etc.) are computed by the QC-data-driven forecasting engine, not fixed offsets. The engine calculates median milestone-to-milestone durations from historical project data, segmented by location, AHJ, and utility. Fallback hierarchy: full segment (min 5 samples) → location only → global baseline.

sections.title: Design Workflow
sections.content:
Design — Design Workflow

From site survey data to stamped plans

Design Lifecycle in HubSpot

Design work happens during the Design & Engineering pipeline stage. The deal record tracks every date in the process:

Design Start Date — When design work begins on a project. Triggered when the deal enters D&E stage.
Date Returned From Designers — When the first draft comes back from the design team.
Design Draft Completion Date — When the initial design is finished and ready for internal review.
Design Approval Sent Date — When the design is sent to the customer for approval via PandaDoc.
Design Completion Date — When the design is finalized (customer approved, ready for permitting).

Key HubSpot Fields — Design & Engineering Group

FieldPurpose
Design LeadWho is assigned to this design
Design Start DateWhen design began
Date Returned From DesignersFirst draft received
Design Draft Completion DateDraft finalized
Engineering Submission DateSent for engineering stamp
Engineering Stamped DatePE stamp received

Design Rejection DateIf customer rejected the DA

Revision Tracking

The deal record tracks up to 4 types of revisions with separate counters:

Revision TypeCounter FieldTriggered By

Permit RevisionPermit Revision CounterAHJ requests plan corrections
Interconnection RevisionInterconnection Revision CounterUtility requests changes
As-Built RevisionAs-Built Revision CounterField changes require updated plans

The Design Revisions dashboard aggregates these across all active projects so you can see your revision workload at a glance.

sections.title: Plan Review
sections.content:
Design — Plan Review

Internal design QC before customer approval

Plan Review Dashboard

The Plan Review dashboard shows all designs awaiting internal review. Before sending a design to the customer for approval, it should be reviewed for:

  • ✅ Correct module count and layout
  • ✅ Inverter/battery sizing matches the proposal
  • ✅ AHJ-specific requirements met (see AHJ Requirements dashboard)
  • ✅ Utility design requirements met (system size rules, production meter, etc.)
  • ✅ Structural calculations complete
  • ✅ Electrical single-line diagram accurate

Design Approval (DA) Process

Internal review complete — design passes plan review checklist.
Send DA to customer via PandaDoc. The Design Approval Sent Date is recorded in HubSpot.
Customer reviews and signs — or rejects with notes. DA status tracked in PandaDoc card on the deal record.
If rejected: Review the "Design Approval Notes from Customer" and "DA Rejection Reason" fields. Create a revision. Increment the DA Revision Counter.
If approved: Design is locked. Submit for engineering stamp if required (PE Letter per AHJ).

Pending Approval Dashboard

The Pending Approval dashboard shows all designs sent to customers but not yet signed. Use this to follow up on stale approvals.

Utility-Specific Rules

sections.content:

Monitoring utility interconnection performance

The Utility Tracker dashboard scores utility companies on interconnection processing performance. It tracks interconnection turnaround times and identifies slow utilities. The tracker shows both overall averages and last-90-day trends so you can spot improving or worsening utilities.

Before submitting an interconnection applicationCheck the utility's average turnaround — set realistic customer expectations

sections.title: Scheduling
sections.content:
Operations — Scheduling

How to schedule installations and inspections

Scheduling an Installation

Open the Construction Scheduler from the Operations Suite. You'll see a calendar view with crew availability and a list of RTB (Ready To Build) projects in the left panel.
Find the project you want to schedule. Projects are sorted by priority score — higher scores mean more urgent (overdue, high value, or PE projects).
Check the project's requirements:
  • Equipment on hand? (check Equipment Backlog)
  • Permits issued?
  • BOM and Sales Order created?
  • Customer confirmed?
Select a date and crew. The scheduler shows crew capacity — don't overbook. Consider drive time between job sites for the same crew.
Sync to Zuper. After scheduling, use the Zuper Sync button to push the job to the field service system. This creates (or updates) the Zuper job with the assigned crew and date.
HubSpot updates automatically — the Construction Schedule Start/End Date fields are set when you schedule through the tool.

Scheduling an Inspection

Open the Inspection Scheduler. Projects appear here after construction is marked complete.
Verify the project is ready:
  • Construction Complete Checklist filled out in HubSpot
  • No loose ends flagged
  • "Ready for Inspection" = Yes
Check AHJ requirements. The AHJ & Utility tab on the deal record shows whether an electrician is required for inspection, what permits are needed, and typical turnaround times.
Schedule the inspection date and assign an inspector if applicable.
Sync to Zuper to create the inspection job for the field team.

Priority Score — How Projects Are Ranked

FactorPointsWhy
Days overdue+1–3 per day (max ~100)Older projects need attention first
PE project+30–50 bonusParticipate Energy milestones have financial penalties
High value ($50k+)Up to +30Larger deals = more revenue at risk
Stage urgencyVariesRTB and Inspection stages score higher

Higher score = schedule this project first.

sections.title: BOM & Inventory
sections.content:
Operations — BOM & Inventory

Bills of materials and sales order creation

What Is the BOM Tool?

The BOM (Bill of Materials) Tool reads the permitted design planset PDF and extracts every piece of equipment needed for the installation. It then matches those items to SKUs in Zoho Inventory and can create a Sales Order automatically.

When to Generate a BOM

  • When a project reaches Ready To Build — permits are issued, design is final
  • After a design revision — if equipment changed, regenerate the BOM
  • Before scheduling an install — confirm all materials are available

BOM → Sales Order Workflow

Open the BOM Tool from the Operations Suite.
Search for the deal by PROJ number or customer name.
Select the planset PDF — the tool auto-detects available plansets from the deal's Google Drive attachments.
Run extraction. The AI reads every page of the planset and extracts modules, inverters, batteries, racking, electrical components, etc.
Review the BOM. Check quantities and items against what you know about the project. Submit feedback if anything looks wrong.
Create the Sales Order. Select the Zoho customer and warehouse, then click Create SO. The post-processor applies rules for your warehouse (e.g., Westminster/Centennial include racking, SLO/CAM do not).

Equipment Backlog

The Equipment Backlog dashboard shows all projects with outstanding equipment needs. Use it to:

  • Track which projects are waiting on deliveries
  • See estimated arrival dates
  • Flag projects that can't be scheduled until equipment arrives

Inventory Dashboard

Monitor warehouse stock levels across all locations. The dashboard pulls from Zoho Inventory and shows current quantities, reorder points, and recent movements.

sections.title: Zuper Sync & Crews
sections.content:
Operations — Zuper Sync & Crews

Field service job management

What Is Zuper?

Zuper is the field service management platform. It's how installation and inspection jobs get dispatched to field crews. Jobs in Zuper have their own lifecycle — separate from HubSpot deal stages — but the two systems stay linked.

Zuper Job Categories

CategoryPurposeCreated When
Site SurveyInitial site assessmentSurvey scheduled
ConstructionInstallation workInstall scheduled
InspectionFinal/utility inspectionPost-construction
Additional VisitPunch list, service callsAs needed

How Zuper Sync Works

Scheduling → Zuper

When you schedule a job in the Ops Suite, clicking Sync to Zuper creates or updates the Zuper job. The assigned crew, date, and address are pushed from HubSpot deal data.

Important: Zuper only allows setting the assigned_to field at job creation time. If you need to reassign a crew, you may need to create a new job.

Zuper on the Deal Record

Each HubSpot deal record shows linked Zuper jobs in the right sidebar (under Zuper Jobs). You'll see:

  • Job number and category (Construction, Inspection, etc.)
  • Current job status
  • Priority level

The Zuper & Operations tab in the center panel shows the Zuper Gallery — install photos uploaded by crews, grouped by date with uploader names and timestamps. This is your visual verification that work was done.

Key Zuper Statuses

StatusMeaning
NewJob created, not yet assigned
Ready To ScheduleNeeds a date assigned
ScheduledDate and crew set
In ProgressCrew is on site
Construction CompleteInstall finished
PassedInspection passed
FailedInspection failed — needs rework

sections.title: Construction Day
sections.content:
Operations — Construction Day

What happens on install day and what to track

Pre-Construction Checklist

Before the crew arrives on site, verify:

  • ✅ Equipment delivered to warehouse or job site
  • ✅ Sales Order confirmed in Zoho
  • ✅ Zuper job synced with correct crew and date
  • ✅ Customer notified of install date
  • ✅ Permits posted on site (if required by AHJ)
  • ✅ Construction Forecasting fields filled in HubSpot (expected days, installer count, difficulty)

During Construction

Field crews update status in Zuper as they work. You can monitor progress via:

  • Zuper Gallery on the deal record — crews upload photos during the install
  • Zuper job status — changes from Scheduled → In Progress → Construction Complete

Post-Construction

After the crew completes the install, the Construction Complete Checklist on the deal record should be filled out:

FieldWhat to Enter
Number of Panels InstalledActual panel count
Loose Ends Remaining?Yes/No — any punch list items?
Loose End NotesWhat needs to be finished
Are the permitted plans accurate?Did the install match the design?
What changed?Note any field modifications
Ready for Inspection?Yes = can schedule inspection

Moving the Deal Forward

Once construction is complete and the checklist is filled:

  1. Move the deal stage from Construction → Inspection
  2. Schedule the inspection (see Scheduling)
  3. If loose ends remain, create an Additional Visit job in Zuper

sections.content:

TypeRequired ByNotes

sections.title: Service & Warranty
sections.content:
Operations — Service & Warranty

Handling service calls and warranty work

Service Pipeline

Service and warranty jobs use the Service Pipeline in HubSpot (separate from the main Project Pipeline). The Service Scheduler manages these jobs.

Creating a Service Job

Open the Service Scheduler from the Operations Suite.
Find the original deal — service jobs are linked back to the original project deal in HubSpot.
Schedule the service visit and assign a crew.
Sync to Zuper as an Additional Visit category.

Service Backlog

The Service Backlog dashboard shows all open service requests. Use it to prioritize which service calls to schedule first.

Detach & Reset (D&R)

D&R jobs (roof work requiring panel removal and reinstallation) use the D&R Pipeline and D&R Scheduler. These are tracked separately because they have different crew requirements and timelines.

sections.title: Zuper Overview
sections.content:
Zuper — Field Service Management

Zuper is the field service platform that manages job scheduling, crew dispatch, and on-site work tracking. Every site survey, installation, and inspection flows through Zuper.

How Zuper Connects to HubSpot

Zuper jobs are created from HubSpot deal records. Each project can have multiple Zuper jobs (survey, construction, inspection). The connection works like this:

HubSpot → Zuper Job Creation Flow
HubSpot Deal
PROJ-XXXX
→
Zuper Jobs
Survey + Construction + Inspection
→
Field Crew
Scheduled & dispatched
ConceptHow It Works
Job CreationJobs are created from HubSpot via the Right Sidebar → Zuper Jobs card, or automatically by workflows. Each job links back to the HubSpot deal via a custom field.
Crew AssignmentAssigned at job creation time. Zuper API cannot change assignment after creation
Status SyncZuper job statuses sync back to HubSpot. The status comparison dashboard catches mismatches between the two systems.
Photos & NotesField crews upload photos and notes to Zuper jobs. These are visible in HubSpot via the Center Panel → Zuper & Operations tab.

What Operations Uses Zuper For

TaskWhere in Zuper / PB Ops Suite
Schedule an installConstruction Scheduler dashboard → drag or assign to crew + date
Schedule a surveySite Survey Scheduler → assign surveyor + send customer portal link
Schedule an inspectionInspection Scheduler → assign date after construction complete
Track crew complianceZuper Compliance dashboard → on-time rates, stuck jobs, grading
Check status syncZuper Status Comparison dashboard → mismatches between HubSpot & Zuper
View revenue calendarRevenue Calendar → construction/detach/reset jobs by week with $ amounts
Manage crew availabilityCrew Availability admin → set weekly hours, block PTO dates
Zuper access: Field crew members use the Zuper mobile app. Office staff manage jobs through the PB Operations Suite dashboards, which call the Zuper API behind the scenes.

sections.title: Job Categories & Statuses
sections.content:
Zuper — Job Categories & Statuses

Every Zuper job belongs to a category. Each category has its own status workflow — the statuses a job moves through from creation to completion.

Core Job Categories

These are the three main categories tied to the project pipeline. Every project should have one of each:

CategoryWhen It's CreatedRevenue?Pipeline Stage
Site SurveyAt deal creation or when survey is requestedNoSite Survey
ConstructionWhen project reaches Ready To BuildYesConstruction
InspectionAfter construction is completeNoInspection

Additional Job Categories

CategoryPurposeRevenue?
Service VisitPost-install service call — see Service PipelineNo
Service RevisitFollow-up to a service visitNo
Additional VisitExtra visit outside normal project flowNo
DetachRemove panels for roof work — see D&R PipelineYes
ResetReinstall panels after roof work — see D&R PipelineYes
D&R InspectionInspection after detach & resetNo
Walk RoofRoof assessment before D&R workNo
Mid Roof InstallRoof work in progress milestoneNo
Roof FinalFinal roof work completionNo

Job Status Workflow

Jobs progress through these statuses. The exact statuses vary by category, but the general flow is:

New
Scheduled
On Our Way
Started
Completed
● Gray = not started ● Blue = scheduled/queued ● Orange = in progress ● Green = done
Status GroupStatusesWhat It Means
Never StartedNew, Unassigned, Ready to Schedule, Ready to Build, Ready for InspectionJob exists but no field work has begun
ScheduledScheduledCrew and date assigned, waiting for job day
In ProgressOn Our Way, Started, In ProgressCrew is actively working or en route
CompletedCompleted, Construction Complete, Passed, Partial PassField work is done
FailedFailedInspection failed or job could not be completed
Stuck jobs: If a job has been "In Progress" for more than 24 hours past its due date, it's flagged as stuck in the compliance dashboard. This usually means the crew forgot to mark it complete.

Inspection-Specific Statuses

StatusMeaningNext Step
PassedInspection cleared — move to PTOUpdate HubSpot → PTO stage
Partial PassMinor items to fix, but mostly goodSchedule correction visit, then re-inspect
FailedMajor issues foundFix issues → reschedule inspection

sections.title: Permitting Workflow
sections.content:
Permitting Workflow

From plan submission to permit issued

Permitting Lifecycle in HubSpot

Permitting work happens during the Permitting & Interconnection pipeline stage. Key fields:

FieldPurpose
Permit LeadWho is managing this permit
Permit Submit DateWhen the application was submitted
Permit Issued DateWhen the permit was approved
Permit Rejection DateIf the AHJ rejected the submission
Permit Revision Complete DateWhen corrections were resubmitted
Permit Revision CounterHow many revision cycles
Permit Turnaround TimeDays from submit to issued

Permit Action Queue

The Permit Action Queue dashboard shows permits that need your attention:

  • Ready to Submit — Design is complete, permit application needs to be prepared and submitted
  • Corrections Requested — AHJ sent back revisions, need to fix and resubmit
  • Awaiting Review — Submitted and waiting for AHJ response (track with AHJ Tracker)

AHJ-Specific Requirements

Every project is associated with an AHJ (Authority Having Jurisdiction) in HubSpot. The deal record's AHJ & Utility tab shows:

  • Stamping Requirements — PE Letter required? Engineer stamp?
  • NEC Code / IRC Code — Which code year applies
  • Design Snow Load / Wind Speed — Structural requirements
  • Submission Method — Portal, email, in-person, etc.
  • Permits Required — PV, ESS, Electrical (varies by AHJ)
  • Average Permit Turnaround — Historical processing time

sections.title: AHJ Tracking
sections.content:
AHJ Tracking

Monitoring jurisdiction performance

AHJ Tracker

The AHJ Tracker dashboard scores authorities having jurisdiction on permit processing performance. Each AHJ gets a score based on:

  • Average permit turnaround time
  • Overdue rate (% of permits that exceed expected timelines)
  • Deal volume in the last 365 days

How to Use It

ScenarioAction
Before submitting a permitCheck the AHJ's score — low scores mean expect delays, plan accordingly
Identifying problem jurisdictionsFocus on AHJs with scores < 50 for escalation or process changes
Staffing decisionsHigh-volume slow AHJs may need dedicated resources
Setting customer expectationsUse average turnaround to give realistic timelines

For utility-side performance tracking, see the Utility Tracker section under the Interconnection role.

sections.title: Sales Pipeline
sections.content:
Sales Pipeline

Active deals, funnel visualization, and proposal tracking — the sales rep's daily workspace.

sections.title: Pricing Calculator (Deep Dive)
sections.content:
Pricing Calculator (Deep Dive)

Price a solar + battery system end-to-end with full COGS visibility, lease factor, and adders. This is the sales rep's authoritative tool for generating an accurate quote — not a guess.

  • URL: /dashboards/pricing-calculator
  • Accent: orange

Equipment Selection (top of page)

Pick from active catalog options:

SectionWhat you select
ModulesBrand + model + count. Wattage drives system size in kW DC.
InvertersString / hybrid / micro — match to module count and system architecture
BatteriesBattery + expansion if applicable
OtherRacking, BOS, monitoring, EV charger — anything not in the first three buckets

Cost Sections (bottom of page)

The calculator builds the full COGS bill on the page:

  1. COGS — hardware unit costs from the catalog (auto-populated from your selections)
  2. Labour — crew labour hours × rate
  3. Acquisition Costs — sales commission + marketing attribution
  4. Fulfillment Costs — permitting, IC, PTO, inspection, third-party services
  5. Extra Costs (Roof/Site) — adders for difficult roofs, trenching, MPU, sub-panels

PE Lease Value

For PE-tagged deals, the calculator surfaces the lease factor and EPC value automatically — same numbers used in the Accounting Suite's PE Deals dashboard. The customer/PE split renders below.

Replace Calculator Data

Switching customers? Click Replace Calculator Data. A confirmation dialog warns you before clearing. Always confirm — there's no undo.

Common Mistakes

Don't free-type module/inverter/battery names. Pick from the catalog dropdown so the COGS pulls correctly. If the equipment isn't in the dropdown, submit it via the catalog wizard FIRST (see Catalog tab) and come back when it's added.
Don't skip the Extra Costs section on roofs that need work. Steep, brittle, or shaded roofs all need adders or the project hemorrhages margin.

sections.title: Customer Estimator
sections.content:
Customer Estimator

The customer-facing estimator — your tool for delivering an accurate quote in real time during a sales call. Same tool customers see when they self-serve from the marketing site.

  • Hub: /estimator
  • Direct flows below

Six Estimator Flows

FlowPathWhen to use
New Install (solar)/estimator/new-installBrand new solar PV system
Battery/estimator/batteryBattery for an existing solar customer
EV Charger/estimator/ev-chargerStandalone EV charger install
System Expansion/estimator/system-expansionAdding modules to existing system
Detach & Reset/estimator/detach-resetCustomer needs panels removed for re-roof
Out of Area/estimator/out-of-areaOutside our service area — declines politely

Address-First Flow

The default flow for new install: customer (or you, if on a call) enters an address. Behind the scenes:

  1. Address is geocoded
  2. EagleView is queried for roof measurements
  3. OpenSolar pricing is applied
  4. Adder rules run against the property
  5. Instant estimate is returned

Lead Capture

The results page collects contact info and creates a HubSpot lead automatically. Make sure the customer email/phone is correct before they hit Submit — the lead routes to whoever's on rotation.

sections.title: Request a Product
sections.content:

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Scheduler Overview

The Tech Ops Suite has four scheduling dashboards, each tied to a specific stage of the project lifecycle and a different Google Calendar set.

SchedulerURLStageCalendar
Site Survey/dashboards/site-survey-schedulerSite SurveySurvey calendars (Denver / Nick / shared)
Construction/dashboards/construction-schedulerReady To Build, ConstructionInstall calendars per location
Inspection/dashboards/inspection-schedulerInspectionInstall calendars per location
Service/dashboards/service-schedulerService Visit, Service RevisitInstall calendars per location

Locations

All schedulers cover five locations: Denver Tech Center (DTC), Westminster, Colorado Springs, San Luis Obispo, Camarillo. DTC and Centennial are aliased — same calendar, same crew. Camarillo and SLO share the same install calendar (the "California" bucket) intentionally.

Common Behavior

  • Calendar events are written via a Google service account with domain-wide delegation
  • Event IDs are deterministic SHA1 hashes prefixed with pb, so re-scheduling the same project updates the existing event instead of creating a duplicate
  • Tentative assignments persist via ScheduleRecord rows with status="tentative" until confirmed
  • React Query refetch interval is 5 minutes
  • Crew/inspector/surveyor assignments persist in browser localStorage, keyed by project ID
If GOOGLE_CALENDAR_SYNC_ENABLED is off, scheduling actions still write the ScheduleRecord row but skip the calendar API call. Useful for staging environments.

sections.title: Priority Queue
sections.content:
Service Priority Queue

The priority queue ranks open service deals and tickets so you know what to work on first. Every item gets a score from 0 to 100 and lands in one of four tiers based on warranty risk, contact recency, time stuck in stage, and dollar value.

Tiers

TierScoreWhat it means
Critical75–100Drop everything else — warranty about to expire, customer hasn't been contacted in over a week, or stuck in a stage well past expected duration
High50–74Work today — multiple risk factors compounding
Medium25–49This week — at least one factor flagged but not urgent
Low0–24On track — no flags, regular cadence

What Drives the Score

Five factors combine into the final score (capped at 100):

1. Warranty Expiry — up to 40 points

ConditionPointsReason shown
Warranty already expired+30"Warranty expired"
Expires within 7 days+40"Warranty expires in N days"
Expires within 30 days+15"Warranty expires in N days"

2. Last Contact Recency — up to 35 points

ConditionPointsReason shown
No contact in 7+ days+35"No contact in N days"
Last contact 3–6 days ago+25"Last contact N days ago"
Last contact 1–2 days ago+5(no visible reason)

3. Stage Duration (Stuck) — up to 20 points

ConditionPointsReason shown
In current stage 7+ days+20"Stuck in 'Stage' for N days"
In current stage 3–6 days+10"In 'Stage' for N days"

4. Deal Value — up to 10 points

ConditionPoints
Amount > $10,000+10 ("High-value service")
Amount > $5,000+5

5. Stage-Specific Urgency — up to 15 points

  • Inspection or Invoicing stage: +5 (these stages should not be lingering)
  • Site Visit Scheduling or Work In Progress stage AND modified 5+ days ago: +10 (active stages going stale)
Reasons shown on each card explain why something landed in its tier — useful when deciding which Critical to grab first.

Manual Overrides

Sometimes the algorithm misses context — a known VIP customer, a verbal commitment to call by EOD, or a deal you want pinned. Use a manual override to lock a tier.

  • Override priorities translate to fixed scores: critical → 90, high → 65, medium → 35, low → 10
  • Overridden items show a "Manually set to tier" reason at the top of their reason list
  • Overrides persist in the ServicePriorityOverride table — they survive page refreshes and queue rebuilds

Refresh Behavior

The queue listens for upstream changes via Server-Sent Events:

  • Cache key: service:priority-queue
  • Cascades from upstream: deals:service* and service-tickets* invalidations
  • 500 ms debounce prevents thundering herd when multiple upstreams fire

You shouldn't need to manually refresh — when a deal or ticket changes upstream, the queue rebuilds within a second.

Common Workflow

  1. Start your day on the priority queue — work top-to-bottom through Critical, then High
  2. Open the deal or ticket in HubSpot via the link on the card
  3. Take the action the reason calls out (call the customer, advance the stage, finish inspection, etc.)
  4. If a card looks wrong (wrong tier given context only you know), apply a manual override with a brief note
  5. If a stage is genuinely stuck for a structural reason (waiting on AHJ, parts ordered), use the manual override to push it down so it stops dominating the top of the queue

sections.title: Ticket Board
sections.content:

Priority Badges

BadgeColor
HighRed
MediumYellow
LowGreen
NoneGray

The priority comes directly from the HubSpot ticket's priority field — same dropdown values you'd see on the ticket in HubSpot.

Filters

Multi-select dropdowns for:

  • Location
  • Stage (limits which columns show)
  • Priority
  • Owner (includes "Unassigned")

Plus a search bar that filters by ticket subject. Filters are client-side — no roundtrip on every change.

Real-time Updates

The board listens for SSE events with cache key filter service-tickets — when an upstream change fires (someone updates the ticket in HubSpot, or another user takes action here), the board refetches automatically.

Click a Card → Detail Drawer

Opens /api/service/tickets/[id] and shows:

  • Subject + content (the original ticket body)
  • Priority, stage, pipeline
  • Create date, last modified, last contact date
  • Owner
  • Associations — linked contacts, deals (with amount + service type), companies
  • Timeline — notes, emails, calls, meetings, tasks (chronological from HubSpot)

Actions From the Detail Drawer

ActionWhat it doesEndpoint
Change StageMove the ticket to a different stage in the service pipelinePATCH /api/service/tickets/[id] with {stageId}
Add NoteAppend a HubSpot note to the ticketPATCH .../tickets/[id] with {note}
AssignSet the HubSpot ownerPATCH .../tickets/[id] with {ownerId}

Each action writes through to HubSpot directly. The board refetches and reopens the detail after the action completes.

There is no "close ticket" button — closing happens by moving the ticket to the Closed stage. This matches the HubSpot service pipeline convention.

sections.title: Customer History
sections.content:
Customer History (Customer 360)

Search across all of HubSpot for a customer and see everything we have: deals, tickets, and Zuper field service jobs in one view. Use this when a customer calls and you need to figure out what's been done for them.

  • URL: /dashboards/service-customers
  • Visible to: Service team and Operations roles

Searching

One search box accepts any of:

  • Name (first, last, or full)
  • Email
  • Phone
  • Address (street or city)

The search hits HubSpot contacts and companies. Results are deduplicated and capped at 25. If you have more than 25 matches, the response includes a truncated: true flag and you should narrow your search.

Result Card

Each match shows:

  • Customer name + primary email/phone
  • Address (linked to the Property drawer when the property views feature flag is on)
  • Counts: # deals, # open tickets, # Zuper jobs

Click into a match to load the full detail panel.

Detail Panel

Three sections, each showing only items associated with this customer:

Deals

  • Project name, stage, amount
  • Service type (when present)
  • Days in stage
  • Last contact date
  • Line items (name, qty, category, unit price)
  • Direct link to the HubSpot deal

Tickets

  • Subject, stage, priority
  • Service type
  • Days in stage
  • Direct link to HubSpot ticket

Jobs (Zuper)

  • Job name, category, current job status
  • Assigned users
  • Scheduled and completed dates
  • Direct link to the Zuper job

How Zuper Jobs Get Resolved

Zuper doesn't share contact IDs with HubSpot, so the resolver tries two strategies:

  1. Deal-linked cache — if any of the customer's HubSpot deals has a Zuper job linked via the ZuperJobCache table, those jobs surface immediately.
  2. Name + address heuristic — for jobs not linked to a deal, the resolver matches Zuper customer name and address against the HubSpot contact's name and address. Looser matches (typos, missing apartment number, etc.) may fail.
If a known Zuper job isn't showing up, it's almost always because the deal-linked cache hasn't been populated yet. An admin can trigger a re-link via the deal detail panel.

Property Drawer Integration

When the NEXT_PUBLIC_UI_PROPERTY_VIEWS_ENABLED flag is on, addresses in the customer detail are clickable — they open the Property drawer with equipment summary, owners, deals, tickets, and a unified property record.

Common Use Cases

  • Customer calls about a problem — search by phone or name, find their open ticket, see the timeline of what we've done
  • Repeat-customer lookup — see if a customer had previous service work, what equipment was installed, who the assigned crew was
  • Address dispute — verify what address we have on file vs what the customer is claiming
  • Pre-call prep — before calling a customer, pull up their full history so you walk in knowing the full picture

The Ten Suites

SuiteWhat lives hereURL
OperationsScheduling, timeline, inventory, equipment ops/suites/operations
Design & EngineeringDesign reviews, clipping, AHJ rules, engineering tools/suites/design-engineering
Permitting & InterconnectionPermit hub, IC hub, action queues, SLA monitoring/suites/permitting-interconnection
ServicePriority queue, ticket board, service scheduling, customer 360/suites/service
D&R + RoofingDetach & reset and roofing pipelines + schedulers/suites/dnr-roofing
IntelligenceAt-risk projects, QC metrics, alerts, optimizer, project management/suites/intelligence
ExecutiveRevenue, command center, capacity, forecasts/suites/executive
AccountingPayment tracking, AR, PE deals, invoicing/suites/accounting
Sales & MarketingSales pipeline, pricing calculator, estimator, adders/suites/sales-marketing
AdminUsers, security, compliance, system config/admin

Who Sees Which Suite

The suite switcher in the top-right shows you only the suites you can access. Visibility is driven by your assigned roles.

SuiteRoles in switcher
OperationsADMIN, OWNER, PM, OPS_MGR, OPS, TECH_OPS
Design & EngineeringADMIN, OWNER, PM, TECH_OPS, DESIGN
Permitting & InterconnectionADMIN, OWNER, PM, TECH_OPS, PERMIT, INTERCONNECT
ServiceADMIN, OWNER, PM, OPS_MGR, OPS, SERVICE
D&R + RoofingADMIN, OWNER, PM, OPS_MGR, ROOFING
IntelligenceADMIN, OWNER, PM, OPS_MGR, INTELLIGENCE
ExecutiveADMIN, OWNER
AccountingADMIN, OWNER, ACCOUNTING
Sales & MarketingADMIN, OWNER, SALES_MANAGER, SALES, MARKETING
AdminADMIN only
Multi-role users see the union of suites across all their roles — the highest-privilege role wins for each individual suite. So a user with OPS + SALES sees both Operations and Sales & Marketing in their switcher.

The Suite Switcher

Click your initials in the top-right of any page → the switcher shows every suite you can access plus the dashboard you're currently on. Pick a suite to land on its hub page.

Direct URL Access vs the Switcher

For PM and OPS_MGR roles, direct URL access to the Executive suite works (/suites/executive loads), but the Executive suite is hidden from their switcher. This is intentional — leadership dashboards are technically reachable for cross-functional analysis but not promoted in day-to-day nav.

Permission Booleans (override role defaults)

Beyond role-based access, individual users can have specific permissions toggled on or off:

  • canScheduleSurveys, canScheduleInstalls, canScheduleInspections
  • canSyncZuper, canManageUsers, canManageAvailability
  • canEditDesign, canEditPermitting
  • canViewAllLocations

If you can see a suite but a specific button is disabled, ask your admin to flip the relevant permission boolean on your user record.
