sections.title: BOM Pipeline
sections.content:
BOM Pipeline (Planset → Sales Order)

The BOM tool reads a stamped planset PDF, extracts every piece of equipment, matches each item to the catalog, pushes line items to a HubSpot deal, and creates a draft Zoho Sales Order — all from one page.

  • URL: /dashboards/bom
  • Visible to: ADMIN, OWNER, PM, OPS_MGR, OPS, TECH_OPS
When to use this: a project hits Ready To Build and needs a Sales Order. The BOM tool replaces the manual "read the planset, type items into Zoho" workflow.

The Four-Stage Pipeline

#StageWhat happens
1ExtractionClaude reads the planset PDF and returns a structured list of equipment (modules, inverters, batteries, racking, BOS, etc.) with brand, model, qty, and category.
2Snapshot & Catalog MatchSnapshot saved with auto-incremented version. Each item searched in Zoho Inventory; matches link to internal product records, mismatches queue a 90-day PendingCatalogPush.
3HubSpot Line Items PushAcquires a per-deal lock, replaces existing BOM-managed line items with new ones from the matched products, logs to BomHubSpotPushLog.
4Sales Order CreationPost-processes items (batches, bundles, suggests additions), resolves the Zoho customer from the HubSpot company, creates a draft Sales Order in Zoho Inventory.

Two Ways to Start

A. Drive File (preferred)

  1. Link the BOM tool to a HubSpot project (search by deal name or PROJ number)
  2. The tool reads the project's design folder from Google Drive
  3. Pick the planset PDF from the file list (most recently stamped one is highlighted)
  4. Click Extract

B. Direct Upload

  1. Drag a planset PDF into the upload zone (chunked upload via /api/bom/chunk — stays on our domain to avoid CORS)
  2. Click Extract
  3. Optionally link to a HubSpot project after extraction

What You See After Extraction

The page shows:

  • Project header — customer name, address, AHJ, utility, system size, planset rev, stamp date
  • Items table — each line with category, brand, model, qty, source, and any AI-flagged warnings
  • Catalog Coverage panel — which line items are matched in HubSpot Products / Zuper / Zoho Inventory
  • Validation badges — module count match, battery capacity match, OCPD match (calculated against the planset summary)
  • Linked HubSpot Project — links to the deal, design folder, OpenSolar, and Zuper (if available)

Edit Before Pushing

Items are editable inline. Common edits:

  • Fix brand/model typos
  • Adjust quantities
  • Add missed items (breakers are commonly missing — check the one-line diagram)
  • Remove duplicates the AI may have generated
Items missing from the catalog: any item without a Zoho Inventory match must be added to the catalog before SO creation will work. Click "Add to Catalog" on the row, or open the Submit New Product wizard separately and come back.

Push to HubSpot

Click Push Line Items to HubSpot:

  • Acquires a PENDING lock keyed on the deal ID. Stale after 5 minutes.
  • Creates a HubSpot line item per matched product with quantity
  • Deletes any prior BOM-managed line items on success
  • Logs the result (success/partial/failed) to BomHubSpotPushLog
If another user is already pushing the same deal, you'll see a "lock contention" message. Wait — the lock auto-releases after 5 minutes if the other process crashed.

Create the Sales Order

Click Create Zoho Sales Order:

  1. Items run through post-processing rules (racking is computed per-module, electrical BOS is bundled, etc.)
  2. Customer resolved from HubSpot company → Zoho contact (matched by name + address)
  3. Draft SO created in Zoho — no PO is created automatically. Review and convert to PO from Zoho yourself.

Purchase Orders

Below the SO, the page offers PO grouping. The tool suggests POs grouped by vendor and lets you create them in Zoho directly:

  1. Click Preview Purchase Orders — see suggested groupings
  2. Adjust if needed (move items between vendors, remove stocked items)
  3. Click Create PO per vendor

Version History

Every save creates a new version of the BOM snapshot. The history drawer shows:

  • Version number
  • Saved-by user + timestamp
  • Item count + total system size
  • Compare two versions to see what changed

PDF Export

Generate a clean BOM PDF from the current snapshot — useful for sharing with the install crew or attaching to a deal.

Common Failure Modes

SymptomWhat it meansWhat to do
"Lock contention" on pushAnother push is in progress for this dealWait 5 minutes; lock auto-clears
"Customer not found in Zoho"HubSpot company doesn't have a matching Zoho contactCreate the customer in Zoho first, then retry
"Item not in catalog"Brand/model not in Zoho InventorySubmit via catalog wizard; queued automatically as PendingCatalogPush for 90 days
Module count mismatch warningExtracted module qty doesn't match planset summaryVerify against the planset string sizing diagram before pushing
Extraction failed / timed outPlanset PDF is unusual format or AI couldn't read itTry again, or extract sections manually

Pipeline Run Tracking

Every full run logs a BomPipelineRun row with status (SUCCEEDED, PARTIAL, FAILED) and per-step details. Admins can view recent runs in the admin panel.

Feedback

If extraction got something wrong, click the feedback button on the row. Submissions land in BomToolFeedback and feed AI tuning.

sections.title: AI Design Review
sections.content:
AI Design Review

One-click AI review of a planset against the project's AHJ rules, utility requirements, and engineering best practices. Available on the project review page.

  • URL: /dashboards/reviews/[dealId] — opened from the project detail or directly via deal ID
  • Visible to: ADMIN, EXECUTIVE, MANAGER, DESIGNER, OPERATIONS_MANAGER, PROJECT_MANAGER, TECH_OPS

How to Run a Review

  1. Open the review page for a deal: /dashboards/reviews/<dealId>
  2. Click the Design Review button
  3. The progress text walks through the steps (typically 15–45 seconds total):
    • "Starting review…"
    • "Fetching AHJ & utility requirements…"
    • "Locating planset in Drive…"
    • "Downloading planset PDF…"
    • "Sending planset to Claude for analysis…"
    • "Claude is reviewing the planset…"
    • "Still analyzing — large plansets take longer…"
    • "Almost done…"
  4. Result appears as Passed (green check) or N errors (red X)
Polling timeout: 3 minutes. If the review hasn't returned by then, the page shows "Review timed out — the background process may have crashed." Try again, or check with TechOps.

Findings Format

Each finding has a severity:

  • ● Error — must fix before approving
  • ▲ Warning — review and decide
  • ℹ Info — informational only

Findings include the rule that fired and a short explanation. Click "View full review history →" to see the run-by-run history for this deal.

Feedback Loop

After every review, you'll see "Was this review helpful?" with thumbs up/down:

  • 👍 Positive — submit immediately, optional notes
  • 👎 Negative — choose this if findings were wrong, missed real issues, or were spammy. Add notes describing what was wrong.

Submissions feed AI tuning. The first negative on a finding type is the most valuable — explicit examples of "the AI flagged X but X is actually fine here" help us calibrate.

Review History

Below the run buttons, the page lists every review ever run for this deal:

  • Status dot (green = passed, red = failed)
  • Trigger source (manual, webhook, etc.)
  • Triggered-by user, timestamp, duration in ms
  • Findings list (collapsed if no findings)

The 50 most recent reviews are shown.

EagleView Integration

The review page also embeds the EagleView TrueDesign panel — order or check status of an aerial measurement. EagleView orders fire automatically via HubSpot workflow when EAGLEVIEW_AUTO_PULL_ENABLED is true; the manual control here is for retries or one-off pulls.

Common Failure Modes

SymptomWhat it means
"Review timed out"Background job didn't return within 3 minutes — likely a crash. Try again.
No "Design Review" button visibleYour role isn't in the allow-list. Ask an admin.
"No reviews yet"Just hasn't been run for this deal — click the button to start.

sections.title: Pricing Calculator
sections.content:
Pricing Calculator

Price a solar + battery system with per-line equipment selection and a full COGS breakdown. Includes the Participate Energy lease value calculator for PE-tagged deals.

  • URL: /dashboards/pricing-calculator
  • Visible from: Sales & Marketing Suite, Accounting Suite, Admin

Equipment Selection (top of page)

Four panels — pick the items going into the system:

  • Modules — pick from active catalog modules (wattage drives system size)
  • Inverters — string / hybrid / micro
  • Batteries — battery + expansion if applicable
  • Other — racking, BOS, monitoring, EV charger, anything not in the first three buckets

Each selection contributes to the line items on the quote and to the COGS calculation.

Cost Breakdown (bottom)

SectionWhat goes in
COGSHardware unit costs from the catalog (driven by your equipment selections)
LabourCrew labour hours × rate
Acquisition CostsSales commission + marketing attribution
Fulfillment CostsPermitting, interconnection, PTO, inspection, third-party services
Extra Costs (Roof/Site)Adders for difficult roofs, trenching, MPU, sub-panels, etc.

Replace Calculator Data

If you change deal context (different customer, different system) and want to start fresh, click Replace Calculator Data. A confirmation dialog warns you before clearing the current state.

PE Lease Value

For Participate Energy deals, the calculator surfaces the lease factor and EPC value automatically — same numbers used in the Accounting Suite's PE Deals dashboard.

All catalog data is read live from the internal product database. If a piece of equipment is missing from the dropdown, submit it via the catalog wizard first (see the Catalog tab).

sections.title: Permit Hub
sections.content:
Permit Hub

The all-in-one daily workspace for the permitting team. Split-pane layout: queue on the left, full project detail with action forms on the right.

  • URL: /dashboards/permit-hub
  • Visible to: ADMIN, OWNER, PM, TECH_OPS, PERMIT (per the P&I suite gating)

Layout

Two-pane view (full viewport height minus the page chrome):

  • Queue (420px left rail) — every project that needs permit attention, ordered by SLA / staleness
  • Project Detail (right pane) — populated when you select a queue item; empty state says "Select a project from the queue to begin."

Real-time Updates

The queue listens for SSE with cache filter deals:permit and refetches automatically. Stale time is 30 seconds for the manual refetch.

Project Detail — Six Tabs

  1. Overview — high-level deal info, current permit status
  2. AHJ — AHJ-specific rules and historical patterns for this jurisdiction
  3. Planset — current planset rev with link to the design folder
  4. Correspondence — log of communications with the AHJ
  5. Status History — every status transition, who changed it, when
  6. Activity — full audit trail of actions taken on this project

External-Link Toolbar

The detail header has quick-launch buttons to the systems you'll need:

  • HubSpot — the deal record
  • AHJ Portal — the AHJ's online submission portal (when known)
  • Application — the AHJ application form/PDF
  • Permit Folder — Google Drive folder with submission docs
  • Design Folder — Drive folder with the planset

Buttons are disabled (gray) when the URL isn't on the deal yet — hover for "[label] not available" tooltip.

Action Forms (right side, status-driven)

Based on the project's current permit status, the right pane offers exactly one action form. Available forms:

ActionTitle shown
SUBMIT_TO_AHJSubmit to AHJ
RESUBMIT_TO_AHJResubmit to AHJ
REVIEW_REJECTIONReview rejection
FOLLOW_UPFollow up with AHJ
COMPLETE_REVISIONComplete revision
START_AS_BUILT_REVISIONStart as-built revision
COMPLETE_AS_BUILTComplete as-built revision
SUBMIT_SOLARAPPSubmit SolarApp+
MARK_PERMIT_ISSUEDMark permit issued

If no form is available for the current status, the panel shows: "No action form for this status."

Each form posts a status transition that updates HubSpot and writes to the status history. The queue refreshes via SSE so the project moves to its new bucket automatically.

sections.title: IC Hub
sections.content:
Interconnection Hub

The all-in-one daily workspace for the interconnection team. Same split-pane pattern as Permit Hub, but tuned to utility/IC workflows instead of AHJ.

  • URL: /dashboards/ic-hub
  • Visible to: ADMIN, OWNER, PM, TECH_OPS, INTERCONNECT (per P&I suite gating)

Layout

Same two-pane view as Permit Hub. Queue refetches via SSE with cache filter deals:ic; 30-second stale time.

Project Detail — Six Tabs

  1. Overview
  2. Utility — utility-specific requirements, IC application notes
  3. Planset
  4. Correspondence
  5. Status History
  6. Activity

Action Forms

ActionTitle shown
SUBMIT_TO_UTILITYSubmit to Utility
RESUBMIT_TO_UTILITYResubmit to Utility
REVIEW_REJECTIONReview utility rejection
FOLLOW_UPFollow up with utility
COMPLETE_REVISIONComplete IC revision
PROVIDE_INFORMATIONProvide information to utility
MARK_IC_APPROVEDMark IC approved

Status drives the form choice — same pattern as Permit Hub.

For per-utility analytics (turnaround, bottleneck detection), see the AHJ & Utility tab's Utility Tracker section.

sections.title: Solar Surveyor
sections.content:
Solar Surveyor

Interactive solar site survey + design tool. Used to design system layouts, run shade analysis, and generate revisions tied to a HubSpot project.

  • URL: /dashboards/solar-surveyor
  • Visible from: Operations, Design & Engineering, Service suites (each carries a ?suite= query param so the back-link returns to the right place)
  • Login required — unauthenticated users redirect to /login

Three Entry Modes

ModeWhat it isWhen it's used
Native ("browser")The full in-app surveyorDefault for users who haven't expressed a preference and the env allows native
WizardGuided step-by-step surveyWhen the user's preference is set to "wizard"
ClassicLegacy surveyor UIWhen FORCE_CLASSIC env is set, or when the user prefers classic

How the Mode is Picked

The page reads resolveNativeMode() from the env and the user's stored preference (solarPreferredEntryMode on the User record), then resolves to the right initial view:

  1. FORCE_CLASSIC env → classic (toggle hidden, wizard blocked)
  2. NATIVE_DEFAULT env + pref "wizard" → wizard
  3. NATIVE_DEFAULT env + pref "classic" → classic
  4. NATIVE_DEFAULT env + pref "browser" or unset → native
  5. Env unset + pref "wizard" → wizard
  6. Env unset + pref "classic" or unset → classic
  7. Env unset + pref "browser" → native

Setting Your Preference

The mode toggle (top of the surveyor) saves your selection to your User record. Once set, every future visit lands you in the same mode.

If FORCE_CLASSIC is on (admin-controlled env var), the mode toggle is hidden and only Classic loads — even if your preference is something else. This is the kill switch we use during native-mode incidents.

Working on a Project

Once inside, the surveyor:

  • Loads aerial imagery via EagleView (when configured)
  • Lets you place modules, draw setbacks, mark obstructions
  • Saves revisions to SolarProjectRevision rows in the database
  • Can be shared via SolarProjectShare for read-only review

Closing the surveyor with unsaved state stores it in SolarPendingState so you can resume on next visit.

sections.title: Master Schedule
sections.content:
Master Schedule

The big drag-and-drop scheduling calendar — the main scheduling surface for ops. Combines crew assignments, projects, and forecast ghosts into one view.

  • URL: /dashboards/scheduler
  • Visible to: ADMIN, OWNER, PM, OPS_MGR, OPS, TECH_OPS (Operations Suite)

What's Different From the Per-Function Schedulers

The four schedulers in the Scheduling tab (Site Survey, Construction, Inspection, Service) are each tuned to one job type. The Master Schedule shows everything at once — useful when you're rebalancing across crews, looking for capacity, or checking what's happening on a single day across all work types.

Layout

  • Sidebar (collapsible) with the project list
  • Calendar grid with day, week, and month views (and a "day view" pop-out)
  • Per-event tooltip showing job name, crew, dollar amount, status (failed inspection ✗ / completed ✓ / overdue ⚠ / forecasted only)

Drag-and-Drop

  • Drag a project from the sidebar onto a day to schedule
  • Drag an existing event to a different day to reschedule
  • Hovering shows the full deal info before commit

Forecast Ghosts

Pre-construction projects without real scheduled dates appear as ghost events on their forecast date. Tooltip says "Forecasted install — not yet scheduled." Once you drag a ghost to a real date, it becomes a confirmed event.

Export

  • Export CSV — daily/weekly schedule as a spreadsheet
  • Export iCal — calendar file for importing into other tools
  • Copy schedule to clipboard — formatted text for pasting into a Google Doc, email, or Slack thread

Quick Links Per Project

  • Open internal deal page
  • Open in Zuper
  • Open in HubSpot

Hover any event to access these.

sections.title: Pipeline Optimizer
sections.content:
Pipeline Optimizer

AI-powered scheduling optimization and bottleneck detection. The Optimizer suggests scheduling moves that improve throughput across the pipeline.

  • URL: /dashboards/optimizer
  • Visible from: Intelligence Suite, Operations Suite

What It Does

The Optimizer runs heuristics over the pipeline to:

  • Identify bottlenecks — stages where projects are accumulating beyond capacity
  • Suggest scheduling moves — projects that could ship sooner if scheduled differently
  • Flag capacity opportunities — open crew-days that could absorb work

How to Use It

Open the page; the analysis runs automatically. Each suggestion includes:

  • The project (with link to HubSpot)
  • The recommended action (reschedule earlier, reassign crew, escalate)
  • The expected impact

Suggestions are ranked by impact. Acting on a suggestion just opens the relevant dashboard (scheduler, deal page, etc.) — the Optimizer doesn't apply changes automatically.

This is a recommendation tool, not an automation tool. Review suggestions, decide which to apply, then make the change in the appropriate scheduler or deal page.

sections.title: Jobs Map
sections.content:
Jobs Map

Geographic view of every job — scheduled, unscheduled, and in the backlog. Useful for spotting clustering opportunities, optimizing crew routes, and prepping morning briefings.

  • URL: /dashboards/map
  • Filtered to service: /dashboards/map?types=service (linked from Service Suite)
  • Visible from: Operations, Service, Executive suites

Three Views (mode picker)

ModeWhat it shows
WeekWork scheduled in the next 7 days. Empty state: "No work in the next 7 days matches your filters."
BacklogPre-construction work not yet on the schedule. Empty state: "No pre-construction work in the backlog matches your filters."
AllEverything — scheduled + unscheduled + completed in window

Filters

  • Office Picker — shows jobs for one or more PB locations. User-specific preference saved (so you don't have to re-pick every visit).
  • Filter Bar — additional filters by job type, status, and assignee. Applied client-side, no refetch needed.

Map Canvas

  • Each job appears as a marker, color-coded by stage / status / type (see the legend top-right)
  • Click a marker → detail panel slides in with the project address, customer, deal info, and links
  • Crew positions overlay when crew tracking is on

Marker Table

Below the map, every visible marker is listed in a sortable table. Click a row to focus the map on that job.

Morning Briefing

The map page also exposes a Morning Briefing view — a printable daily summary of every scheduled job for the day, grouped by crew, with addresses, customer names, and key project notes. Useful for crew-day prep.

Export

Export markers as CSV via the exportMarkers helper — useful for emailing route plans or pasting into a separate routing tool.

sections.title: Equipment Backlog
sections.content:
Equipment Backlog

Forecast equipment demand by brand, model, and stage. Use this to make sure modules, inverters, and batteries arrive when crews need them.

  • URL: /dashboards/equipment-backlog
  • Visible from: Operations Suite
  • Service-only equipment forecast: /dashboards/service-backlog (separate dashboard in the Service Suite)

What's on the Page

Three product breakdown sections:

  • Modules — by brand + model
  • Inverters — by brand + model
  • Batteries — by brand + model

Each row shows the total quantity needed across all in-flight projects, and you can expand the row to see which projects contribute (with stage, location, and quantity per deal).

Per-Project Equipment Card

The top of the page lists every active project with its full equipment specification. Projects with missing equipment data show a warning indicator (⚠ "Missing equipment data").

Filters

  • Location filter — show only projects from selected PB offices
  • Stage filter — focus on a specific pipeline phase (e.g., RTB only)
  • Search — filter by project name, brand, or model

How to Use It

  1. Filter to your PB location
  2. Look at the Modules / Inverters / Batteries totals — these are what you need on hand for projects currently in flight
  3. Cross-reference with current Zoho stock to identify shortfalls
  4. Place bulk POs (or follow up with vendors) for any item where stock + on-order < backlog
For the Service-pipeline equipment forecast, use /dashboards/service-backlog — same UI but scoped to service deals only, with stage classification (backlog / in-progress / built).

sections.title: Workflow Builder
sections.content:
Admin Workflow Builder

Visual workflow builder for chaining existing actions into automated sequences. Built on Inngest (the same engine the BOM pipeline runs on). Admin-only.

  • List: /dashboards/admin/workflows
  • Editor: /dashboards/admin/workflows/[id]
  • Run history: /dashboards/admin/workflows/runs
  • Per-run detail: /dashboards/admin/workflows/runs/[runId]
  • Visible to: ADMIN only

Anatomy of a Workflow

A workflow is a saved AdminWorkflow row containing:

  • A trigger — what fires the workflow
  • A definition (JSON) — the ordered list of steps
  • Activation flag — only ACTIVE workflows actually run

Triggers

TriggerDescription
MANUALAdmin clicks "Run now" from the editor — useful for one-off batch jobs and testing
HUBSPOT_PROPERTY_CHANGEFires when a HubSpot deal/contact/ticket property changes (piggy-backs on the existing deal-sync webhook)
ZUPER_PROPERTY_CHANGEFires when a Zuper job property changes (handled at /api/webhooks/zuper/admin-workflows)
CRONFires on a cron schedule
CUSTOM_EVENTFires when any other workflow emits a custom event (chaining workflows together)

Action Palette

The editor offers a fixed set of actions, organized by category:

Messaging

  • send-email — Send an email via the dual-provider system (Google Workspace primary, Resend fallback)

AI

  • ai-compose — Generate text via Claude using a prompt with template substitution

HubSpot — Reads

  • fetch-hubspot-deal — Pull a deal record by ID
  • find-hubspot-contact — Look up a contact by email/phone/name

HubSpot — Writes

  • update-hubspot-property — Set a deal property
  • update-hubspot-contact-property — Set a contact property
  • update-hubspot-ticket-property — Set a ticket property
  • add-hubspot-note — Append a note to a deal
  • add-hubspot-contact-note — Append a note to a contact
  • create-hubspot-task — Create a HubSpot task

Zuper

  • fetch-zuper-job — Pull a Zuper job
  • update-zuper-property — Set a Zuper job custom field

HTTP

  • http-request — Generic outbound HTTP call (POST/GET/PATCH/etc.) — escape hatch for systems without dedicated actions

PB Ops

  • run-bom-pipeline — Trigger the BOM pipeline for a deal
  • log-activity — Write an ActivityLog row for audit purposes

Control Flow (special — handled outside step.run)

  • delay — Wait N seconds/minutes/hours/days before continuing
  • stop-if — Halt the workflow if a condition matches

Template Expressions

Step inputs support two interpolation prefixes:

  • {{trigger.X}} — values from the trigger context (e.g., the deal property that just changed)
  • {{previous.stepId.field}} — outputs from a prior step (chain a fetch → an update)

Templates

The editor offers "Start from template" — code-defined starter workflows in src/lib/admin-workflows/templates.ts. Click one to clone it into a new editable workflow.

Run History

Every execution writes an AdminWorkflowRun row capturing:

  • Status: succeeded / failed / partial
  • Per-step inputs and outputs
  • Duration in ms
  • Error message and stack on failure

The cross-workflow run history view (/runs) and per-run detail (/runs/[runId]) make it easy to debug.

Feature Flags

FlagWhat it gates
ADMIN_WORKFLOWS_ENABLEDEditor + API + manual runs
ADMIN_WORKFLOWS_FANOUT_ENABLEDWebhook → workflow event fan-out (separate kill switch from manual runs)
Be careful with HubSpot writes. A workflow that sets a property on every deal that matches a trigger can quickly snowball if the trigger is too broad. Test with the MANUAL trigger first, then narrow the property-change trigger before activating.

sections.title: Deal Detail Panel
sections.content:
Deal Detail Panel

The unified deal-detail page used across every pipeline (Sales, Project, D&R, Service, Roofing). One layout, pipeline-specific sections, role-aware visibility.

  • URL: /dashboards/deals/[pipeline]/[dealId]
  • Real-time: SSE listener with cache filter deals:<dealId> — page auto-refreshes when the deal mirror updates

Layout

The page has three main areas:

Header (top)

  • Deal name + customer
  • Stage chip with color from the pipeline's stage palette
  • Refresh button — calls POST /api/deals/[id]/sync to pull the latest from HubSpot, then refetches the page data
  • Milestone Timeline — visual progression through the pipeline stages
  • Status Flags Bar — at-risk, overdue, etc.

Main Content (center)

Three switchable tabs (selection persisted to localStorage as deal-detail:active-tab):

TabWhat's there
DetailsPipeline-specific Field Grid sections (e.g., Site Survey, Design, Permitting…) — collapsible
ActivityHubSpot activity feed — every property change, status update, etc.
CommunicationsNotes + emails + calls timeline

Sidebar (right)

Always-visible cards:

  • Team — assigned PM, designer, permitter, IC owner, etc.
  • Equipment — module/inverter/battery counts
  • Contact — primary contact details
  • External Links — HubSpot, OpenSolar, Zoho SO, design folder, planset
  • Quick Actions — common one-click ops (run BOM, generate reports, etc.)
  • Zuper Jobs — every Zuper job linked to this deal
  • Change Log — recent property changes
  • Related Deals — same customer or address
  • Photo Gallery — site photos

Role-Based Section Visibility

Some sections (e.g., install-planning) are hidden from non-operational roles. The "operational roles" set is:

  • ADMIN, OWNER, PROJECT_MANAGER, OPERATIONS_MANAGER, OPERATIONS, TECH_OPS

If you're SALES or DESIGN, those operational sections won't appear — same data, just role-appropriate filtering.

Pipeline-Specific Sections

Section composition is defined in section-registry.ts per pipeline. The Project pipeline shows Site Survey + Design + Permitting + IC + Construction + Inspection sections; the Service pipeline shows Service Visit + Equipment + Resolution sections, and so on.

Sync Button Behavior

Clicking Refresh:

  1. Sends POST /api/deals/[id]/sync
  2. Server pulls fresh data from HubSpot
  3. Client waits 500 ms for the persist to settle
  4. Page does an RSC refresh — new server-rendered data flows in without a full reload
In normal use you don't need to hit Refresh — the SSE listener picks up deals:<dealId> events and refreshes automatically. Use it when you suspect HubSpot has new data we haven't seen yet (e.g., a workflow you triggered manually).

sections.title: Property Drawer
sections.content:
Property Drawer

Slide-in drawer that shows everything we have on a physical address — equipment, owners, deals, tickets, jobs — anchored to a canonical HubSpot Property record.

  • Feature flag: NEXT_PUBLIC_UI_PROPERTY_VIEWS_ENABLED — when off, the drawer never renders even if mounted
  • Surfaces with the drawer wired up: Service Suite customer-360, Deals detail panel address row

What is a "Property"?

One row per normalized address in HubSpotPropertyCache. Dedup is enforced via addressHash (SHA-256 of street + unit + city + state + zip) and an optional googlePlaceId. So two deals at the same house always link to the same Property record.

Data You'll See in the Drawer

  • Map — Google Maps Static API satellite view, centered on the lat/lng
  • Address — canonical formatted address
  • Equipment summary — system size, battery presence, module count (rolled up from associated deals)
  • Owners — Current Owner, Previous Owner, Authorized Contact (each is a HubSpot contact link)
  • Deals — every deal at this address, all pipelines
  • Tickets — every ticket at this address
  • Open ticket count — quick scan for "is there active service work here?"
  • Warranty dates — when applicable

How to Open It

Anywhere a <PropertyLink> wraps an address (must pass structured AddressParts, not a raw string):

  • Click the address → drawer slides in from the right
  • Press Escape or click the backdrop to close

"No Property Record" State

If the address you clicked doesn't have a Property row yet (the back-fill or webhooks haven't created one), the drawer shows a "No property record yet" message with a placeholder Manual Create button (currently disabled — coming with task 6.2).

Caching

  • React Query stale time: 60 seconds
  • No refetch on window focus / mount / reconnect — keep the drawer fast and quiet
  • Map static URL is memoized per (propertyId, lat, lng) so the browser only issues one Google Maps request per property-open

How Properties Get Created

Three paths:

  1. Contact webhook — when a HubSpot contact's address changes, onContactAddressChange() geocodes the new address and upserts the Property cache
  2. Backfill script — scripts/backfill-properties.ts walks contacts → deals → tickets → rollups and creates Property rows for every existing address
  3. Reconcile cron — /api/cron/property-reconcile runs daily at 9am, re-fetching any property touched in the last 24h to fix cache drift
ATTOM-sourced fields (yearBuilt, squareFootage, roofMaterial, etc.) are null until ATTOM integration ships. Current implementation populates only HubSpot-derivable + Google-geocoded fields.

Feature Flag Reference

FlagWhat it gates
PROPERTY_SYNC_ENABLEDWebhook + cron + backfill kill switch (server-side; cache tables sit empty when off)
NEXT_PUBLIC_UI_PROPERTY_VIEWS_ENABLEDUI surfaces (drawer, links, providers) — independent of the sync flag

sections.title: AHJ Tracker
sections.content:
AHJ Tracker

Per-AHJ (Authority Having Jurisdiction) view of permit volume, rejection rates, and turnaround. Use this to identify slow AHJs, recurring rejection patterns, and where to push permitting effort.

  • URL: /dashboards/ahj-tracker
  • Visible to: all roles

Hero Stats

  • AHJs with Deals (count)
  • Active Permits (total)
  • Overall Rejection Rate (%)
  • Avg Permit Turnaround (days)

Main Table

ColumnNotes
AHJName + city/state
DealsSortable; default sort descending
Active PermitsOpen permits in this AHJ
In RevisionPermits currently being revised
Rejection Rate %Red > 30%, yellow > 15%
Avg Turnaround (days)Red > 45d, yellow > 30d
RevenueSortable; total project value in this AHJ

Rejection rate formula: permit_rejections / (permit_issued + permit_rejections) × 100

Drill-Down

Click any AHJ row to expand and see its individual projects:

  • Project name (linked to HubSpot)
  • Stage
  • Permit Status
  • Permit Lead
  • Days in Stage (red > 21d, yellow > 14d)
  • Amount

Drill-down rows are independently sortable.

Filters & Search

  • Multi-select filters: Location, Permit Lead, Stage
  • Search bar matches project name, AHJ name, stage, location, or permit lead (case-insensitive)
  • Filter state persists in a Zustand store across sessions

Export

CSV export includes rejection/approval counts and revenue for audit trail.

Data Source

Merges HubSpot project data (stage, lead, deal amount) with the custom AHJ HubSpot object (location, rejection rate, turnaround time). Projects are matched to the AHJ object by case-insensitive name comparison on the projects.ahj field.

Turnaround is stored in milliseconds in the database and converted to days for display (rounded).

sections.title: Utility Tracker
sections.content:
Utility Tracker

Per-utility view of interconnection (IC) application throughput and PTO timing. Identify bottleneck utilities and prioritize follow-up.

  • URL: /dashboards/utility-tracker
  • Visible to: all roles

Hero Stats

  • Utilities with Deals (count)
  • Active IC Apps
  • PTO Pipeline (count)
  • Avg IC Turnaround (days)

Main Table

ColumnNotes
UtilityName + state · service area
DealsSortable; default sort descending
Active ICSortable
PTO PipelineSortable
IC Turnaround (days)Red > 60d, yellow > 45d
RejectionsRed if > 5
RevenueSortable

Bottleneck Detection

Utilities with above-average IC turnaround display a red "Slow" badge and a tinted row. The threshold is dynamic — it's the dashboard average, not a fixed number. So a utility flagged as slow today might not be flagged tomorrow if the overall average shifts.

Drill-Down

Click any utility row to expand and see its projects:

  • Project name (linked to HubSpot)
  • Stage
  • IC Status
  • PTO Status
  • IC Lead
  • Days in Stage (red > 21d, yellow > 14d)
  • Amount

Filters & Search

  • Multi-select filters: Location, IC Lead, Stage
  • Search bar matches utility name, project name, stage, location, or IC lead (case-insensitive)
  • Filter state persists in a Zustand store

Data Source

Merges HubSpot project data with the custom Utility HubSpot object. Projects match utility by case-insensitive name comparison against either record_name or utility_company_name.

Like AHJ turnaround, IC turnaround is stored in ms and displayed in days.

sections.title: Workflow Overview
sections.content:
Zoho Inventory Workflow

End-to-end inventory lifecycle for solar projects — from Sales Order creation through final invoicing. Six stages across Ops and Accounting, with three steps automated by the system.

Workflow Overview

# Step Owner Trigger Status Inventory Effect
1 Create Sales Order (SO) Ops Ready To Build Confirmed Inventory "Committed" — reserved but still in stock
2 Create Purchase Order (PO) Ops SO created Issued No stock change — order sent to vendor
3 Receive Purchase Order (PR) Ops Shipment arrives Received Inventory into physical stock
4 Pick / Pack / Ship (PKG/SHP) Ops Install date Shipped Inventory out of physical stock
5 Invoice Sales Order (INV) Acctg Construction complete Confirmed Inventory out of accounting stock — closes SO
6 Receive Vendor Bill Acctg Bill received — Inventory into accounting stock — closes PO

Stage Details

1. Sales Order (SO)

Automated at Ready To Build stage

One SO per project (PROJ)

Purpose

  • Complete listing of all materials the install team needs for the project (stock & special order items)
  • The SO is the comprehensive list — even items pulled from warehouse stock should appear here

Timing

  • Project has moved into construction scheduled
  • Can also be created manually by extracting the BOM from HubSpot into Zoho

Key Fields

FieldValue
SO #Matches deal number (PROJ-####)
Reference #PROJ-### | Customer Name
Order DateCurrent date
Expected Shipment DateConstruction schedule date
When viewing a sales order, you can see how much inventory is currently available. The system pulls directly from warehouse stock.

2. Purchase Order (PO)

Multiple POs per Sales Order

Purpose

  • Listing of all materials needed to order from vendors
  • Emailed to supplier to purchase required inventory

Timing

  • At creation of the Sales Order (convert from SO via three-dot menu)

Creating a PO from a Sales Order

  1. Open saved Sales Order → three-dot menu → "Convert to Purchase Order" → confirm "Yes"
  2. Select the vendor you are ordering from
  3. Set the delivery date
  4. Remove items that are already in stock or will be pulled from the warehouse
  5. Remove items not being ordered from this specific vendor
  6. Save and send

Key Fields

FieldValue
PO #Matches deal number (PROJ-####)
Reference #Sales Order # only (no name or additional information)
Order DateCurrent date
Delivery DateWhen shipment is needed
Delivery AddressWarehouse name and address
Reference # format: Only include the Sales Order number on the PO reference — do not add customer name or other info.
Multiple vendors: If parts are needed from different vendors, create a separate PO for each. Remove the items not from that vendor on each PO.

3. Purchase Receives (PR)

Multiple PRs per Purchase Order

Purpose

  • Records receipt of inventory and moves it into physical stock count

Timing

  • When inventory is received from vendor at the delivery location

How to Receive

  1. Locate the PO number (usually on the shipping slip)
  2. Verify all received line items against the PO
  3. Hit "Receive"
  4. If partial shipment: adjust quantity to what was actually received → system creates another slip for the remaining items
Important rules:
  • Only receive items that are physically in the shipment
  • If a backorder occurs, it must be a separate PR
  • Vendor bills can only match to a full PR — partial receives break the match
  • Always double check quantity against packing slip
  • If an incorrect item was sent, receive it under a separate PR
  • Receive promptly — Accounting often gets the invoice within 1–2 days and will auto-receive if the team hasn't done it

4. Pick / Pack / Ship (PKG/SHP)

Automated on Date Installation Begins

One per Sales Order

Purpose

  • Reserves inventory in physical stock count once "confirmed"
  • Removes inventory from physical stock count once "shipped"

Timing

  • Start of construction
At Photon Brothers, this is all one step. The three sub-stages are:
Sub-stepWhat it meansPB approach
PickPulling items from shelvesPB does not do this step
PackConsolidating items on cartPKG-#### (project number)
ShipLoading items onto truckSHP-#### (auto assigned number)
Pack & Ship dates must be set to when inventory actually leaves the warehouse.

5. Invoice (INV)

Automated at Construction Complete

One per Sales Order

Purpose

  • Removes inventory from accounting stock count
  • Closes the Sales Order

Timing

  • Following Pick/Pack/Ship completion

Key Fields

FieldValue
Invoice DateConstruction complete date

6. Receive Vendor Bill

Accounting

Purpose

  • Records vendor costs into accounting stock
  • Closes the Purchase Order

Process

  • Accounting matches the invoice to the corresponding PO
  • Vendor invoices are often numbered with dashes (e.g., PO-1, PO-2) for partial shipments related to the same order

Quick Reference

RelationshipRatio
Projects → Sales Orders1 : 1
Sales Orders → Purchase Orders1 : Many
Purchase Orders → Purchase Receives1 : Many
Sales Orders → Pack/Ship1 : 1
Sales Orders → Invoice1 : 1

sections.title: Edge Cases & Exceptions
sections.content:
Edge Cases & Exceptions

Common scenarios that fall outside the standard workflow. These apply to all locations.

Pulling from Stock (No PO Needed)

When items are already in warehouse stock and don't need to be ordered:

  1. Confirm the Sales Order (all items appear on SO regardless of source)
  2. Do not create a Purchase Order for stocked items
  3. Create a Package and Shipment directly to account for items leaving the warehouse
The Sales Order is the comprehensive list of everything the job needs. The Purchase Order is only for items that need to be ordered from a vendor.

Ramp Card / Credit Card Purchases

For items purchased with a Ramp card (e.g., picking up pavers/blocks for ballast kits):

Do NOT create a Purchase Order for Ramp card purchases.
  • Use an Inventory Adjustment instead to add items to stock
  • This also applies to any ad-hoc pickups paid by credit card on the way to a job
  • If accounting cannot close an SO because of these items, they will do an inventory adjustment for the line item to allow the SO to close

Multiple Vendors for One Job

When parts for a single Sales Order come from different vendors:

  1. Convert SO to PO for the first vendor
  2. Remove all items not being ordered from that vendor
  3. Save and send the PO
  4. Go back to the SO → create another PO for the next vendor
  5. Repeat for each vendor
The SO stays the same — it's the master list. Each PO is just a slice of the SO for a specific vendor.

Bulk / Stock Purchase Orders

For ordering inventory not tied to a specific project (replenishing stock):

  1. Go to Purchase Orders → create new (top corner button)
  2. Select the vendor
  3. Select the correct warehouse
  4. Search and add items, set quantities
  5. Save and send
Warehouse default: Centennial is the default warehouse on all forms (SOs, POs, everything). Always verify you have the correct warehouse selected before saving.

Every bulk purchase still requires a PO — even if not originating from a sales order — so accounting can match invoices.

Partial Receives

When only part of an order arrives:

  1. Open the Purchase Order
  2. In "Receive Items," adjust the quantity to what was actually received
  3. Mark items not yet received as zero
  4. Save as received
  5. The system automatically creates another receive slip for the remaining items
Accounting expects partial receives to happen. Vendor invoices are often numbered (e.g., -1, -2) to correspond to partial shipments. Receiving promptly prevents accounting from auto-receiving and keeps counts accurate.

Missing BOM Items

The automated BOM extraction currently does not capture all items. Known gaps:

  • Breakers — not typically included in planset BOMs. Check the one-line diagram to identify required breakers.
  • Wire lengths — standard wire (THHN 6, etc.) is ordered as bulk stock, not per-job. Job-specific wire (e.g., 1/2/3 gauge for MPUs) should be ordered per job.
  • Twins / panel accessories — items like twins for main panel space should be added to the SO manually.
If the BOM tool pulls incorrect information, submit feedback via the BOM tool so errors can be corrected.

Damaged / Defective Items

If a part is found defective or damaged:

  • Use the Damaged Items category in Zoho to account for the part
  • Do not leave it as regular stock — it will skew inventory counts
  • Field techs should report defective parts immediately so the adjustment can be made

Inventory Adjustments

Use inventory adjustments when items enter or leave stock outside the normal PO/SO flow:

ScenarioAction
Ramp card / credit card pickupInventory adjustment to add items
Damaged or defective partAdjustment to damaged items category
SO won't close due to missing stock recordAccounting does a one-line-item adjustment
Quarter-end stock countsAdjustments to reconcile physical vs. system counts

sections.title: California Operations
sections.content:
California Operations

California-specific inventory procedures. CA operates a bulk-stock model rather than per-job ordering due to distributor reliability challenges.

Ordering Model

California does not need to follow the Colorado per-job ordering model. CA can order stock at any cadence.

How CA orders:

  • Rail, attachments, breakers: Stocked in bulk at the shop. Reordered when running low.
  • Job-specific items: Ordered per job (e.g., specific three-phase inverters for commercial jobs). Reference the customer's last name on the PO for warehouse identification.
  • Electrical distributor (CES): Visits the shop approximately every 2 weeks, checks inventory, and reorders automatically. QR-based scanning system available.
  • Standard wire (THHN, 6 gauge): Ordered as bulk stock. Job-specific wire (1/2/3 gauge for MPUs) ordered per job.

Why Bulk Ordering

CED (CA distributor) is unreliable — lead times of 1.5 to 2+ weeks for standard materials like rail and attachments are common. Competitors in the area also stock their shops, depleting local supply. Bulk ordering prevents construction delays.

Stock Flow for Bulk-Ordered Items

When items are pulled from existing warehouse stock for a job:

  1. Confirm the Sales Order (items show as available from inventory)
  2. Skip creating a PO for stocked items
  3. Create Package → Shipment to account for items leaving the warehouse

When warehouse stock needs replenishing:

  1. Create a standalone PO (not from a sales order)
  2. Select vendor and CA warehouse (not Centennial)
  3. Add items and quantities needed
  4. When received, create a Purchase Receive to add to stock
Every purchase must have a PO — even bulk stock orders — so accounting can match invoices. The only exception is Ramp card purchases (use inventory adjustments instead).

Getting Started: Quarter-End Stock Counts

For locations onboarding to Zoho inventory, the starting process is a quarter-end stock adjustment:

  1. Accounting issues stock counts broken into small groups (15–20 counts)
  2. Groups are organized by category: modules, batteries, inverters, rail, breakers, etc.
  3. Team physically counts each group and enters the actual quantity
  4. Zoho adjusts stock to match the physical count
Breaking counts into small groups prevents the entire stock count from getting stuck on a single discrepancy. Each group can be completed independently.

sections.title: Service & RMA
sections.content:
Service & RMA Inventory

Inventory procedures for service operations, Return Material Authorizations (RMAs), and field truck stock.

This area is actively evolving. Integration with vendor portals (Tesla Energy Service Portal, SolarEdge, etc.) is planned to automate much of the manual entry described here.

Service Parts from Inventory

When a service job needs parts from warehouse stock:

  1. Check Zoho to see if the part is available in inventory
  2. If available, pull from stock
  3. Account for the part leaving the warehouse via package/shipment or inventory adjustment
Service teams need to track all parts used — even if pulled from general stock for a repair. Untracked usage causes inventory counts to drift.

RMA (Return Material Authorization)

RMA parts are vendor warranty replacements and require special handling:

Key Principle

RMA parts should be tracked by customer, not as general stock. They are received for a specific customer's warranty claim and should be linked to that customer/deal in Zoho.

RMA Flow

  1. Vendor ships replacement part to warehouse
  2. Receive the part into Zoho, associated with the customer
  3. When the service team takes the part for the job, account for it leaving inventory
  4. If the RMA part doesn't match what's needed (common with Tesla), flag it and work with the vendor

Using Stock for an RMA Job

If the correct RMA part hasn't arrived but you have the part in stock:

  1. Pull the part from general stock for the service job
  2. When the RMA replacement eventually arrives, receive it back to general stock (it's replacing what was used)

Service Truck Inventory

Service trucks carry common parts for on-site repairs (breakers, TRMs, backup switches, etc.).

  • Truck inventory should be tracked — consider setting up service trucks as inventory locations in Zoho
  • When a tech uses a part from the truck, it needs to be accounted for
  • If a truck part is found defective, report immediately and move to the damaged items category
  • Regular truck inventory counts (e.g., weekly) help keep numbers accurate

D&R (Detach & Reset) Parts

D&R jobs also consume parts that need tracking. Currently, parts used on D&R jobs are often unallocated — they need to be accounted for just like install jobs.

When using parts for a D&R job, follow the same SO → Package/Ship flow to account for items leaving the warehouse.

Vendor Portal Integration (Planned)

Future integration will connect vendor portals (Tesla Energy Service Portal, SolarEdge, etc.) with PB Operations Suite and Zoho:

  • Automatically pull tracking numbers and case information
  • Reduce manual sales order creation for service tickets
  • Improve RMA part tracking and receipt verification
